quotes.race: FBD Hotels And Resorts Heritage Hotel Phoenix Sprint Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Givemethebeatboys took a little blow inside the last and he'll improve plenty for that, so it's all systems go now for the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock next month - Kate Harrington, assistant trainer.

quotes.race: Space Traveller Irish EBF Committed Stakes (Listed)
quotes.quote: Givemethebeatboys was good with his penalty. He was fresh early on and took a blow a furlong out, but once he got rolling again he was always going to get there. He'll go to Naas and then the Commonwealth Cup - Jessica Harrington, trainer.
The ground was probably a little bit slow for Givemethebeatboys. We might step him up to seven - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

Givemethebeatboys ran a great race and tried his heart out. As far as he was concerned he was second - he didn't see the others. We'll probably go for the Phoenix Stakes - Jessica Harrington, trainer.
Givemethebeatboys was very professional and he outbattled them to the line. He learned a lot at Navan and he will be even more competitive when we go to Ascot - Kate Harrington, assistant trainer

stable_tour.quote: He's in great form and has wintered very well, I'm very happy with him. We're planning to go to Navan with him for the Committed Stakes on Saturday week but I'm not sure the ground will be dry enough for him. Hopefully he'll get back to Royal Ascot this year after finishing fourth in the Coventry last season. He ran very well in the Phoenix Stakes and Middle Park on his final two starts. 24-04-24

quotes.race: Betfair Exchange Old Borough Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: We weren't expecting Post Impressionist to do that as Tom said he didn't want to go early. I was hoping if that was the case they would go fast and they seemed to and he managed to pick them off. He ran a good race in the Ebor and he's in a terrific vein of form. He's probably up for another rise which will knock him out of any serious handicaps - William Haggas, trainer.

runners.owner: Australian Bloodstock, S Tsalikidis Et Al

stable_tour.quote: He goes into quarantine in two weeks. I think the plan will be to run him in either the Cox Plate or the Caulfield Cup. We were very pleased with his run at Goodwood behind Jan Brueghel, as the ground was probably quicker than he'd want. He hadn't done masses of work before that race and how he went, including between the three-furlong marker and the one, was very good. It was a great bit of placing by Andrew Balding to win such a huge prize with Bellum Justum (second in the Gordon Stakes, just in front of our horse) over the weekend at Kentucky, and if you'd stopped the race a furlong out you'd have counted us the winner. He looked like the fastest horse in the race. He's rated 109 and Jan Bruegel could win the St Leger. We've had some good horses like Mugatoo, Rodrigo Dias and Red Cardinal for Australian Bloodstock and he looks to be another. 04-09-24"

quotes.quote: He did it well. Mr Weld rang me this morning and said that he'd sharpened up a lot from his last run. He probably just got outstayed by a classy horse at Fairyhouse and he told me to ride the race like a piece of work. I haven't sat on him since last year, I rode him before he ever ran and thought he was a grand horse. He travelled around with a lot of pace, probably more pace than I gave him credit for because he quickened up well and put the race to bed. I got there too soon and he was just idling a bit in front. The blinkers probably sharpened him because he didn't ride as not genuine, he's a grand, straightforward horse and for the start around here you need that early pace to get up. Options are open for him and I think he could come back another two furlongs again if he wanted to. It's great to ride another winner in these colours again - Chris Hayes, rider of Taramansour

quotes.quote: Things didn't go right for Bur Dubai in the first half of the season and we could have done a few things better, but hopefully we have a promising horse for the second half. We always thought he was a nice horse. I probably didn't campaign him the best but today we let him use his stride and he's a big, strong horse.He picked up nicely and put the race to bed in good fashion. We let him bowl along in front and he stayed very well. I think he's a stayer in the making and he'll be able to stay a mile and six - Kevin Philippart de Foy, trainer.

quotes.quote: Via Sistina is definitely good. It takes a good one to beat the big boy [Romantic Warrior] though, but she's well up there and she turns up each and every time, which I'm so proud of her because if she was going to get beat, it could have been easily a race like this. As we've seen she's getting more versatile. It felt like it was a mile race. She was really tanking up and hence the bead of sweat, so I've earned my money - James McDonald, jockey.

Via Sistina is right up there. She has done amazing things. She won her first start in Australia, she won a Winx Stakes over 1,400 metres, she won a Turnbull and she won a Cox Plate, breaking the track record by a big margin. Now she's backed it up here today. Take out her only defeat, which was on a heavy track, and she has been unbeaten in Australia. That is pretty remarkable - Chris Waller, trainer.

It was a Winx-like performance. I can't believe it. What this mare has been through; Via Sistina is just an absolute star. I'm so lucky to be a part of it - James McDonald, jockey, speaking to Racing.com.

quotes.race: Sumbe Prix Jean Romanet (Group 1) (4yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Round Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Via Sistina has run a blinder again and it's not taken much out of her, she just got a bit lonely in front. I think we'll probably come back for the Opera on Arc day. It looks the right race for her as she's a filly who handles all types of ground. It'll probably be her last race, but she's done amazing and has only just come into herself. She's a very good filly - George Boughey, trainer.

quotes.race: Tattersalls Falmouth Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: It was the step back to a mile and Via Sistina just got tapped for toe. Jamie only gave her one flick and I was pleased to see her stay on well as she looked like she was booked for fifth or sixth at one point, but up the hill she stayed on well all the way. She'll have a bit of a break now and then we'll prep her for the autumn back to ten furlongs. You won't see her over a mile again - George Boughey, trainer.

It's unlike her as she's never hung before. Once I saw the head-on I wasn't too concerned but you'd rather win more smoothly. I had a plan going out to jump smartly and sit third but Via Sistina bobbed the gate and it was straight to plan B. I wasn't very comfortable for the first six furlongs of the race but, once I got her around the turn into the straight and went past one on the outside, she came alive. I was in business then - Jamie Spencer, jockey.

The ground is key to Via Sistina, she's got a hell of a turn of foot in soft ground. Her work is good on decent ground but it's a lot better on softer ground. It didn't come as a huge surprise when the rain started to fall, we were pretty happy. I think even the back end of last year we were talking about the Prix de l'Opera on Arc day for her. That's a realistic possibility as it's been soft however many times on that day and we'll work back from that. The Pretty Polly Stakes on Irish Derby Day is quite possibly the next step - George Boughey, trainer.

Via Sistina is a very good filly. It just didn't quite happen on her French trip . She came back a little bit low and we just sat and bided our time and give her a break. When the ground comes right she is a very good filly and I'm sure she is a black type filly. She handles that ground very well. Hopefully she doesn't get sold like a lot of my good ones do. She is as big as house and she will only improve with time. She was too keen in France and the plan was to miss the kick and drop her in and there is no better man in the country than Jamie Spencer if you are going to do that - Joseph Tuite, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She had been on my radar long before she joined us in the autumn. She was in training with Joe Tuite and landed a nice touch for them at Newmarket in a big handicap off 89 and when I heard Joe was retiring, I hoped we might acquire her and when I got the phone call saying she was coming to us I was absolutely delighted. I have spoken to Joe about her, and he has been a big help. She was a very big raw filly when she got here but is maturing all the time. She ran a lovely race first time for me when a fast-finishing second in the Group 3 Pride Stakes behind Creative Flair before opening her Group-race account at Toulouse in the Prix Fille de l'Air in November over 1m2½f. She enjoyed a good winter, and I had the Group 2 Dahlia Stakes as her intended target, but I needed soft ground. I remember looking up to the sky on 2,000 Guineas day and being delighted at the amount of rain that fell as every drop aided her cause the following day, and she romped in, coming home six lengths clear of Al Husn, who subsequently impressed with a Listed success. She won't get her ground next week at Royal Ascot and I'm not going to risk her on anything other than ground she relishes. She is a Group 1 filly in the making and my main objective this season for her is the Prix de l'Opera on Arc weekend. She has an entry in next month's Pretty Polly Stakes at the Curragh, but the ground there is riding very quick at the moment. I'm not sure what her best trip is yet and we could even consider the Arc if everything went right. 14-06-23

quotes.quote: Thesecretadversary ran a great race. It's annoying we didn't get there, but he ran a great race. He's been very straightforward, he's always looked a bit above average from the minute we started doing a bit with him. I know he's quite colourful, but he's actually a very good model. Seamie was delighted with him, he said he has the pace of a good horse, he feels he will mature in time - Fozzy Stack, trainer.

runners.comment: Needs to be taken seriously despite having more on his plate.
runners.spotlight: Gained a 1 1/2 l success last month overcoming four of these rivals in an allowance race; seeking a hat-trick today and has a nice pedigree (by War Front); could have less on his plate in this; more on his plate this time but strong claims.

runners.spotlight: Was well on top at the finish here on his debut at evens last month; this son of Hard Spun looks the part on breeding; tackling an extra 2f now; up in class to allowance company.

runners.comment: On target once in three runs in Britain and third in a Group 2; rates a likely contender.
runners.spotlight: Won a Haydock maiden (7f) before just getting pipped in a Newmarket novice event (7f); improved slightly when third in Group 2 Superalative Stakes (again 7f at Newmarket); rates a likely contender given trainer's record Stateside

quotes.race: Betfred Supports Jack Berry House Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Wild Desert did everything professionally and obviously he's got a super page. I managed to get a lead into the straight and although it took a while for the penny to drop, when it did in the last three-parts of a furlong I really liked his attitude. He was quite green and will come on a lot for the experience - Billy Loughnane, jockey.

runners.comment: Might find this company a little easier but one to tread carefully with.
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth, beaten 6l by Two Out Hero last month in an allowance race; hit the bar in a stakes in July and has a nice pedigree (by Upstart); down in class this time but easy enough to take on.

runners.comment: Jockey's C&D record from low draws is useful and looks a big player.
runners.spotlight: Last year's 2,000 Guineas winner who bagged the Sussex less than three months later; hasn't added to tally since then, though; proved a shade disappointing back in trip in the July Cup but returned to close to his best when head second in the Prix Jaques le Marois at Deauville; clealry the one to beat if near the peak of his form.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1)
quotes.quote: William [Buick] was really disappointed, Notable Speech didn't hit the line like we thought he would -  Charlie Appleby, trainer.

It's disappointing when you have a race like that and they've gone the pace they have. From our point of view, it was a bit hard to watch. Notable Speech was sat there, travelling like he was, and you knew it was going to turn into a sprint. We will put it behind us and he has run a solid race. I take on board what William said afterwards and, if you wanted to pigeon-hole him into a trip now that he is older and stronger, then seven furlongs is his gig. I still wouldn't walk away without taking a good strong view of the Sussex again, but we all know the City Of York Stakes is going to be one hell of a race this year - Charlie Appleby, trainer.
I'm delighted. Will said Notable Speech showed all his usual enthusiasm. He travelled and was one of the last to come off the bridle but then got tired close home - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

quotes.race: Prix du Moulin de Longchamp (Group 1) (3yo+) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Charlie [Appleby] is very philosophical and it's all now geared towards the Breeders' Cup, where Notable Speech will get the ground he needs. We all saw at Goodwood and Newmarket that he has fantastic acceleration on the right surface - Hugh Anderson, Godolphin chief executive.

Notable Speech was firing on all cylinders coming in here today. He's a brilliant Guineas winner, with a very rare turn of foot. He showed that again today and I'm delighted. Full credit to Charlie and the whole team, they've done such a good job with him to get him back after a disappointing run in the St James's Palace where he was obviously too bad to be true. He's such a brilliant horse. He's a horse that really excites you and it's a real privilege to be riding him - William Buick, jockey, speaking to ITV Racing.

quotes.race: St James's Palace Stakes (Group 1) (Colts) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: It's disappointing. That's obviously not the horse we know from Newmarket. Notable Speech relaxed nicely and I thought everything was going how I expected. Take nothing away from the winner, who has danced every dance, but we've not run anywhere near what we can do - William Buick, jockey.

quotes.race: Qipco 2000 Guineas Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: Notable Speech really was impressive. He's the kind of horse who I've not known for that long. I came back from Dubai and rode him in February and here we are. I've not very often believed in a horse as much as this guy. I've had that much belief in him and sometimes you go home at night and think 'have I lost the plot all together?' because that's how he does things, he does it so easily and his change of gear is incredible. The race couldn't have worked out any better, fair play to the team he was in great form today. He's done nothing but improve. He's an out-and-out miler, he's all about speed. He's got a strong mind, I really think he's a horse who is going to keep improving - William Buick, jockey, speaking to ITV.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Best Odds Daily British EBF Conditions Stakes (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: I was very pleased with how well Notable Speech has done physically since the last time I rode him. He's really filled out and I think he's grown a little as well, which is always nice to see. I couldn't be more happy with him. I tested him a little bit today and he had it covered in a stride. I had to try and pick a path with him but he's genuine and easy to deal with and he's got a bright future ahead of him. Obviously at the moment it's all wet [on the grass] so this was a nice opportunity for him to have another run on this surface. He'd handle a little bit of cut in the ground but he's a very fast horse with a low action, so he would like decent ground. He's a speedy miler. It was a really good performance and he deserves to go up in grade now. I thought this was a warm race and he gave away weight to all of them, so I'm delighted - William Buick, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: R M Sewgoolam & Betting Entertainment Technologies Intern

runners.comment: His jockey has an impressive record from low numbers over this C&D so should not be ruled out.
runners.spotlight: Has picked up some decent prize money in nine starts in South Africa, winning six times; likely to be thereabouts as jockey does well from inner gates here.

runners.breeder: Lanny McDonald, Linda Barron & Susan Rasmussen
runners.comment: Step up to allowance company will make life tougher so not an obvious one.
runners.spotlight: Resume shows that he is a proven winner (seeking a eighth career win); unbeaten at this C&D after two runs; went close when second here over 1m 1/2 f (allowance optional claimer) last month; up against it.

runners.comment: Others more appealing despite this looking a little easier than last time.
runners.spotlight: Running consistently; beaten three-quarters of a length in an allowance Grade 2 behind Gas Me Up 28 days ago; down in class this time but others more appealing.

runners.comment: He's an attractive contender; not beaten too far in Saratoga Grade 1 last month.
runners.spotlight: String of consistent efforts of late and course specialist who has won six times here; finished just behind Win For The Money when fourth (Saratoga) upped to this trip six weeks ago; an easier task than his previous race; has to be taken seriously.

runners.owner: Pepe Stable & J L M Jacona Stable

prev_owners.owner: Robert V LaPenta & Madaket Stables LLC

runners.comment: Not hard to turn elsewhere despite the last time out success.
runners.spotlight: Proven that he knows how to get his head in front (seeking a sixth win); gained a 2l success two weeks ago at Colonial Downs in an allowance race; Victor Carrasco having only ride on the card; steps up in grade so unlikely to trouble the principals.

runners.breeder: Kenneth L Ramsey & Sarah K Ramsey
runners.comment: Could have a say; placed in the Fourstardave Handicap 42 days ago.
runners.spotlight: Turned in a solid effort in defeat 42 days ago, when third in the Fourstardave Handicap at Saratoga; has managed five wins from 21 career runs and running consistently; might find this easier; is not ruled out.

runners.owner: Exline-Border Racing LLC, Michael Jawl Et Al

prev_owners.owner: Exline-Border Racing LLC, Michael Jawl & Aaron Kennedy

runners.comment: Should not be far away despite having more on his plate.
runners.spotlight: Narrow scorer in an allowance Grade 2 upped to this trip last month; specialist here with three wins from four visits and not had much racing for his age; this represents an easier task than his previous race; this looks tougher but rates a likely contender.

racecards.verdict: NOTABLE SPEECH is hard to oppose and can confirm form over Win For The Money if the pace is strong.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Notable Speech, 4/1 My Boy Prince, 6/1 Gas Me Up, 15/2 Win For The Money, 12/1 One Stripe, 13/1 Stanley House, 14/1 Wyoming Bill, 16/1 Naptown, 18/1 Hunt Master

racecards.race_name: Abbie Moon Is 18 Today Handicap

runners.comment: Badly out of form last year; returns from 446-day absence with a lot to prove
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles for a different stable in summer 2023 but was badly out of form on the Flat for this yard last year and returns from 446-day absence with a lot to prove.

runners.comment: Recently placed over 11.6f here on heavy ground; big player if this new trip suits
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who was placed on the last two of his five Flat starts, more recently when third in handicap here (11.6f, heavy) ten days ago; could easily have more to offer over today's longer trip; big player.

runners.comment: 0-13 under all codes but was denied clear run when third at Redcar last time; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: 0-13 under all codes but was denied clear run before keeping on well for third on Flat return at Redcar (2m, good to soft) in July and has travelled from North Yorkshire for this race (trainer's first runner here).

runners.comment: Latest run was slightly more encouraging be she has basically been out of form this summer
runners.spotlight: Took small step back in right direction when fourth of seven here (1m5f, firm) last month but has basically been out of form this summer; sports refitted cheekpieces today; seems versatile ground-wise; others preferred.

runners.comment: Four good runs in today's headgear combination since upped to 2m; in the mix again
runners.spotlight: Four good runs in today's headgear combination since upped to 2m in July, most recently when first past the post (demoted to second) at Ffos Las (2m, good to soft) last month; untested on ground slower than good to soft but can make his presence felt if he handles today's conditions.

prev_owners.owner: Catch Us If You Can Racing & Dylan Cunha

runners.comment: 0-21 now but posted a sound effort when second to Rupert The Prince here recently
runners.spotlight: No threat to Rupert The Prince when second here (1m6f, soft) ten days ago and now 0-21 but that still goes down as a good effort and he'll be fine over today's longer trip; each-way claims at least.

runners.comment: Improving 3yo who made all for a clearcut course win on soft ground ten days ago
runners.spotlight: Improving 3yo who made all for a clearcut course win (1m6f, soft) ten days ago and is now 2-3 since fitted with a visor; 7lb higher today but could easily have more to offer over this longer trip.

runners.comment: Lightly raced and disappointing for current stable since last spring; looks too risky
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on consecutive 1m3f AW starts in February 2024 but lightly raced and disappointing for current stable since last spring; looks too risky on this first venture beyond 1m6f.

runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.comment: Veteran; runs this track well but below form this year; this looks too competitive
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is suited by this track and seems very versatile regarding trip/ground; soundly beaten over 2m1f here yesterday, though, and will probably find this race too competitive.

stable_tour.quote: He was unlucky at Bath last time when he got turned sideways. At least with Fact Or Fable you can semi-predict whenever he's going to win but old Uther is more unpredictable. He won three times in a week just before he turned eight. He's versatile at any trip from a mile and a quarter to two and a quarter miles. He enjoys it and pays his way every year, which is nice. I wouldn't mind a few more horses like him. I raced him in France a fair bit as a two- and three-year-old and he had won €98,000 in prize-money and was still a maiden. 04-07-24

runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who needs to find some improvement from somewhere
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was about 10l behind Rupert The Prince when fifth of eight here (1m6f, soft) ten days ago and needs to find some improvement.

runners.owner: R M Levitt And A Bromley
runners.comment: Improved form when fifth on handicap debut (AW); contender if switch to slow turf suits
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her three qualifying races (1m-1m3f) but fared considerably better when upped to 2m for last month's handicap debut at Lingfield (AW; first-time visor), shaping well before fading into fifth of 13; could plausibly improve again if this switch to slow turf suits.

runners.comment: No win since 2023, and has had several chances off lowly marks this year
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his 1m4f AW win in March 2023 and has had several chances off lowly marks this year; difficult to enthuse over.

runners.comment: Unexposed after only five starts but both handicap runs were very disappointing
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only five starts but both handicap runs this season (1m4f/1m6f) have been very disappointing; swaps visor for first-time blinkers here; leap of faith required to support him.

runners.comment: Unplaced all seven starts and needs this new trip to enable improvement
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield in AW classified race (1m4f; first-time visor) last month and has now been unplaced all seven starts; needs this new trip to show him in a much better light.

racecards.verdict: Placed over 11.6f here on his recent Flat handicap debut, GREGORIANS STAR could have untapped potential over today's much longer trip.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Rupert The Prince, 4/1 Star Of Jupiter, 9/2 Gregorians Star, 6/1 Somebodycomegether, 7/1 Wannabeawallaby, 10/1 Girl Of Gold, 20/1 Endofastorm,  Ridgemaster, 25/1 Abstract, 33/1 Sobegrand, 40/1 Sky Dancer,  Uther Pendragon, 50/1 Izakaya,  Godstone

racecards.race_name: Andersons Drainage Solutions Supporting Bath Racecourse Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Soft ground will be a first but she was well on top in a 6f maiden at Chepstow a month ago
runners.spotlight: Soft ground will be a first but she was well on top in a 6f maiden at Chepstow a month ago (good) and that was her best form yet; doesn't look handicapped out of contention off this mark, especially with Elizabeth Gale taking off 5lb.

runners.comment: First-time cheekpieces did the trick when a clearcut winner at Brighton (maiden)
runners.spotlight: Had been expensive to follow but first-time cheekpieces did the trick when a clearcut winner at Brighton (maiden over an extended 5f; good); now 5lb higher than when narrowly held in his two previous handicaps but that rise is erased by Jack Callan's claim; soft ground a first.

runners.owner: Dave Evans, Mrs C Derham & Partner

prev_owners.owner: Dave Evans, Mrs C Derham & Partner
Dave Evans, Mrs C Derham & Partner

runners.comment: 0-5; now tongue tied and on soft ground for the first time for nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Went close on her previous visit here (second start); things haven't got any better since then but she was outclassed in a sales race at York last time; now tongue tied and on soft ground for the first time for nursery debut.

runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing
runners.comment: Only fourth of six on nursery debut but could be interesting back over 5f
runners.spotlight: Close fourth at Windsor but still went off 11-1 for nursery debut at Ffos Las (good to soft) and was only fourth of six; has had no trouble holding her position at 6f and drops to 5f for the first time here.

runners.comment: Not progressed; bit risky on nursery debut, especially as soft ground will be a first
runners.spotlight: Newmarket debut in April was encouraging but hasn't progressed at all and was only seventh of ten in a C&D novice when last sighted in June; bit risky on nursery debut, especially as soft ground will be a first.

runners.comment: 0-7 but closely matched with Calafiori on their nursery clash on the AW in July
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but closely matched with Calafiori on their nursery clash on the AW in July and got back on track with a second at Lingfield (5f novice); genuine soft ground would be a first but hard to dismiss.

runners.comment: Chepstow second shows that he can win races off this mark; some ground concerns
runners.spotlight: Flattered to push the winner quite so close on his nursery debut at Chepstow (5f, good) but that shows he can win races off this mark; unraced on soft and his useful dam appreciated a quick surface.

racecards.verdict: A trappy nursery with no soft-ground form on show. Brighton winner CALAFIORI earns marginal preference.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Cloudbuster, 5/2 Calafiori, 6/1 Truly Glamorous, 13/2 Carefree Dream, 7/1 Storm Flag, 10/1 Diamond Alexander, 12/1 Woolisle

racecards.race_name: Andersons Drainage Serving The South West Handicap

runners.comment: In good form on AW over the winter but 0-10 on turf and soft ground an unknown
runners.spotlight: All wins at Wolverhampton, including three last winter (6f and 7f twice); still on a feasible mark but unraced on slower than good to soft and the wait for his first turf success looks set to continue.

runners.comment: Ended 2024 with two good course runs on soft ground; can go well fresh; yard run two
runners.spotlight: Ended 2024 in good form, winning over 5f here in October before finishing second to an improver over C&D ten days later (both soft ground); has gone well after a break in the past and she's one to consider; connections also run Bungle Bay.

quotes.race: Dragonbet - Born From The Betting Ring Handicap
quotes.quote: Mintana has had a great year. She's only lowly rated but she's got a heart of gold and loves her racing. It's great to continue a good season - James Evans, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs A M Sturges , Ilka Gansera-Leveque

runners.comment: All wins on AW and best turf form on good ground; dangerous mark but enough to prove
runners.spotlight: All wins on AW and his best turf form has come on good ground; lowly mark but others have more compelling claims.

runners.comment: Three-time course winner who lurks on a good mark; probably better on faster ground
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, including three at this track, and he's not run badly at Brighton on his last two starts; has form on soft but is probably better on a sounder surface.

quotes.race: Best Free Tips At valuerater.co.uk Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Under Curfew travelled beautifully. I was told to follow our other horse [Bezzas Lad] but he went off too quick. We slotted in and he couldn't try any more for me ? I couldn't stop screaming from two out - Mollie Phillips, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Bath in May and then again at Salisbury last month.  He was second at Bath last Friday night - it looked an easy opportunity for him with just three runners, but they're never easy and 5f on very fast ground probably just caught him out - he's more a 6f horse really. The winner of the race is possibly all right. He'll run in the Bullet at Brighton on Friday. All ground comes alike to him and he's in good form. 07-08-24

runners.comment: Late headway into 2nd at Ffos Las on Tues (well backed; 6f, heavy); drop in trip a query
runners.spotlight: On a losing run stretching back two years; heavily backed at Ffos Las on Tuesday and made some late headway into second, albeit never the slightest threat to the winner; ground no problem but dropping back in trip could be (all wins from 7f to 1m).

stable_tour.quote: He won seven races for us last year, so it's hard to think he's well handicapped. He'll improve for his comeback and will run himself back into form. He likes to lead. 04-05-23

runners.comment: Struggled since last June; return to sprinting can help but far from solid
runners.spotlight: Two good runs last spring, winning at Wolverhampton (5f) before a close fifth at Ascot on handicap debut (6f, good); nothing to celebrate since and although the return to sprinting should help, he's not a solid option.

prev_owners.owner: P Simpson, D Tierney, J Portman

runners.comment: First turf win when scoring at Brighton (5.3f) in June; mixed since and ground concerns
runners.spotlight: Gained his first turf success when coming out on top in a 5.2f classified event at Brighton in June (good to firm); mixed bag since and although he handles good to soft, he was well beaten in one run on soft.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs A M O'Shea & Sean Delaney

runners.comment: Two 6f wins at Ffos Las this summer; not at best on Tuesday but capable of bouncing back
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins at Ffos Las this summer (good and good to soft); not at his best back at Ffos Las on Tuesday where the heavy ground shouldn't have been an issue; not at his best in two visits to Bath either but he's still a good candidate at this level.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Earl Cadogan

runners.comment: 4lb lower than for his Brighton win in May; something to prove on slow ground
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 5.2f handicap at Brighton in May and runs here off a 4lb lower mark; that is a result of some modest recent efforts though and ground slower than good is a concern.

runners.comment: Only win from 23 starts came over C&D; not at best this year but e-w claims nonetheless
runners.spotlight: His sole win from 23 starts came over C&D last May; not at his very best so far in 2025 but handles slow ground and is more likely to give his running than several of these.

runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts this season; down in class but others are still more appealing
runners.spotlight: Conditions won't faze her but she's been well beaten at big prices in two runs this year; down in class and better could be forthcoming today.

runners.comment: C&D win last May completed a good-ground hat-trick; nothing comparable in last 12 months
runners.spotlight: Last win came over C&D in May 2024, when completing a good-ground hat-trick; not come close to that level in the last 12 months though and too much to prove for comfort.

racecards.verdict: Question marks over most and perhaps MINTANA, who will relish conditions and ended 2024 in fine form, can make a winning return.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Mintana, 5/1 Under Curfew,  Autumn Festival, 11/2 Diamondsinthesand, 7/1 Malham Tarn Cove, 8/1 Lynwood Lad, 12/1 Voodoo Ray, 14/1 Bungle Bay, 16/1 Port Hedland, 25/1 Once Adaay,  Firenze Rosa, 40/1 Angel's Call

racecards.race_name: Andersons Commercial And Domestic Drainage Specialists Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Modest bumper form and finished a remote sixth on his recent Flat/stable debut; no appeal
runners.spotlight: Modest bumper form and finished a remote sixth on his recent Flat/stable debut; no appeal.

runners.owner: Ursa Major Ltd & Hicky Parmar Racing
runners.comment: Four-time hurdle winner; neither Flat run suggests he is the answer today
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner; neither Flat run suggests he is the answer today.

quotes.quote: I wasn't sure after the last. I could hear something behind me and then I saw the shadow of the loose horse and that I wasn't really worried. Then when we crossed the line I saw the runner-up and I hoped I hadn't eased up too much going to the line but thankfully I got away with it - Power Roche, jockey of Prince Quattro.

runners.comment: Hurdle winner in July; just modest form on recent Flat debut; needs a good deal more
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner at Uttoxeter in July (2m, good); modest form on last month's Flat debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good to soft); big step forward needed.

runners.comment: Sold for 1,000gns last October; fair form on debut but likely best watched this time
runners.spotlight: 310,000euros yearling who left the Gosdens for 1,000gns last October; while his sixth of nine at 14-1 in a Windsor maiden (good to firm) last July was fair form, he carried his head rather awkwardly and is best watched for new yard after the absence.

runners.comment: 0-5 in bumpers in France; not easily recommended switched to the Flat
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in bumpers in France; not easily recommended switched to the Flat.

runners.comment: Tailed off in one run for David Evans (1m, AW); no appeal back from 619 days off
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off 100-1 shot at Kempton (1m, AW) in one run for David Evans; no appeal back from 619 days off.

runners.comment: RPR in the 80s on last two starts; this looks a golden opportunity to open his account
runners.spotlight: Improving with experience and his Pontefract second (1m, good) in July is comfortably the best piece of form on offer; not quite so good upped to 1m2f (heavy) at Goodwood 11 days ago but even that form reads well in the context of this race; the one to beat.

runners.comment: Beaten over 9l but still shaped with promise on recent Salisbury debut; each-way shout
runners.spotlight: Third of six, beaten 9.5l, on his Salisbury debut two weeks ago (1m2f, good to soft); open to improvement and he holds each-way claims.

runners.comment: Well-beaten 40-1 shot on his Southwell debut six weeks ago (1m3f); no hood today
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for his Southwell debut (1m3f) six weeks ago and finished a remote ninth; no hood today; not easily recommended.

runners.comment: Finished last in two maidens this summer; can't be recommended
runners.spotlight: She has finished last in two maidens this summer and the switch to slower ground needs to have a dramatic effect.

runners.comment: 300-1 and offered nothing on her Kempton debut (1m4f) last month
runners.spotlight: 300-1 and offered nothing on her Kempton debut (1m4f) last month.

racecards.verdict: This looks a golden opportunity for GOODWOOD MOGUL to open his account. Hellion is best of the rest.
racecards.betting_forecast: 1/3 Goodwood Mogul, 11/2 Hellion, 7/1 Prince Quattro, 8/1 Soul Limbo, 16/1 Tajer, 50/1 Joyful Voice,  Persian Warrior, 100/1 Hopjes,  Moon's Reflection, 150/1 Lynda's Dream,  Miss Raffles

racecards.race_name: Rupert Hunt-Grubbe Cup EBF 'Confined' Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C & D) (Qual') (GBB/GBBPlus)

runners.comment: 35,000gns yearling; with a top stable; wouldn't need to be anything special to feature
runners.spotlight: 35,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 1m4f/1m6f AW winner (RPR 78), half-sister to useful winners Galapiat (1m4f) and Madame Ambassador (8.5f-1m4f including 2yo); represents a top stable and wouldn't need to be anything special to pose a threat on debut.

runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on first two starts (6f/7f)
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on first two starts (6f/7f).

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Non-runner.

runners.comment: Came last in three novice races this summer; not yet given a handicap mark
runners.spotlight: Came last in three novice races this summer (7f-1m) and will be of greater interest once the handicapper has seen fit to give him a mark.

runners.comment: Showed only minor promise on his first two starts (7f/1m) and low-grade handicaps beckon
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise on his first two starts (7f/1m) and probably has low-grade handicaps on his agenda after this run.

runners.comment: Unlucky not to finish closer than fourth at Chepstow (6f); a possible if new trip suits
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on her debut in July and looked unlucky not to finish closer than fourth at Chepstow (6f, good) a fortnight later, having been denied a clear run; can feature if suited by the new trip here.

runners.comment: Stoutly bred filly who struggled to get competitive on her first two appearances
runners.spotlight: Stoutly bred filly who struggled to get competitive on her first two appearances (7.4f/1m); sports first-time cheekpieces here.

runners.owner: R J Manning & Mrs Fiona Williams
runners.comment: Beaten about 35l when 33-1 for debut at Lingfield (7.6f) last month
runners.spotlight: Out of a fair 7f winner; beaten about 35l when 33-1 for debut at Lingfield (7.6f, good) last month.

runners.comment: Kept on for fourth in Class 2 Salisbury maiden last month; leading contender
runners.spotlight: Bred for middle distances; kept on from the rear for very respectable fourth in Class 2 Salisbury maiden (7f, good to firm) on last month's debut; leading contender if she copes with the slower ground here.

runners.comment: Second run was a shade disappointing but she can still build upon her encouraging debut
runners.spotlight: First foal from a useful 1m2f winner; weakened out of contention at Chelmsford last month but her debut fourth at Wolverhampton (also 7f, AW) was quite promising and she retains scope to build on that.

runners.comment: Made reasonably encouraging debut but second run was a backward step
runners.spotlight: Made reasonably encouraging debut when eighth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good) in July but took backward step at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month and has a point to prove over today's even longer trip.

racecards.verdict: Her ability to handle slow ground must be taken on trust but TAMZAN made a pleasing debut in a better race than this.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Tamzan, 5/2 Magic Trip, 5/1 Beryl's Girl,  Lapidarist, 8/1 Paroled, 10/1 Therefore, 50/1 Sueshine, 66/1 Fuzeyya, 100/1 Addmann,  Favourite Friend

racecards.race_name: Euram Air Leases Ireland Handicap (Div I)

runners.comment: Generally consistent this season but dropped out rather tamely at Epsom on Thursday
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mainly over 6f/7f but stays 1m well enough; generally consistent this season and has slipped to a workable mark but he dropped out rather tamely at Epsom on Thursday and needs to rebound quickly.

runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who was soundly beaten on all three starts this season; now with new yard
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on all three starts for Denis Coakley this season and now 0-10 overall; makes stable debut from tempting mark but needs to have been revitalised by her new regime, and is untested on turf slower than good; headgear removed.

runners.comment: Won 7f nursery last autumn but well below that form this season (five runs)
runners.spotlight: Improved to win 7f nursery on heavy ground last autumn but well below that form this season (five runs; 7f-1m2f), including when back on slow turf a fortnight ago.

runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner
runners.comment: Gave good account when fourth last month but the slower ground is a worry here
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; gave good account before fading into fourth at Salisbury (1m2f, good to firm) last month and may benefit from today's drop back to 1m but her only soft-ground run resulted in a heavy defeat.

runners.breeder: Moyns Park Estate And Stud Ltd
runners.comment: 0-25 now but looked unlucky not to finish closer than fourth over C&D last week
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 25 starts but earns good marks for his consistency this summer and looked unlucky not to finish closer than fourth over C&D (heavy) ten days ago; cheekpieces refitted; likely contender.

stable_tour.quote: He was second on his debut at Leicester in a three-horse race, but that doesn't tell the story. He was very slowly away and lost several lengths yet still managed to run on and get second once the penny dropped. I am not saying he would have beaten the winner but he would have been much closer. 10-05-23

runners.comment: In the frame on three of first four stable starts; handles soft ground; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on three of his four starts for Tony Carroll since June, most recently when third of seven at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) last month; continues to edge down the weights and should also be suited by today's trip-and-ground combination; one to consider.

runners.comment: No win since summer 2023 but posted sound effort when placed over C&D last week
runners.spotlight: Without a win since summer 2023 and patently not the force of old but posted a sound effort when third over C&D (heavy) ten days ago and has obvious each-way claims off the same mark.

quotes.quote: Everything went wrong for Eye Of The Water at Wolverhampton last time but, although he's won at that track, he's a much better horse on soft ground. That's the key to him - Ron Harris, trainer.

Last time Eye Of The Water ran on soft ground at Ffos Las he won. Conditions were slightly heavier today and he relished them. He was able to get a smart break and he never saw another horse after that - David Probert, jockey.

runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Partner
runners.comment: Ran as though something may have been amiss on seasonal/handicap debut; still unexposed
runners.spotlight: Ran as though something may have been amiss on last month's seasonal/handicap debut but, after only four starts, remains very unexposed.

runners.comment: Nursery winner last September but absent since lesser effort a few days later
runners.spotlight: Came good in Chepstow nursery (7f, good to firm) just over a year ago, his fourth start, but absent since a lesser effort a few days later and his tendency to race far too freely remains a worry; recently gelded.

quotes.race: AK Bets Home Of Free Bets Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Liricist was given a ridiculous handicap mark by the handicapper. I thought he was a 55 [rated] horse and they gave him 32, which was a bit of an embarrassment. I was half expecting him to run well, but at the same time, the word from the George Boughey yard was that the favourite is a 75 [rated] horse. We've had very few runners this year and we've got quite a few two-year-olds who aren't quite ready to run. He's a homebred by the owners, Joli Racing, who I've trained for over many years. They sell them at the sales, but if they don't get what they want, they send them to me. It's a welcome winner and a winner with a two-year-old for a long-term owner is brilliant - Tony Newcombe, trainer.

runners.comment: Didn't fire on latest AW start but is largely consistent; looks worth another crack at 1m
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month but has largely been consistent since shock win over that C&D on stable debut last November, and is probably worth another crack at 1m; not ruled out.

runners.comment: Safely held in three 7f nurseries last autumn; returns for new trainer here
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who was safely held in three 7f nurseries for Amy Murphy last autumn and now makes debut for Hayley Burton after 11-month absence; has been given a wind op; considered only if lively in betting.

runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive when 80-1 for recent handicap debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW)
runners.spotlight: Struggled to get competitive when 80-1 for recent handicap debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW); needs to take a huge step forward here.

racecards.verdict: The suggestion is UNKNOWN JOURNEY, who has posted some fairly encouraging efforts for his new stable since the spring.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Unknown Journey, 4/1 Beau Jardine,  Eye Of The Water, 9/2 Too Much Trevor, 7/1 Baynoona, 11/1 Ashford Hill, 12/1 Liricist, 16/1 Unforgetable Belle, 25/1 Sweet Sister,  Champagnebreakfast, 50/1 Dinah Myte

racecards.race_name: Euram Air Leases Ireland Handicap (Div II)

runners.comment: Soundly beaten on both runs in the spring and absent for another three months since
runners.spotlight: Best known as a sprint/7f performer but was runner-up on only previous 1m attempt (June 2024); versatile ground-wise but returned from break with two below-par runs in spring and absent for another three months since.

runners.comment: Second in C&D maiden in April but four poor runs have followed; ground a worry too
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in C&D (good to firm) maiden on seasonal debut in April but four poor runs have followed; needs new tongue-tie to make a big difference; recently withdrawn from C&D race with heavy ground deemed unsuitable.

runners.comment: Won tactical 1m race in June but might be stretched by a proper test at this trip
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Jack Jones with 1m win in a tactical race at Leicester (good to firm) in June and was not beaten far over 6f for new stable the following month (good to soft); seemed to find a truly run 1m too taxing next time, though, and was pulled up in early stages of latest race (lost action).

runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but will be very well suited by the slow ground here; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Has modest 2-46 strike-rate but comes here after two fairly good efforts (both 1m2f, good) and will be very well suited by the slower ground today; features prominently in calculations.

runners.comment: Needs to better recent efforts but won off this mark on soft ground in June
runners.spotlight: Opened account off basement mark at Chepstow (1m, soft) in June, her 13th start; below that form lately but now back down to her winning mark, and will be suited by today's conditions.

runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
runners.comment: Good servant over the years and this C&D suits; best on fast ground, though
runners.spotlight: Gained three of his 11 wins over this C&D, the latest in fast-ground classified race in June; has some form on slow ground too but would appeal more on a quicker surface.

stable_tour.quote: I've got two lovely old campaigners who are half-brothers - Fact Or Fable and Uther Pendragon. Between the two of them, they've won just short of £200,000 in prize-money. They always turn up and probably on the days you'd don't expect them to win, they do. They're characters in the yard and keep everybody on their toes who rides them. They're owned by the same people - Wendy and Stuart Jarrett - who get so much fun out of it. It's what the game's about. Wendy and Stuart have point-to-pointers as well and they absolutely love their racing. They come from the Bath area and that's why, when you see them, they'll always turn up at Bath and Chepstow. They're the sort of owners racing needs, who are in it just for the love of the sport. 04-07-24

runners.comment: Opened account on soft ground at Chepstow (7f) recently; big player if stamina holds out
runners.spotlight: Seemingly boosted by first-time cheekpieces when keeping on well to score at Chepstow (7f, soft) 11 days ago, opening her account at the ninth attempt; not yet proven over 1m but recent 3lb rise ought to be manageable; high on the list.

runners.comment: Fast-ground sprint winner last summer but out of sorts this season; had wind op last month
runners.spotlight: Won at Brighton (5.2f, good to firm) last summer but has looked out of sorts this year and it's hard to predict whether today's trip-and-ground combination will suit; had wind op last month and now sports a tongue-tie.

runners.comment: Suited by soft ground and ran quite well last month but very rarely wins
runners.spotlight: Suited by return to soft ground when keeping on for respectable third in recent Ffos Las classified (1m) but has very modest 2-53 strike-rate and younger rivals appeal more for win purposes.

runners.comment: Has no form claims but is worth a glance in the betting before this handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise in his three qualifying races (1m-1m2f) but makes his handicap debut from a lowly mark and is worth a glance in the betting.

runners.comment: Eight-race maiden; unproven over 1m and needs to up her game on this stable debut
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was safely held in 7f Lingfield classified in July, her only run for Barry Brennan; not yet proven over 1m and needs to up her game on debut for latest new stable.

racecards.verdict: This return to forecast heavy ground is a big plus for MARCHETTI (nap), who has her favoured conditions for the first time this year.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Blue Point Express, 4/1 Marchetti, 7/1 Anticipating, 8/1 Up The Anti,  Fact Or Fable,  James Park Woods, 14/1 American Rose,  Pave The Way, 16/1 All About George,  Lady Diesel, 25/1 Broadhaven Bay

racecards.race_name: Signs Express 'Confined' Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Which Have Not Won A Flat Race In 2025)

runners.comment: Multiple winner, including C&D, but he never found his form in 2024; off for a year
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner at 1m2f-1m4f, including over C&D, but he never found his best form in 2024 and it's 12 months since he was last seen.

quotes.quote: Bug Boy deserved that. He has been running well of late and he was travelling super all the way round. I'm very happy with that. They have gone a nice gallop and I was able to get him to settle and switch off out the back. Turning in, I wanted to stay covered up for a little bit longer, but it all opened up a little quicker than I wanted. He is genuine and game, so he ran on to the line - Rhiain Ingram, jockey

runners.comment: Won at Yarmouth (1m3f, soft) last October but not in the same form this year
runners.spotlight: Has won over 2m but most recent wins at about today's trip, the latest at Yarmouth (soft) last October; not in anything like the same form for either yard this year, though, and now has it to prove.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Bloodstock Pro Colwick Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: If we had an easy lead we would have taken it, but the pace was perfect. Alpine Stroll hung right around the left-hand bends, but that was quite good as I was able to get a breather into him. We kicked in the straight up the inside and he's picked up lovely, so it's worked well - Rhys Clutterbuck, rider.

runners.comment: Maiden; had few suitable chances on Flat and running well of late; obvious claims
runners.spotlight: A maiden under both codes but he's had few suitable opportunities on the Flat and, after getting hampered at Newbury two starts ago when making a forward move, he was a solid third of five at Ffos Las (1m4f, good; acts on good to soft) recently; close up.

runners.comment: Well handicapped on best 2024 form for A Tregoning; handles most surfaces; can have a say
runners.spotlight: Her wins come round infrequently now but she's well handicapped on her best form at about this trip for Alice Tregoning in 2024; versatile ground-wise and it's likely that she'll have a say.

runners.breeder: Skymarc Farm & Haras De La Fontaine
runners.comment: Maiden in France and Britain; needs to improve for a hood being added and the new trip
runners.spotlight: Placed four times at up to 1m3f in France; missed all of 2024 and ran as well as could be expected when fifth at Chepstow (1m2f, good) in June for new yard; no improvement on that when a one-paced fourth on Chelmsford AW since and now has a hood added; needs to improve for it and the new trip.

prev_owners.owner: Blind Squirrels Syndicate, M T Pockets Syndicate Et Al
Mrs J Murphy & Blind Squirrels Synd' & M T Pockets Synd'
Blind Squirrels Syndicate, M T Pockets Syndicate Et Al

runners.comment: Minor hurdle form off modest marks but might have more going for her back on the Flat
runners.spotlight: Hasn't gone on off modest marks over hurdles but perhaps the Flat is more her calling, having had limited opportunities but running well when second of 11 at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in May 2024; hard to be sure she's in much form, though.

runners.owner: R Greenwood & John And Peter Seabrook
runners.comment: Won on soft last autumn but a slog this year; not ruled out after the recent rain though
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 2024, over 1m4f (AW) and 1m2f (soft), but it's been a slog this year and he's gone into reverse since a better effort in third of 11 at Windsor (1m2f, soft) in July; the recent rain is in his favour now back on turf, though, and he's not ruled out.

runners.comment: Some AW promise in Ireland; placed hurdle form of late; career-low mark back on Flat
runners.spotlight: Some AW promise in Ireland early in 2024 but she didn't make the grade in handicaps and her placed form over hurdles has settled into an ordinary groove; career-low mark back on the Flat but it may have further to fall.

runners.comment: Maiden; up and down but capable at 1m4f on his day and handles soft ground
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has his good and moderate days but he's capable off his current mark and testing ground hasn't been a problem in the past; probably better for the run when returning from a break last week and comes into it on a second of nine at Haydock (1m4f, good) under Megan Jordan in May.

runners.comment: Signs of revival latest and 2lb lower than when winning this last year; has won on heavy
runners.spotlight: Handful of starts this year, not showing much until Chelmsford recently, when beaten only by one who'd dropped a long way in the weights; comes here in better form than when winning this race last year and good chance of another win now 2lb lower; has won on heavy.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Supports Safer Gambling Week Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
quotes.quote: Even when you've got a soft-ground horse you never know if they'll go on heavy and it's like a ploughed field, but Mrs Meader handled it beautifully. We've found the key to her, which is softer ground, and she's flourished. I think the owner wants to give her another year racing and then breed from here - Ross Birkett, jockey.

quotes.race: OCS Goodliffe Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: Mrs Meader has always been a late maturer. She's been unlucky not to have won, we got beat a nose at Windsor last season but a mile and two (furlongs) here on that ground is ideal. At home she felt so much better this year and I'm glad she's finally got her head in front - Ross Birkett, rider.

runners.comment: Five turf wins, the latest over 1m6f on good to soft, but two heavy defeats this year
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins, most recently at Ffos Las (1m6f, good to soft; other wins on good or quicker) in July 2024; suffered two heavy defeats this year but refitting regular cheekpieces could make a difference.

quotes.quote: Mujid did that nice considering he didn't really handle the track. He was still a bit green as it was only his second start but I thought it was a nice performance. He had a lovely run at Gowran and he stepped forward from it. I don't know how strong the race was but he did it well - Conor Hoban, rider

runners.comment: Consistent at Ffos Las since returned to turf this summer; engaged 7.15 here last night
runners.spotlight: Returned to turf with four pretty good efforts at Ffos Las (1m-1m2f) this summer, the last three for current stable, and seems quite versatile regarding ground; due to run in 7.15 here last night; will warrant consideration if he contests this race.

runners.comment: Promising 3rd on handicap debut (1m1f, AW) but poor results since and risky again
runners.spotlight: Improved on earlier form when third of ten on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (about 1m1f, AW) in January but has finished remote since, in a hood then cheekpieces (retained) on last two starts; risky.

racecards.verdict: Most have a query but MRS MEADER showed signs of life last time and is taken to repeat her 2024 win, which came off a 2lb higher mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Moab,  Mrs Meader, 6/1 Apodictique, 13/2 Jlow, 7/1 Bobby Dassler, 9/1 Bloodhound, 10/1 Atalanta Breeze,  Dubawi Time, 12/1 Masonbrook Meadow, 14/1 Bug Boy,  Alpine Stroll, 25/1 Mujid, 50/1 Mostly Mozart

racecards.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Fourth of 18 in a valuable 7f nursery at York last time; probably on the premises
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of ability, as shown particularly on second and latest starts; in the latter he was fourth of 18 in a valuable 7f nursery at York; probably on the premises but may well be vulnerable again.

runners.comment: Races freely and front-running lately; turned over at 1-3 latest but leading form claims
runners.spotlight: Hooded all three runs; third at Kempton (6f, AW) and Glorious Goodwood (7f, good) on first two starts, front-running in the latter and his best form; raced too freely in front at Epsom (7f, good) on latest outing, hanging left and turned over at 1-3, but even that gives him leading form claims.

runners.comment: 33-1, soon behind when tailed off in a novice at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) four weeks ago
runners.spotlight: 33-1, soon behind when tailed off in a novice at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) four weeks ago.

runners.comment: Similar 6f placed form when favourite at Newmarket and Ascot; stamina on the dam's side
runners.spotlight: Similar placed form when favourite at Newmarket (6f, good to firm; slowly away) and Ascot (6f, good; always prominent); there's stamina on the dam's side and she has strong each-way claims again.

runners.comment: 18-1 at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) but always in touch and kept on stoutly for 3rd of 10
runners.spotlight: £65,000 breeze-up 2yo by Ardad; 18-1 in novice at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago but always in touch and kept on stoutly for third of ten; should improve and have a major say.

runners.comment: 25-1, unable to get competitive when 7th of nine in Lingfield maiden (5f, good) in June
runners.spotlight: 25-1, unable to get competitive when seventh of nine in Lingfield maiden (5f, good) in June, showing low-level form; late foal and upped 2f.

runners.comment: Close third of five here (7.6f) but beaten nearly 10l at Newmarket (7f) 12 days later
runners.spotlight: Close third of five here (7.6f, good to firm) early last month was a bright start but going down by nearly 10l at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) 12 days later seemingly puts a dampener on her prospects.

runners.comment: 10,000gns yearling by Territories; trainer has had two 2yo debut winners this season
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Territories half-sister to 5f/6f winner Spanish Angel (including 2yo; RPR 85); dam placed at 7.6f/1m as 2yo (64), out of maiden half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Wake Up Maggie; trainer has had two 2yo winners this season, both first time out (28-1 and 9-1).

runners.comment: Tasleet filly; yard has few 2yo runners
runners.spotlight: Tasleet filly; fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to 5.4f 2yo winner English Hero, out of maiden half-sister to useful 5f/6f winner Englishman; yard has few 2yo runners.

racecards.verdict: Ghost Mode has a the best form but tends to race freely. WILD DAHLIA, Enamorus and Advance Twentyfive look serious rivals.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Ghost Mode, 3/1 Advance Twentyfive, 7/2 Wild Dahlia, 4/1 Enamorus, 12/1 Emerald Coast, 28/1 Bright City Lights, 40/1 Far Too Fizzy, 50/1 Rock Of Destiny, 66/1 Steel Fixer

racecards.race_name: Virgin Bet Stand Cup Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.comment: Distinguished career includes two wins here; turf runs last six years on softer than good
runners.spotlight: 9yo who's won a hatful of Group 3s over 1m4f-1m6f in his long and distinguished career, including two Ormondes over the extended 1m5f here; also won 1m4f Listed races at the Curragh (yielding) last November and Goodwood (soft; by a neck from Military Academy at level weights) this June, which were his last two outings; all of his turf starts in the last six years have been on softer than good, so rain may be needed.

quotes.quote: Hamish was pretty naughty at the start but he's been crying out for a race for a long time. He needs soft ground and we haven't had any. He's been ready to go since the John Porter, which was the middle of April, so he was just pretty bored now and he needed a day out. I ride him every day and he doesn't feel like a nine-year-old, he feels great -  Maureen Haggas, trainer's assistant, speaking on Racing TV.

quotes.race: Curragh On Course Bookmakers Finale Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Hamish did what you wanted to see. We got a nice run around from a good draw and everything went well - Richard Kingscote, rider.

quotes.race: Prix du Conseil de Paris (Group 2) (3yo+) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: I thought Hamish ran really well. The winner is a very good horse and I never expected to beat him. To run as close to him as he did is great. We're really pleased. There's only one more race for him in England [the St Simon Stakes] but that's next Saturday, so it will come too quick. I don't know if there's anything left for him in France, but he loves soft ground and is in great form - Maureen Haggas, trainer's assistant.

quotes.race: Holland Cooper Coronation Cup (Group 1) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: I'm thrilled. Hamish could have done with more rain. It wasn't wet enough and I asked William this morning if we were doing the right thing but it might not rain for three months so you've got to run. He's like my pet and is a bloody star - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Finest Surprise Stakes (Registered As The John Porter Stakes) (Group 3)
quotes.quote: I was delighted. Hamish doesn't really do a lot in front. The ground wasn't really soft enough, he had a 3lb penalty but he keeps winning. It'll be the Ormonde or the Yorkshire Cup - it always rains at Chester, so I suppose it'll be that next - William Haggas, trainer.

Hamish has been great. He needs that slow ground so it's never easy, but he's had his problems and we've needed to look after him. It's because he's been looked after that he's still able to do this. He's not easy. He was very naughty at Goodwood, he doesn't like it there, and he can be difficult. I was a bit worried about today but we saddled him in the stables and it's gone like clockwork, so I'm relieved really. He doesn't like Newbury either, so I was quite pleased the race was called off and he didn't go. I hope he'll be back next year, as long as he's in one piece. He has nothing to do other than be a racehorse, or my hack, and I think he'd rather be a racehorse - Maureen Haggas, trainer's assistant and wife.
Hamish won't run in the Ebor. He's hard to place. The Irish St Leger is a possibility, but he wants soft ground - William Haggas, trainer.

Hamish is a great horse and he got Tom out of a bit of trouble today. He's in the Irish St Leger, he's in everything in case it rains. He handles really horrible conditions whereas a lot of the classier horses don't. We'll see what the weather does and go where the ground suits him - Maureen Haggas, assistant trainer.

quotes.race: Peroni Nastro Azzurro Cumberland Lodge Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: There's the St Simon Stakes for Hamish, but I don't know about the penalties for that. We'll see, but he loves this soft ground - Maureen Haggas, wife of William Haggas, trainer.

quotes.race: tote.co.uk Proud To Support Chester Racecourse Ormonde Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Hamish has done really well because he was a little bit fresh and would have needed it. We haven't been on the grass at all because the ground has been so firm. He had the penalty as well. I walked the course and thought it couldn't be that bad with the rain yesterday and it's going to be better than York next week. I'd imagine he'd go for the Hardwicke but we'll see because it all depends on the ground and if it's firm he won't be going anywhere - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

Hamish was fit and well but has obviously been off the track for a long time and was a bit fresh and gassy today. He's a talented horse. He had great form as a 3yo and ran a really good race in the Hardwicke last year, where unfortunately he picked up his tendon injury. He needs soft ground and we ran him today as we needed to get out and give him a race as he was ready to go. We aim at the stayers' race on Champions Day at Ascot and work back from that -Maureen Haggas, assistant trainer.

Hamish was giving away lumps of weight and he's done it well really. I thought the ground might be a bit quick for him in the Melrose but he won regardless. I think he's at his best on slower ground, like a lot of the family. I wouldn't have thought he'll run again this year, he's won two nice races now - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

runners.comment: Best form on AW, on which he's 5-7 (including a 1m4f Listed win) whereas 0-8 on turf
runners.spotlight: Best form on AW, on which he's 5-7 (including a 1m4f Listed win from the front last December) whereas 0-8 on turf; has shown easily his best turf form in two of his last three starts (gelded before latest) but much better is seemingly required today.

runners.comment: Tailed off last two runs; better seems likely on return to Chester but stamina is in doubt
runners.spotlight: This course rather dominates his turf record, most notably with a big career best to win 1m2f handicap in July in good style from the front; the scale of his defeats at other tracks on his last two outings is a concern and although better seems likely on this return to Chester, stamina is in doubt.

quotes.quote: Grey Cuban is an honest, straightforward horse. He needs to be ridden up with the pace. He's an amazing horse for the syndicate. He continues to improve and this ground suited him - Mike Edwards, head of Roudee Racing.

stable_tour.quote: I was hopeful he'd stay a mile and three furlongs in the Rosebery but he just didn't appear to. He loomed large in the straight looking like he was going to do something but his run flattened out. We'll be dropping back to a mile and two. He'll go for a 0-105 handicap at Chester, where he's won twice, and he should be a fun handicapper at that sort of level this summer. He likes a bit of cut in the ground. He's almost rated 100 now and the air gets a bit thinner up there, so he'll have to improve again. 09-04-25

By the sire of the moment Havana Grey, this colt made a very satisfactory debut when second to Les Bleus at Newmarket last month and then was runner-up again at Nottingham last week. It's not often I'm happy finishing second but I had to be pleased with the way he ran at Nottingham. He finished like the proverbial train and in another dozen strides would have won. That was over 6f again but having witnessed that effort you'd be stunned if he didn't get further as he looked to be crying out for a longer trip. It was an eyecatching run and hopefully his turn is very close. He's a smashing colt who was a Goffs UK purchase and we've been kind of targeting him at the sales race at York next week. He's also in the 6½f sales race at Doncaster's St Leger meeting next month. In time I see him doing well over a mile. He was bought very cheaply as he was a very big horse. Basically, he looked like a giant but he isn't at all and I think his owners have bought very well. I liked him from the word go and would be very disappointed if he didn't win this year. 17-08-23

runners.comment: Neck 2nd to Hamish when level weights at Goodwood (1m4f, soft) is easily best turf form
runners.spotlight: 3-4 last term, notably with a 1m4f Listed success on AW; cheekpieces have gone on this season, with two solid seconds and the modest shows on his other two starts (Bahrain and the Goodwood Cup) can probably be forgiven; going down by a neck to Hamish when level weights in Listed race at Goodwood (1m4f, soft) was easily his best turf form and gives him a big shout.

quotes.quote: Military Academy started his career a little later than usual but has progressed with every start and is still learning. He's taken a step forward since Newmarket. He's got plenty of size and scope and hopefully he will mature over the winter for next year. He's run a few races in quick succession, so we'll leave him alone and bring him back next year - Thady Gosden, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Needs better and also to bounce back from a shocker (front-running was blamed) at York
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2024 and better still this term, winning tidily in a six-runner handicap at Ascot (1m4f, good to firm; acts on good to soft) in July; appeared to run well when fifth of six in Group 3 here in May but better is needed to win this and he needs to bounce back from a shocker at York (front-running was blamed) three weeks ago.

quotes.quote: Mount Atlas stays well, has a nice attitude and is learning all the time - he's going the right way. He's quite immature at home so it was difficult to know what to expect. He can be a little bit lazy in his work but he does it on the racecourse, which is the most important thing - Hayley Turner, jockey.

runners.comment: Irish Derby 5th is well ahead of his other ratings; needs a second look back down in class
runners.spotlight: 40-1 fifth in the Irish Derby at the Curragh (1m4f, good) in June, front rank long way; it's a concern that his rating that day is well in advance of his others, and also that he was beaten so far in the storm at Glorious Goodwood on latest outing, but he needs a second look back down in class.

racecards.verdict: There's a question over Hamish on ground not softer than good. Similar could apply to MILITARY ACADEMY but he's the big form threat.
racecards.betting_forecast: 10/11 Hamish, 7/2 Military Academy, 11/2 Sir Dinadan, 7/1 Mount Atlas, 14/1 Grey Cuban, 33/1 Champagne Prince

racecards.race_name: Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Watergate Cup (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Won a slowly run, 2m Group 3 at Ascot in April; behind in tough assignments since
runners.spotlight: Given wind surgery and tongue tied for new connections this season, which he started with a bang when winning a slowly run, four-runner Group 3 at Ascot (2m, good to firm) in April; remote since, however, in the Gold Cup there, a French Group 2 and the Ebor at York; this is a lesser handicap but he does not inspire great confidence even so.

quotes.race: Longines Sagaro Stakes (Group 3) (A Gold Cup Trial)
quotes.quote: Yashin is imposing and should be getting better with age, but Kate Harrington did say he doesn't jump!  He wasn't 100 per cent ready today and the Gold Cup needs to be considered. He's going to have to take on Kyprios, who has had the beating of him before, but after beating Coltrane today it's got to be an option. He'd have to go up another half mile but I don't see why he wouldn't get it. The Melbourne Cup could be on the agenda as well - Nick Bell, son and assistant to Michael.

Yashin will have to go up a bit to get to the Melbourne Cup, but he stays well and he has to have good ground. He probably likes going left-handed too. He was hung wide and keen all the way there. Shane said he really had no right to pick up and win like he did. I know it was only a short head but he was always getting there - Jessica Harrington, trainer

quotes.race: Fund A New Home For Festina Lente Handicap
quotes.quote: Yashin was good. We went from 1m to 1m4f and it probably looked a bit stupid but we knew after the last day that he really needed to go a distance There was no maiden coming up for him so when this three-year-old only handicap came up we thought this was where he should be, because we thought he was probably quite nicely handicapped - Jessica Harrington, trainer

stable_tour.quote: He won the Saval Beg at Leopardstown last season and ran very well when seventh in the Ebor. The Ebor will probably be his main aim again but we won't run him unless the ground is quick. 24-04-24

He'll be going for the Ascot Gold Cup. He might not appear in the Racing Post entries yet as it's a bit of an anomaly - he was entered by Ascot racecourse as a free entry after he won the Sagaro Stakes and for some reason there was a bit of a glitch being transferred over. It's a lovely race to be involved in. We gave him a minor wind op and, while we haven't done anything different with him, I just think he's matured and grown. He had a small back issue last year but that hasn't bothered him and he seems to be moving really well. He fully deserves to line up in the Gold Cup. Obviously a few like Kyprios have fallen by the wayside but I think Aidan O'Brien still holds the aces with Illinois, who'll be a very tough nut to crack. But if ours is there turning for home we'll give him a good shout. 09-06-25

runners.comment: While peak 2024 efforts give him a fine chance, some caution is currently advised
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in Saudi Arabia in February (heart issue) and well held in two British handicaps since; closer in Listed race here (first visit) on latest start but he was only fifth of six in that slowly run race; while peak 2024 efforts give him a fine chance, some caution is currently advised.

quotes.race: Liz And Izzy's Hoap Podcast Handicap
quotes.quote: That was great, I was delighted with Prydwen. Callum gave him a lovely, confident ride. It was an unbelievable effort carrying that weight. He obviously loves the AW so the Northumberland Plate looks a legitimate next step. He'll be weighted out of something like the Copper Horse at Ascot, but we might try the Goodwood Cup, he's moving into that sort of realm now - George Scott, trainer.

It was a really good performance. Prydwen has been a light-framed horse so we've given him a bit of time and he's filled into his frame. He's a bit older and wiser and that performance opens a few doors, I think he deserves a go at Royal Ascot in something like the Copper Horse Handicap - George Scott, trainer.
Prydwen has been a nervous horse in the past and has been slow to come to hand. They didn't go too hard up front which enabled Marco to get a smooth run into it - George Scott, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a career best on his last start when narrowly second, which is quite a good effort considering he's a six-year-old. He's been pretty consistent considering how much he's run and he might go for a break now, have one run before Royal Ascot, and he'll go for the Copper Horse Handicap. He deserves a chance to run at a meeting like that and going back in trip there would suit him. 29-03-24

runners.comment: Second, first and third in the last three Chester Cups; trouble sustaining his form though
runners.spotlight: Second, first and third in the last three Chester Cups; he didn't quite reproduce that form in the 2023 or 2024 campaigns, however, and tailed off at Royal Ascot on first attempt this season; comfortably held in fourth in this race last year; this does not look the time to catch him.

quotes.quote: It was a huge run. I thought the horse who'd win finished second, but we've been beaten by two better handicapped horses on the day. Zoffee has run a tremendous race and obviously goes well fresh. He's getting older and we don't want to overrace him this year, and I expect he'll be back here next year - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

quotes.race: Duke Of Westminster Supporting The Chester Cup (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: We were a good second last year, and had the third with Rajinsky the year before, so it's great to have the winner. Harry gave Zoffee a great ride and I'm delighted to win this big race with him - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

The question mark was the ground, Zoffee has now proved he's totally versatile. He's still not there in his coat, we've been waiting for him to come and maybe if we'd got a run into him three weeks ago - Ben said he just took a slight blow with 100 yards to go. He'll come forwards and the Ascot Stakes or the Northumberland Plate would be possible races for him - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

quotes.race: Jenningsbet Northumberland Vase Handicap (Consolation Race For The Northumberland Plate)
quotes.quote: Zoffee did everything right today. He has plenty of ability and he was turned out in great shape. Hugo said he had flourished since Carlisle, he couldn't be happier with him - Ben Curtis, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He's a year older but looks as good as ever. He's where he should be at the moment and I'm just as happy with him as I was last year. His first runs of the year have definitely been his best in the last two seasons, so we'll go to the Chester Cup without a prep run. He's rated 1lb higher than when he won it last season and 2lb lower than when he was a close second in 2023. 09-04-25

He's had a great year with Hugo Palmer on the Flat and hopefully he's got a workable mark back over hurdles. The Peterson family are keen to have a go jumping this season, so he'll have a little break and maybe run around Christmas. If he can show his Flat ability over hurdles then he could have a nice mark. 26-10-23

He has had a great year with Hugo Palmer on the Flat and hopefully he has got a workable mark back over hurdles. The Peterson family are keen to have a go jumping this season, so he'll have a little break and maybe run around Christmas. If he can show his Flat ability over hurdles, then he could have a nice mark. 01-11-23
