runners.comment: Two C&D wins this stage last year, including this race; won Northumberland Plate this June
runners.spotlight: While his peak form was in 2022, he won twice over C&D at this stage last year (including this race by a nose) and was 25-1 winner of the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, AW) this June; fair efforts on last two starts; needs respect as each-way candidate.

quotes.race: JenningsBet Northumberland Plate Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Spirit Mixer is a star. Andrew [Balding, trainer] offered me the ride early in the week and I couldn't turn him down because Spirit Mixer is so honest. He's such a cool horse. He's seven years old now and a beautifully bred homebred of Mr [Jeff] Smith's. He's just thriving and really enjoying his racing at his tender age. The race went pretty smooth. I thought I was jostling for the lead early enough but he got into a battle and outstayed them inside the last furlong - Rob Hornby, jockey.

quotes.race: Newmarket Academy Godolphin Beacon Project Handicap
quotes.quote: Spirit Mixer is very well bred and did that very nicely. I waited for the gap to appear up the rail but I reckon she would have won anywhere across the track - Hayley Turner, jockey.

runners.comment: Finally broke his British duck at Southwell (2m, AW; from Artisan Dancer) 16 days ago
runners.spotlight: Three wins in France (2023); tailed off on sole start here in this race last year; finally broke his British duck at Southwell (2m, AW; from Artisan Dancer) 16 days ago and some 2025 turf form wasn't far behind it, but he's back up 3lb and seemingly needs to find a bit extra.

runners.comment: Never better as hurdler; 2m4f might have stretched him at Royal Ascot 2024; respected
runners.spotlight: Second past post in the very valuable Galway Hurdle (2m, good) six weeks ago but awarded it due to interference; that followed his second in the County Hurdle at Cheltenham (2m1f, good to soft) in March; he's never been better in that sphere and shaped a bit better than seventh might suggest at Royal Ascot 2024 when 2m4f might have stretched him; back to Flat but respected.

quotes.quote: Jack [Kennedy, winning rider] said as soon as he came in and before they called the stewards' inquiry that he thought we'd get it. He said the other leant on top of him for the last 50yds and intimidated Ndaawy all the way to the line. I'm delighted I won the race, but Noel [Meade, trainer of Helvic Dream] and I are very good friends. It's just not the same when you win it in a stewards' inquiry. Rules are rules though and if they're broken, the result deserves to be changed. This is unbelievable. We said we'd wait for today [after Cheltenham] and there's a race for him in October in America now. Hopefully the expenses have been paid for now - Gordon Elliott, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Download The BetVictor App Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Ndaawi improved from the last day, we knew he had and he was entitled to do that. The Boodles is the plan - Gordon Elliott, trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual) (GBB)
quotes.quote: Ndaawi is still a big baby who is learning - William Buick, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: We fancied him to run a big race in the Cesarewitch, but he didn't get home in it. He's got some solid form over hurdles, finishing third in the Fred Winter and then second in the Galway Hurdle. Hopefully, there might be a nice handicap hurdle in him this season. 28-10-24

runners.comment: Chance judged on his peak efforts on AW or good to firm, the latest a 1m6f win in June
runners.spotlight: Hooded last four starts; chance judged on his peak efforts on AW or good to firm, the latest being his 1m6f win at Sandown in June; there's a bit more to prove away from that ground and after his three attempts at 2m+.

runners.owner: Dooley T'breds, J Fairhurst, Kabin Racing Synd, N O'Hare

prev_owners.owner: Dooley T'breds, J Fairhurst, Kabin Racing Synd, N O'Hare

runners.comment: Running well at 1m2f-2m on AW but his only two appearances on turf this season were poor
runners.spotlight: Twice second in the spring, plus fourth in the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, AW) and a 1m2f AW win at Dundalk on latest outing; however, his only two appearances on turf this season were poor.

runners.breeder: S C E A Ecurie Haras Du Cadran Et Al
runners.comment: Second to Sheradann at Southwell 16 days ago and beaten a nose at York last Sunday
runners.spotlight: Usually held up; below form on worse than good to soft; third to Gibside in the Chester Plate in May on his only race at this track, having won at Ripon the time before; that's his latest win but he's been bang there in a string of races, including when second to Sheradann at Southwell 16 days ago and beaten a nose off this mark at York last Sunday; leading contender.

runners.comment: 11yo; softer than good (preferably soft or heavy) has suited him best for a long time now
runners.spotlight: Sixth in 2022 Chester Cup (sole visit); well handicapped on best 2024 form; now an 11yo, well beaten on final outing last season and his only start this term (June); the latter was on good ground, whereas it's softer (preferably soft or heavy) which has suited him best for a long time now.

quotes.quote: If we had that shower we were forecast then they would have struggled to catch Torcello but it has just dried back a bit and it is a bit tacky. I thought he was going to hang on but I'm happy with the run. He didn't run well at Doncaster as it was horrible, tacky ground that day and he didn't like it at all. He always runs well here as he likes these straight tracks. Sharp tracks like Goodwood he can't get into a rhythm but around here he can - Shaun Lycett, trainer.
We've been all over the country trying to get this ground for Torcello and he's a good horse when he gets it - Shaun Lycett, trainer.

quotes.race: Weatherbys General Stud Book Online Handicap
quotes.quote: Torcello is a progressive horse and hopefully he will now get a penalty for the Old Rowley Cup - Andrew Balding, trainer.

quotes.race: Equine Supplements You Can Trust Handicap
quotes.quote: Torcello is by an Irish Derby winner and is related to some two-milers so it was no surprise he needed every yard - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

runners.comment: Won 2m2f Chester Plate (2lb higher today) in May; could be a lot more interesting again
runners.spotlight: 2m2f Chester Plate (2lb higher today) from off the pace in May was the last of his seven wins and he was second last July on his only other visit; well held last three outings, absent eight weeks before York last Sunday; 25-1 that day and he could be a lot more interesting with that under his belt.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Get Rewarded With Ladbucks Chester Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: I was supposed to be a bit handier than that but Gibside didn't come away from the stalls very well so I thought to try and be brave and to see what happens. I was stuck where I was for a long time but he crept a few places forward and turning in he came really good. He's a super horse who is really versatile - ground and trip - and a bit of a yard favourite - Duran Fentiman, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Any Excuse For A Holiday Syndicate

runners.comment: Tailed off 11 days ago on first go at 2m but that was on heavy going after four months off
runners.spotlight: 2-14 in Ireland and so nearly added a third career win when nosed out at Goodwood (1m6f, good) in May on second run for this yard; tailed off back at Goodwood (first go at 2m; 14-1) 11 days ago but that was on heavy going after four months off; could bounce back from that and is not dismissed even though he needs a career best.

quotes.race: Racing Again Here On June 10th Handicap
quotes.quote: I thought the step up in trip would be a big help to Sixpack. It was a bit of a messy race here last time, they didn't go quick and didn't the gaps when he needed. Everything went to plan today, we went a nice even gallop and I was able to get the gap turning in. I thought he stayed on well to the line and the more racing the better he'll get - Ben Coen, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

stable_tour.quote: I bought him from Johnny Murtagh. He's having a break but I really like him and I think he can compete in the nice Saturday staying races for new owners in the yard. I was keen to go jumping with him but we'll probably do that next year. 01-01-25

racecards.verdict: Ndaawi is a deservedly high-profile candidate but GIBSIDE could flower again, returning to the scene of his win in May.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Ndaawi, 5/1 Spirit Mixer, 11/2 Artisan Dancer, 7/1 Zoffee, 8/1 Sheradann, 9/1 Tribal Star, 10/1 Prydwen, 14/1 Youthful King, 20/1 Yashin,  Gibside,  Sixpack, 50/1 Torcello

racecards.race_name: Lotus Manchester At H.R. Owen Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Ran okay in sales races the last twice; proven in nurseries but drawn wide here
runners.spotlight: Odds-on when easily winning a Haydock nursery (6f, good) and so nearly won another when again short odds on fast ground at Ascot; not disgraced in two sales races since then but has a wide draw to contend with here on his first run at 5f; tries cheekpieces.

runners.comment: Not progressed as expected from debut win but he has been gelded
runners.spotlight: All runs on good to firm; highest RPR was his debut win at Thirsk (5f); down the field in the Windsor Castle before finishing only fifth of seven on nursery debut at Haydock (5f); now on a recovery mission but has been gelded.

stable_tour.quote: We've got lots of options for the Windsor Castle but at this point he'd be a definite after winning on his debut at Thirsk and finishing third at York. 12-06-25

runners.comment: Could have been given every chance but has to deal with the widest stall
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent gelding who has raced ten times and the last twice he split two of these in a Windsor nursery (5f) before finishing third of 20 in a 6f sales race at Newmarket; could have been given every chance but has to deal with the widest stall.

runners.comment: 2-2 in a tongue-tie; raised 9lb for the Ripon win and in stronger company this time
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since the tongue-tie went on, winning a Beverley maiden before a 2l success on nursery debut at Ripon (again over 5f on good to firm); may have to deal with slower conditions here and a 9lb rise in stronger company.

runners.comment: Second after a troubled run on nursery debut; competitive mark and well drawn
runners.spotlight: Debut winner; ran well behind smart fillies in a conditions race at Goodwood (good) and didn't get the clearest of runs on nursery debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm), doing well in the circumstances; on a competitive mark and well drawn.

runners.comment: Maiden winner; close up on nursery debut but looks held by Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Maintained his progress to win a maiden at Leicester (5f, good to firm; led over 2f out) before a fourth on nursery debut at Windsor; finished behind Angel Numbers and Kesta that day, albeit hot on their heels; might be vulnerable from stall 7.

runners.comment: Goodwood third reads well; below par since but could bounce back from a handy draw
runners.spotlight: Debut winner and had Angel Numbers behind when third to quality fillies at Glorious Goodwood; given the promise of that run she's been disappointing the last twice, but could bounce back from a handy draw.

runners.comment: In the van throughout for her two wins, the latest on nursery debut at Chelmsford; up 5lb
runners.spotlight: Was up in the van throughout for her two wins, the latest on nursery debut at Chelmsford (5f AW); that was her most realistic opportunity for a while and has been raised 5lb; likely to go well.

runners.comment: Brings momentum into her nursery debut and might not be on too bad a mark
runners.spotlight: Has advanced her RPRs with each run, winning comfortably on the AW before prevailing narrowly on fast ground at Salisbury, again over 6f; she's quick enough for 5f and might not be on too bad a mark.

runners.comment: Impressed at Brighton but this will be tougher, in a better race from a bad draw
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 211 in nurseries and did it very easily in the latest at Brighton (extended 5f, good to firm); the handicapper took note with a 9lb hike and this will be tougher, in a better race from a bad draw.

racecards.verdict: A competitive nursery with all ten runners previous winners. ANGEL NUMBERS did well to finish runner-up at Windsor and is well drawn.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Nifty, 9/2 Angel Numbers, 5/1 London Is Blue, 11/2 Hanney Girl, 7/1 Front Line Fury,  Artista, 10/1 Kesta, 11/1 Army Bugler, 12/1 Jan Steen, 16/1 Temple Of Athena

racecards.race_name: LiveScore Bet Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.owner: On Top Racing Ltd & Two Brothers
runners.comment: Won at York last October but he returns after 308 days off and this is highly competitive
runners.spotlight: 7yo who was as good as ever with his two wins last season and latest was off 3lb lower at York (1m2f, soft) in October; lost a shoe when in mid division in the November Handicap in final run last term but he's been absent since then and this looks tough after 308 days off.

quotes.quote: Two Brothers doesn't like making the running but Serena (Brotherton) had little choice at Doncaster but that blew the cobwebs off him and he came here a fresh horse. A mile and a half on this type of ground is perfect for him - Billy Garritty, winning jockey.

runners.comment: Record of 4-10 at Chester and he was only just caught at Chelmsford latest; respected
runners.spotlight: Has record of 4-10 at Chester and he won this race in 2023; no joy in Bahrain last winter and has mixed record since returned to Britain but he won at Haydock (1m2f, good) in June and was only just caught at Chelmsford last time; remains feasibly treated on his best form and he's respected back at this track.

quotes.quote: It just wasn't his day at York but this time he was super. He loves it here with the crowd and the atmosphere really improves his gate speed. It's the highest mark he's run off, so I just hope the handicapper is kind - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a grand old servant who had a slightly underwhelming winter in Bahrain, having gone well there last year, but he didn't run badly. The handicapper has given him a bit of a chance, so having struggled off a slightly higher rating last year I'd be hopeful he can find life a bit easier. He'll start his season in a 1m2f handicap at Chester in May. 09-04-25

He won three times last year and on the back of that we took him out to Bahrain for the winter, but he disappointed in his three runs there. He has bounced back to form now he's home, as having won at Chester in June he has finished runner-up back on the Roodee and most recently at York. He's holding his form very well and if he can get some good ground for the first time this season he can win again. There's a 0-105 handicap next week at York over 1m2f but he'd want drying ground and I'm not sure he'll get in off 88. Failing that, there are possible races at Newmarket or Chester coming up. 17-08-23

runners.comment: Reliable type who has form figures of 32313 in last five starts; in the mix up in trip
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate of 3-37 but his last win was at Thirsk (7f) last month and he ran another good race when a close third at Newmarket (1m) last time; now goes into unknown territory at this trip but he ran well over 1m1f in July and he's not ruled out; acts on good to firm and good to soft.

quotes.quote: Rhythm Master has been a bit of a rogue. We had to relieve him of his manhood, we tried the visor today and it made the world of difference - John Dance, owner.

runners.comment: 0-18 on turf but he went close at Goodwood last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner who is 0-18 on turf but he's had some close calls in this sphere since May including when third at Goodwood (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; now steps back up in grade but he's on same mark as last time and should be in the mix again.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden
quotes.quote: Seamus was very happy with Londoner. He'll be a nice horse for next year and will probably start off at a mile and a quarter. - Chris Armstrong, stable's rep.

stable_tour.quote: We purchased him at the horses in training sale last autumn. He won a valuable Newcastle handicap a few weeks ago and there should be a lot of fun to be had with him still. He goes well at home, he's very straightforward and there are more nice races in him. 11-06-25

runners.comment: Prolific winner but he's been well held in last five runs; needs to rediscover his spark
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner, with six on AW and four on turf; last success was by 7l at Pontefract (1m2f, good) in April but he's been well held in his last five runs; on dangerous mark on this drop back in trip but he needs to rediscover his spark.

quotes.quote: King's Code is a big lad and, with the ground as quick as it was, I thought he might struggle a bit, which he did early doors. The cheekpieces helped him concentrate, the penny dropped late and he closed really well - Sean Levey, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Got back on the scoresheet with clearcut win at Haydock (1m2f) last month; key player
runners.spotlight: Mostly competitive off reduced marks for new yard this season and Haydock (1m2f, good to firm) five weeks ago saw him lead final 1f to break a long losing run, doing it in good style too; back up 5lb but he used to be effective on a range of ground and has returned to serious calculations.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap
quotes.quote: Gaassee wasn't as impressive as I'd hoped he'd be but he won and that was the most important thing. He was lugging a bit to his left and I don't know why. He looked a bit rusty. He's in the Hardwicke but I don't know if he's up to that yet, he will be a stakes horse in time. My record in the Ebor is appalling but he'll stay and he's progressive - William Haggas, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was formerly with William Haggas and had a short spell with Archie Watson too before coming to me. He's always had plenty of ability and was rated as high as 103 at one stage, although he's back down to 83 now. He ran very well in a classy handicap at Kempton on his latest start in March, finishing third behind Almosh'her and Arqoob, and is now back in full work after a short break. He'll be targeted at the Old Newton Cup at Haydock on July 5. 18-06-25

runners.comment: Won here in May but he hasn't threatened in last four runs and has work to do back in trip
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his last 26 starts but that came at Chester (1m4f, good) in May; only 2lb higher than for that success but he hasn't threatened in last four runs and his tactical speed will be tested back at this trip; others are more persuasive.

runners.breeder: Floors Farming & The Duke Of Devonshire
runners.comment: Hasn't been with James Owen for long and he needs watching in market; visor added
runners.spotlight: His last win was 16 months ago but that was off 3lb higher at Newmarket (1m, good); held in fourth of ten on stable debut at Chelmsford (7-2) last month but he had to use up some energy to get a prominent position from a wide draw; looks worth another try at this trip and he's not ruled out on second run for James Owen; visor added.

quotes.quote: Crack Shot is a nice, progressive horse. Oisin muttered something about the Cambridgeshire or the Silver Cambridgeshire so we'll see where we go, but he's definitely on an upward curve. We've always wondered whether he wanted a mile and a quarter or a mile. We were one of those people who learned nothing at Goodwood where the ground was too soft. He just doesn't want it like that - Alex Cole, representing owners Jim and Fitri Hay, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: He has an engine but there's a key to him and we're piecing together the puzzle. There are certain conditions he doesn't like, so we're making sure we get all the correct boxes ticked for him. Once we sort that there's definitely a decent race in him. 11-06-25

runners.comment: Good second at Beverley and has each-way claims if he can back that up
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he won in first-time cheekpieces at Hamilton (8.3f, good) in July and was runner-up behind Light Speed at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last time; only 1lb higher and has each-way claims if he can repeat that form.

runners.comment: C&D winner who had a nightmare with traffic issues here last time; dangerous
runners.spotlight: C&D win (good) in May and he's been placed here in his last two starts; finished a 5l third when last seen in July but that was behind a major improver and he had a nightmare with traffic issues; still has potential and could be a big factor if he gets some luck this time.

quotes.quote: Warren [Fentiman] gave Let's Dream an absolute peach of a ride. I told him to get in the first two or three and just control the race. With a low weight, Warren's claim and his two-year-old form he was well handicapped. He rode it to perfection and we've gone back-to-back in this. It's a lovely race to get in the bag but there's no immediate targets - Michael Bell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Chester's May meeting at 33-1. Like a lot of his sire's progeny, he favours a bit of dig in the ground and they did an unbelievable job watering at Chester, so the ground worked out for him there. He finished seventh at Ripon last Thursday having been second for a long way, trying to keep tabs on the leader. 09-06-25

runners.comment: In-form 4yo but this is tougher back up in grade and he looks weighted near best
runners.spotlight: Got back in the groove with an emphatic C&D win last month and he backed that up with a near-miss at Ayr (1m2f, good); gave it another good shot when third behind Spioradalta at Beverley but this is tougher back up in grade and he looks weighted near his best.

runners.comment: Resurgent 5yo who has won three of his last six and remains feasibly treated; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 5yo who has won three of his last six starts; got hampered and was awarded the race at Beverley (1m2f) two weeks ago but he still looks feasibly treated off this revised mark and is versatile ground-wise; respected in hat-trick bid.

runners.comment: Cashed in on reduced mark when beating Individualism at Beverley; respected off 3lb higher
runners.spotlight: Course winner who took advantage of a reduced mark when beating Individualism at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; back up 3b but he remains well handicapped on his best form and is still unexposed at this trip; possibilities.

quotes.quote: I thought Joanna [Mason] gave Light Speed an amazing ride. Obviously, it was a really tricky draw, but she's got a lovely split through and it was nice to see him bounce back to form. I felt that we needed to get him to run well back in trip before we stepped him back up. I thought it might have been on the sharp side for him, but I thought, given his stride length, that the track would suit him. He had a brilliant winter for Mr [Andrew] Balding and he's been in good form recently at home, so we were quite hopeful - Gemma Tutty, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Chester last year. He's a bit further behind in his prep because he didn't come back to me until late but he was looking like he had another win in him at the back end of last year off his current mark. Hopefully he can do the business when he gets back on the racetrack. 23-04-25

racecards.verdict: This looks wide open but the vote goes to BOX TO BOX, who had a near-miss at Chelmsford last time and is 4-10 at this track.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Box To Box, 6/1 Londoner,  Spioradalta, 7/1 Gaassee,  Crack Shot,  Let's Dream, 9/1 Rhythm Master,  Light Speed, 12/1 Glistening Nights, 16/1 Dream Harder,  Individualism, 28/1 Two Brothers

racecards.race_name: Oktoberfest Season Finale Saturday 20th September Handicap

runners.comment: Won here (7f) in May and he didn't get any luck when third here last time; respected
runners.spotlight: Produced a strong finish for a last-gasp success off this mark here (7f, good) in May; hasn't hit the same level since but he had some serious traffic issues when a creditable third here last month; looks interesting on this step back up in trip and could be a big factor if he gets the breaks.

quotes.quote: Probe is a straightforward horse and Jennie's done a great job getting his head right. We learnt the last time here that we probably got there a bit soon. We waited a bit longer today and the rain has helped. I'm really pleased as he's a proper horse - Alan O'Keeffe, assistant trainer.

Probe is progressing. The step up to a mile suited him last day and he has sharpened up since. He handles the ease well and I think he's versatile as to trip, I think he could get as far as 1m2f - Colin Keane, rider

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: I rode Probe on debut when he was plenty green and raw and I loved him. Oisin (Orr) said he was plenty green again the second day and said he'd appreciate going that little bit further and the step up to a mile with juice in the ground suited. It probably wasn't the strongest race but you'd like the way he did it and hopefully he can keep improving. I wouldn't think he's too far off Stakes level - Colin Keane, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He's a proper good sprinter on his day. He was a great servant for us in 2023 when he won the big sprint handicap on Guineas day at Newmarket. He's had a few setbacks but is on the way back and we'll be looking to start off again in January for the big all-weather handicaps. 05-12-24

runners.comment: Hit and miss since his Redcar win in April and this looks tough from wide draw
runners.spotlight: Recorded his ninth win when scoring at Redcar (7f, good to firm) in April but he's been hit and miss since and was beaten 12l at Thirsk last time; needs to bounce back again after that and this looks tough from stall 13.

quotes.quote: Cruyff Turn was eliminated from the Buckingham Palace Handicap at Royal Ascot so we came here as he likes the track and good ground. I hope this will give him a bit of confidence and we could aim him at the mile handicap at the Ebor meeting he won two years ago - William Easterby, assistant trainer.
We swerved races at Ascot and Goodwood as Cruyff Turn loves fast ground. He's always been a lovely horse, he's a big horse who has just taken time - Tim Easterby, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's training well and we're very happy with him. He's just as good over seven furlongs and a mile, and he likes good ground and doesn't want it too heavy. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Well held in last three runs and losing run is up to 13; opposable from wide draw
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner split between turf and AW; well treated on a couple of close calls in the spring but he's been well held on turf and AW in last three runs; now drops back into a Class 4 but losing run is now up to 13 and he looks opposable from wide draw.

quotes.quote: Blue Prince picked up and put the race to bed very smartly. He's an improving colt who has plenty of ability in the tank - Cam Hardie, jockey.

runners.comment: Won at Ripon last month and was good third in Class 2 there last time; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season and latest was at Ripon (1m, good to firm) on his penultimate run; held in third there last time but that was a Class 2 and he did well to hang around after being trapped out wide; still looks on a workable mark and he's respected on this big drop back in grade.

runners.comment: Two C&D wins this term; tough tasks last twice and could resume his progress back in grade
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row over C&D (good/soft) in June/July; no impact at Goodwood and York in last two starts but those were valuable handicaps and this lightly raced 3yo could resume his progress on this drop back in grade; not ruled out.

runners.owner: Ian O'Connor & The Fox And Hounds

prev_owners.owner: Ian O'Connor & The Fox And Hounds

runners.comment: Progressive 3yo who completed a double when scoring in good style at Galway; key player
runners.spotlight: 3yo who made his breakthrough at Ffos Las (7.3f, soft) in June and he completed a double when beating 14 rivals at Galway (7f, yielding) the following month; up another 6lb but something has really clicked in his last two runs and he's open to more progress; key player.

quotes.quote: It is soft Flat ground and It'sneverjustone loved it. He is a fine, big, strong horse and won nicely. I hope he is in again during the week! I've had two rides for David and two winners. He is an up-and-coming young trainer, so it's good - Declan McDonogh, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's a half-brother to Aberama Gold and El Hombre, who have won 20-odd races between them. He looks a pretty smart colt who I think will progress through the ranks each season. 30-05-24

runners.comment: Apart from third in Carlisle Bell he's been a bit disappointing this term; headgear added
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap, and apart from a close third in the Carlisle Bell (1m, soft) in June he's been a bit disappointing this term; cheekpieces are now added and he needs to get back near his best.

runners.comment: Only 1lb higher than for his last success but he needs to get back in the groove
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 7f and 1m this season but his form has cooled in last two runs and his latest seventh at Kempton was in a Class 4; only 1lb higher than for his last success but he needs to get back in the groove; regular hood is removed.

quotes.quote: That's Knights Gold's fourth win of the year and after the last one we thought we'd try something tougher and throw him in at the deep end. I thought it was an exceptionally competitive race today and Elizabeth [Gale] gave him a great ride - Richard Newland, trainer.

runners.comment: In-form 5yo and has claims if he responds well to a first-time visor
runners.spotlight: Won at Newmarket in May and he did well to finish third over C&D (good) in July, having taken a wide route into the straight; has remained in good form since and has claims if he responds well to first-time visor.

runners.comment: Five-time course winner who had an excuse at Haydock latest; respected back at Chester
runners.spotlight: Five-time course winner (5f-7f) on good or slower; returned to form and ran a big race from the front when second at Haydock (7f, good) last month, and can be excused subsequent lesser effort (finished lame); respected back at Chester.

quotes.race: tote+ Pays You More At tote.co.uk Handicap
quotes.quote: Paws For Thought jumped well and got himself into a nice pocket and travelled strongly. The boss had him nice and fresh and has done a good job with him - Richard Kingscote, jockey.

runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but he needs to kick on again on this return to turf
runners.spotlight: Won a Wolverhampton maiden (7f, AW) last month but was a well-held fourth of seven on handicap debut at Kempton ten days ago; still unexposed after only four starts but he needs to kick on again on this return to turf.

runners.comment: Off the mark with comfortable win in hood at Thirsk (7f) last month; respected up 5lb
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when overcoming trouble to score with hood added at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) last month; up 5lb but he found plenty of progress with that comfortable success and he's respected.

runners.comment: Made all on yard debut at Chelmsford (1m) and always close up for 2nd here (6f) on Friday
runners.spotlight: Joined this trainer for 21,000gns in July and immediately recouped some of that outlay when making all at Chelmsford (1m; 25-1) last month; hasn't had many opportunities on turf (0-5) but has a 6lb lower mark than on AW; always-prominent second over 6f here on Friday showed he is one to consider.

quotes.race: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap
quotes.quote: I think the gelding (operation) has helped and a bit of a break and Giant has come back fresh and well. Everything went to plan and he's done it nicely - Richard Spencer, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is the progressive 3yo IT'SNEVERJUSTONE (nap). who has won in good style at Ffos Las and Galway in his last two starts.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 It'sneverjustone, 4/1 Fire Flame, 7/1 Miami Matrix,  Giant, 15/2 Capital Guarantee, 10/1 Paws For Thought, 11/1 Be Frank, 14/1 Probe,  Knights Gold, 16/1 Sailthisshipalone,  Marquis Pompeo, 20/1 Cruyff Turn

racecards.race_name: John Smith's Jump Jockeys Challenge Handicap (Professional Jump Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race) (Div I)

runners.comment: Three-time winner for the O'Meara yard; unraced over this far which is a worry
runners.spotlight: Bought for 20,000gns out of the O'Meara yard for which he won three times, the latest over 1m2f at Ripon in June; well held at York since then; unraced over this far which is a worry.

stable_tour.quote: I was happy with his first run this year and then it was a tactical race last time. I'm not sure about his trip, I've run over a mile and a quarter a few times and we might run him over shorter. He looks progressive. 19-04-24
He's had a couple of setbacks since we bought him two years ago and he has struggled with his consistency. He's on a bit of a break and will be back for the all-weather season. 26-09-24

runners.comment: Recent AW winner but arrives here with a record of 0-20 on turf
runners.spotlight: Won by a head at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) but only seventh under a penalty when upped to a Class 4 at Southwell; all four wins on the AW and 0-20 on turf.

stable_tour.quote: He's still a maiden after six races but is definitely improving. He ran a very good second at Goodwood last month and went up 2lb, rather harshly. He's owned by Australians and if I can get him up one more grade, he could be worth taking over there. I ran him in a hot handicap at Haydock at the start of the month and he ran well to finish fifth. We'll stick to handicaps now. 13-07-22

runners.comment: C&D winner in June and excuses next time; running well when falling over hurdles latest
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m4f here in June (soft) and got upset in the preliminaries before finishing tailed off on his return visit; was running well when falling back over hurdles; entitled to be involved.

runners.comment: Two 1m4f wins in five runs for this yard; chance if the ground is genuinely good
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f wins in five runs for this yard and got competitive a good way out when below par last time, having previously run well in a competitive handicap hurdle; this ground could be borderline (enjoys good or quicker) but worth considering.

quotes.quote: Nibras Gold's an improving horse as well as being gelded. He thinks he's done when he hits the front but the horse who creeped up my inside made him go again. He makes hard work of it - Ray Dawson, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Ballylinch Stud, P Inglett & N Jones

runners.comment: Continued good run of form when runner-up in a similar race to this at Epsom on Thursday
runners.spotlight: Mixes the Flat with hurdling and won under both codes this year; continued his good run of form when runner-up in a similar race to this at Epsom on Thursday (soft); respected if turned out again.

quotes.quote: This is a better track for Saligo Bay than Chester and he deserved a win, although he wants a bit of dig in the ground. He'd stay further, but I think we'll stay at a mile and a half and hopefully he'll improve and win another, while I think he's the type who would do well hurdling - Roger Charlton, trainer.

runners.comment: An assured stayer who is capable off this mark and could have a part to play; new yard
runners.spotlight: Below best at York in June and since left David O'Meara for 20,000gns; an assured stayer who is capable off this mark and could have a part to play.

quotes.race: BetUK. It's Where The UK Bets Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: I think two miles last time just stretched Heathen. He seems to be a horse who runs well fresh. He's quite delicate to handle and Dave [O'Meara, trainer] does a great job with horses like him. It all went smoothly, I was a bit worried about lack of pace but, even when the raced turned into a sprint, he could still travel into it well - Jason Watson, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He's the best-bred horse we have as he's by Lope De Vega out of an Irish Oaks winner. He's much better on the all-weather than turf. After the last all-weather season we put him away and we'll bring him back for a full all-weather campaign. He's back down to a good mark and tends to go well following a break, so first time out he'll be ready to go. David O'Meara thinks the break could have really benefited him and he could be one to follow through the winter. 26-09-24

runners.comment: Has struggled badly in three runs (two hurdling) following a long absence
runners.spotlight: Following a long absence this 9yo wasn't the force of old over hurdles earlier this year and was tailed off back on the Flat three weeks ago.

quotes.race: White Horse Kids Eat Free Handicap
quotes.quote: Cormier is tough and consistent and always manages to pull it out of the bag. He stays two miles so I was a bit worried about the trip, but he loved it. They were Brian's [Ellison] orders to bring the horse wide - Harry Russell, jockey.

quotes.race: bet365 Morebattle Hurdle (A Handicap Hurdle) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Cormier was very keen when we first got him but he's settled now. He's grown up a bit and he's a lot easier at home. Early on Sean said he was off the bit, they were going that quick, then he's come hard on it round the top bend. If anything he got there too early but he tries. Taking the hurdles out didn't bother him because he's won a few on the Flat. Cheltenham will be different but we know he acts round there. Whether he's good enough doesn't matter, he's won a 100,000GBP race and his owner lives just over the road from Cheltenham - Brian Ellison, trainer.

quotes.race: SSS Super Alloys Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Cormier made one mistake at three out, I know it's on a different course but he did exactly the same thing in the Greatwood. He came back on the bridle fairly quickly after and I was always happy. Every time I asked he was always giving me a bit, he's a nice horse and they'll have plenty of fun seeing where he ends up. It could be here in March (County Hurdle) - Jonny Burke, jockey.

Cormier has got very good hurdle form and he's fresh off a run from Pontefract where he won quite easily. My only doubt for today was the sun drying the ground out but his class brought him through it and he got away with it. His owner Dan Gilbert loves dual-purpose horses so I'd say he'll do a bit of both - Ben Robinson, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well in the Greatwood but the ground was too quick for him. We're going to go novice chasing now, which has always been the plan this season. We haven't started schooling him over fences yet, but he likes the soft ground and the intention is to head to Newcastle in January. 20-11-22

runners.comment: Won three of his last five starts and capable of a bold show off this mark; has form here
runners.spotlight: Three thirds from five races at Chester, including a division of this 12 months ago; has won three of his last five starts and capable of a bold show off this mark.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Green Team had a bit of a hold-up with a little problem with a foot and we gave him time. I'm not sure if he'll run again this season, he's going to be a lovely horse over ten furlongs next year - Kevin Ryan, trainer.

runners.comment: In a good run of form and only 1lb higher than when a rallying fourth last week at Haydock
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles and now in a good run of form on the Flat, following an easy win at Ripon with a rallying fourth over Haydock's extended 1m3f (good); just 1lb higher here.

runners.comment: 1m1f/1m3f winner; could have run better last time but she's still lightly raced
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; it was a Class 6 handicap she won at Ffos Las (1m1f, good) and then only fourth in a Class 4 at Southwell; still one to consider, having won a 1m3f handicap with give underfoot.

racecards.verdict: The 6yo GREEN TEAM usually runs well here and he returns on the back of a string of good efforts.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Spartan Times, 4/1 Nibras Gold, 6/1 Saligo Bay, 8/1 Percy Jones,  Heathen,  Green Team,  Cotswold Cottage, 10/1 Westernesse, 25/1 Polling Day, 33/1 Cormier

racecards.race_name: John Smith's Jump Jockeys Challenge Handicap (Professional Jump Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race) (Div II)

runners.comment: Six-time winner on turf last year but lacking a recent run is probably not ideal
runners.spotlight: Won six times on turf last year and still not handicapped out of contention as he's only 3lb higher than for the last of those wins (1m4f, soft); lacking a recent run is probably not ideal.

stable_tour.quote: He's been in great form, having won his last three races, going up from 46 to 61 in the ratings. He's entered in the 1m2f handicap at Brighton on Wednesday, and won over two furlongs further at the track last time. However, he is ground dependent - he'd like nice going and if it got very quick there we might have to think about whether he runs or not. He's a lovely horse. 07-08-24

runners.comment: 1m4f winner this summer; back into a Class 4 here but might be high in the weights
runners.spotlight: Running well prior to breakthrough win at York (1m4f) this summer and didn't enjoy a clear run when fourth of nine at Windsor (11.4f), though wasn't unlucky; back into a Class 4 here but might be high in the weights.

runners.comment: Five runs in handicaps have yielded two seconds and a win; visor on for cheekpieces
runners.spotlight: Five runs in handicaps have yielded two seconds and a win, the victory coming narrowly at Newmarket last time (1m2f; second run in cheekpieces); only 1lb higher, stays this far and now goes in a visor; hard to rule out.

quotes.race: Harrogate Theatre Panto Tickets Now On-Sale Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was a lovely introduction. I've never been to Ripon before but I had a walk round and it's quite undulating. Sir Edward Lear was learning on the job, Sean gave him a lovely ride - James Owen, trainer.

runners.breeder: E A R L Haras Du Logis Et Al
runners.comment: Course winner and forgiven last two defeats; ideally wants further, though
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m (good) here in June and the saddle slipped on his return visit the following month; can probably forgive him his Ascot run as well as that was a Class 3; could find this trip on the tight side, though.

runners.comment: Pulled very hard on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m4f) so did well to finish so close
runners.spotlight: Only had four races and did well to be beaten under 3l on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m4f AW) given how hard he pulled; the ability is there to go well if giving himself a chance.

runners.comment: Placed over 1m2f here last month before finishing midfield last week at Haydock
runners.spotlight: The winner of two novices, on fast and slow ground; placed over 1m2f here last month before finishing midfield last week at Haydock (extended 1m3f); in the mix.

stable_tour.quote: This colt is another example of us working closely with the 'Rogues'. Obviously he has a lovely pedigree and was purchased at the breeze-ups. He never saw a racecourse until April when a very impressive winner at Yarmouth over 1m2f. He didn't enjoy the heavy ground next time out when second to Perfuse, but on much quicker ground at Ripon last time he was a comfortable winner under a penalty. He had been given an entry in the King George V Stakes at Royal Ascot and was around 16-1 when we scratched him. We have had to be very patient with this colt and Tony [Elliott] and the Rogues have been very supportive, as they always are. He has won over 1m2f and 1m3f and I don't think 1m4f will pose any problems and he could even get 1m6f in time. His opening handicap mark is 90, which was to be expected after his two wins in novice company. There is a three-year-old handicap at Ascot which is run on that extraordinarily busy Saturday when the July Cup is run which is an option, but he is entered to run in the 1m6f handicap at Haydock this week. There is definitely more to come from him this year. 06-07-23

runners.comment: Recent efforts none too inspiring but has twice hit the frame on four visits here
runners.spotlight: Nosed home in a four-runner race at Epsom in July (1m2f, good to firm) but comfortably beaten in the meantime; on the plus side, he has twice hit the frame on four visits here.

runners.comment: 1m4f winner last winter but nothing good to take from his subsequent races
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Harry Charlton; won over 1m4f (AW) on yard debut last winter but nothing good to take from his subsequent races.

runners.comment: Has to be respected as a dual C&D winner last summer; nothing wrong with his mark
runners.spotlight: Some sign of returning to form last week at Down Royal and has to be respected as a dual C&D winner last summer; nothing wrong with his mark and conditions should be about ideal.

runners.breeder: S C E A Marmaion Vauville & Alain Jathie
runners.comment: 12-race maiden and ran moderately last time; did go close over C&D in July
runners.spotlight: Just missed out over C&D three runs back but remains a maiden and finished last of the six on her final run; clearly a contender if replicating that run here in July.

racecards.verdict: Last week's Down Royal run after a break was quite encouraging, so GREY FABLE is taken to record a third C&D success.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Sir Edward Lear, 4/1 Secret Beach, 5/1 Rogue Sea, 7/1 Kokinelli, 8/1 Playtime, 10/1 Giselles Defence,  Grey Fable, 16/1 Tailorman, 25/1 Three Dons, 50/1 Arazzo

racecards.race_name: PJ Towey Construction Handicap

runners.comment: Handicap debutant who is on a handy mark based on the pick of his Listed/Group form
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant who is unlikely to defy top weight judged on 2025 efforts; however, very useful on his day and he's is on a handy mark based on the pick of his Listed/Group form.

quotes.race: MansionBet Best Odds Guaranteed EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We didn't expect Cash to win first time out but we've always thought he was a nice horse and he's a nice prospect for next year - Anthony Burke, owner's racing manager.

stable_tour.quote: I was delighted with his run at Windsor in a Listed race last time out. I know he was beaten six lengths, but he's been plagued with injuries at the wrong time. He got another fracture, of his humerus this time, in the spring and it was a little bit of a rush back. I don't think he stays a mile and a half but what he did for nine-tenths of that race was very good. I think if we'd have run him over shorter than that distance he'd have been off the bridle earlier, whereas he only got tired very late on at Windsor. It'll have served a very good purpose. He hasn't done a lot wrong and he enjoys a bit of juice in the ground. He's got a QEII entry but whether we go there will depend on his next run - I'd imagine that will be in two to three weeks. I think he retains all his old ability. We'll drop him back to ten furlongs or a mile. 04-09-24

runners.comment: On a long losing sequence but ran well (close second) in this race last year
runners.spotlight: Placed several times this season, showing he retains plenty of ability aged nine; remains on a long losing sequence but ran well (close second) in this race last year and has each-way hopes.

quotes.quote: Bopedro just found it a bit sharp here but was staying on in fifth, so they all ran well - David O'Meara, trainer.

Ours were needing the run and the Lincoln run might have just put Bopedro right. Lee Bond (owner) told me to leave the visor off as it might just freshen him up so I have to give him the credit for it. He had good form for Jessie Harrington and there is a good programme for these milers - David O'Meara, trainer.

We had Bopedro at the end of last year and had a good run at Leopardstown. He won well for Mr Bolger here in the past so we were hoping the track would bring about some improvement in him. He's a bit of a boyo and is a good lead horse at home, he's been leading Lucky Vega for the last while - Shane Foley, rider

quotes.race: Barry's Tea Supporting Marymount Hospice Handicap
quotes.quote: Myself and Kevin were just saying beforehand that we couldn't believe the horse was 40-1. He has good form in France and was winning around Fontainebleau and running well at Vichy for his previous connections. They're no backwards tracks, just a drop down from Parisian courses. We haven't had the horse for long and took a chance with him. Kevin said he handled the track and ground well, I'd say slower ground would be no problem to him either. He's a lovely-looking horse who is great to deal with, uncomplicated, and it's nice to get our first winner for Mr Zhang and Yulong, having hit the crossbar with one or two before. We have about a half dozen for him - Ger Flynn, representative for Jim Bolger, trainer of Bopedro

stable_tour.quote: He could go for the mile handicap at Newmarket on Thursday as he likes the track there. He's done a good bit of work and has been in good form, so we're looking forward to starting him back. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Has failed to win since 2yo days but remains well treated on peak form
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in the Lincoln here on return from spell in Australia; not in same form subsequently and has failed to win since his 2yo days; current mark is handy judged on peak RPRs.

quotes.quote: We were worried about the draw, worried about the ground but he's just done it. Me and my wife are elated. I thought Galleron was unlucky at Doncaster. I was hoping for a place and he came fourth, just was half a length behind the third - Gary Robinson, owner.

stable_tour.quote: The Hampton Court Stakes is where we could try him, but I need to speak to the owners before confirming it. I'd love to see him over a mile and a quarter ? we've got to bite the bullet and try it one day, so why not here? He finished fourth in the 2,000 Guineas and was fifth in the Irish Guineas, although he finished closer to the winner than at Newmarket. Both times he hit the line really well and should get the extra distance on the way he races. 13-06-23

He won twice last year including the Goffs Million, where he earned his owners ¤611,000. He has run well without success this season, notably when a good fourth to Chaldean in the 2,000 Guineas. His subsequent effort at the Curragh in the Irish Guineas could have seen him finish much closer if things had gone his way. I know he's better than that fifth behind Paddington and his subsequent ninth in the St James's Palace Stakes on Tuesday. 21-06-23

runners.comment: Record is only 1-11 but a couple of good seconds last month suggest his turn is near
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good/firmer; record is only 1-11 but good seconds at Haydock (1m) and Chester (7.5f; cheekpieces applied) last month suggest his turn is near; one for the shortlist.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well when second at Kempton last month, just bumping into an impressive winner. He had a really solid blow, so I think he'll improve a great deal. The Esher Cup could be his next port of call but we've got half an eye on the Italian 2,000 Guineas. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Perhaps best to forgive latest effort; generally progressive otherwise
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly on good/firmer; has never scored when fresh, so perhaps it's best to forgive latest effort (back from a break); generally progressive otherwise and has won five of his last nine starts.

runners.comment: Ran well in first couple of British starts; backward steps since
runners.spotlight: Two wins in France last term; ran well in first couple of British starts, including in the Britannia at Royal Ascot, but has taken backward steps since; resurgent display is required.

runners.comment: Close second, showing useful form, in two races switched to handicap level; respected
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo; 1-1 on softer than good (debut); close second, showing useful form, in fast-ground events over 1m at Thirsk and Goodwood since switched to handicap level after his wind/gelding operations; appears to have a race of this nature in him.

quotes.quote: Treble Tee is learning now. He did things the wrong way round in the first part of the year but he settled nicely there and came home really strongly. He's turning into a good horse. He'll get a penalty for the Cambridgeshire and we'll talk about it seriously - Ed Crisford, joint-trainer.
Treble Tree is a nice horse for the future. We'll put him away for the winter and he'll get a mile in time - Ed Crisford, joint-trainer.

prev_owners.owner: W J and T C O Gredley

runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival last month on return from lengthy absence
runners.spotlight: Completed a hurdles hat-trick in 2023; failed to beat a rival last month on return from lengthy absence and needs to prove he retains ability; raced mainly over longer trips.

quotes.quote: It was a good performance, Father Of Jazz has always been a keen horse but he's getting the hang of jumping now. Aidan rode him lovely and professionally and he said he was always going to pick them up in the straight. We'll give him a little rest now and probably go for a handicap somewhere - we could look at the Summer Hurdle at Market Rasen. We'll see what the handicapper does, but he'll keep going through the summer - James Owen, trainer.

quotes.race: Kempton Park Working With The Community Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We have been trying to run for the last three weeks but the ground just keeps going and then we thought it might again with the rain that came. Father Of Jazz has battled that out well for a Flat horse so that was pleasing. We've talked about the big handicap hurdle at Market Rasen on Summer Plate day and that is our plan. I thought we would go straight there but Aidan wants to give him one more run before we go there - James Owen, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He just had a dirty nose which ruled him out of running at Cheltenham on Sunday. I'll find him a race in a fortnight and I'm very positive about him as a novice hurdler this season. 15-11-22

He was my first winner under rules, at Kempton in May, and then won his next two. He's a top-of-the-ground horse who's having a rest and will come back next year. He's a two-miler and a very fast horse. He has some very decent form and would be quite a classy horse for next summer. Flat tracks suit him and he could be one for the Summer Hurdle. 06-12-23

runners.comment: Has a question mark over whether he retains peak ability
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Father Of Jazz, with a question mark over whether he retains peak ability; needs to improve on last week's effort, which came after a lengthy layoff.

quotes.quote: Isla Kai ran a great race last time at Newbury and it's fantastic to win this old race, I'd never won it before. It's great for the owners Martin and Lisa Webb, they are loyal supporters to the yard - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a bit unfortunate but has run some solid races in defeat in top handicaps this season. He ran a cracking race when sixth in the Royal Hunt Cup but, while he doesn't want soft ground, he desperately doesn't need firm ground. We would have run him last weekend but there was no rain about at all. We might run him at the Shergar Cup meeting at Ascot next month and then at York in the mile handicap at the Ebor meeting. They're quite close together but I'll be disappointed if he doesn't run well in at least one of those. 27-07-22

runners.comment: Won at Glorious Goodwood; totally blew the start last time and comes with risk
runners.spotlight: Has developed a habit of starting slowly, markedly so last time (lost all chance); showed he's a useful sort with Glorious Goodwood success (1m) in July but comes with some risk.

runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in sole run this term; debut for new yard after 65,000gns sale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2024 campaign with AW win but failed to beat a rival, back on turf, in sole run this term (May); sold out of Charlie Appleby's yard for 65,000gns in July; market may guide.

prev_owners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.comment: Tough sort but may struggle to follow up Beverley win; goes back up two grades
runners.spotlight: Tough sort but has done his winning this year in Class 4, including at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) last time when taking record to 8-55; faces a tougher task back up two grades.

quotes.quote: I had no idea whether I'd won or not, but Urban Sprawl is game and he's in good form. He was really gunning for the line and he was able to stick his head down where it matters. He's tough - David Probert, rider.

quotes.race: Twickenham Nursery Handicap (Jockey Club Grassroots Nursery Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Urban Sprawl probably won't go to Carlisle on Thursday. I don't know what Darley will want to do with him. He's quite exposed for one of theirs and is what he is, but is very genuine and Andrea said he could go a bit further, which could be key in getting more out of him. I don't think ground matters to him - Mark Johnston, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sir Robert Ogden

runners.comment: Went close at York in June; inconsistent otherwise this year
runners.spotlight: Well suited by York and went close there in June; inconsistent otherwise during a 2025 campaign that has been markedly less productive than 2024; cheekpieces refitted for the first time since 2022; opposed.

quotes.race: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
quotes.quote: Tolstoy is unreal. We might step him up to a mile next as he's not stopping in his races. He's settled now and is grand. He'll come back here for something at the Ebor meeting - Brian Ellison, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Proud To Support Macmillan Handicap
quotes.quote: Tolstoy didn't have a penalty so we thought it was worth giving him another shot. Everything was fine this morning and he was a lot more settled in the paddock today. He was very game in the race - he's tough - Brian Ellison, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Tolstoy was well entitled to do that and the race didn't look to have too much depth to it. His last run was good and he likes soft ground. He's quick and I can see him staying over six furlongs - he should get stronger and quicker with a winter on his back - Rab Havlin, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: A recent purchase who hasn't run for us yet. He's a grand horse who ran a lot for Stuart Williams. He wouldn't have the best of feet, but he's fine and has quite good form. He'll run through the summer at Saturday meetings at places like York. 03-04-24

runners.comment: Proving consistent; close fifth in warm 3yo handicap at York last time; solid claims
runners.spotlight: Successful in Newbury maiden on reappearance and has consistent form since, close fifth in warm 3yo handicap at the York Ebor festival (1m, good to firm) most recently; solid claims.

runners.comment: Seems to be on his way back to winning form; suited by soft/heavy on turf
runners.spotlight: Very productive on turf/AW in 2024 and last two efforts suggest he's on his way back to winning form; one to consider provided the ground is ideal (suited by soft/heavy in this sphere).

racecards.verdict: Unexposed 3yo TREBLE TEE gets the vote ahead of Hawksbill and Point Of Contact.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Treble Tee, 7/1 Hawksbill, 8/1 Cash,  Great Chieftain, 9/1 Galeron,  Point Of Contact, 11/1 Bopedro, 12/1 Supido, 14/1 Urban Sprawl,  Atlantic Gamble, 16/1 Principality, 20/1 Wild Nature, 40/1 Isla Kai,  Tolstoy, 50/1 Father Of Jazz

racecards.race_name: Betfred Champagne Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.comment: Had subsequent winners behind at Ayr on debut but this is a severe rise in class
runners.spotlight: Brings the least compelling form credentials, for all that he accounted for subsequent winners with a strong finish to collect by 2l at Ayr in early July (6f, good to soft; 10-1); clearly useful but whether he's up to this level remains to be seen.

quotes.race: Ayrshire Cancer Support EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Cape Ashizuri showed signs of his inexperience but you'd have to like how he came home. We've liked him from day one but we've always thought he's a seven furlong horse who has the speed for six - Sean Quinn, joint-trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.breeder: Sas Le Thenney - Pearl Champs Elysses
runners.comment: Looks very progressive after impressing in a valuable sales race at Deauville a month ago
runners.spotlight: Unraced at Group level but looks highly progressive after finishing well on top in a valuable conditions race over Deauville's extended 6f (soft) a month ago (the second has since won a handicap); quotes would suggest he's held in high regard by powerful connections and dangerous to pass over here, especially should it get wet.

quotes.quote: I suppose if you ever get a horse who is qualified for this race, you've always got to have one eye on it. Cape Orator wants to go further and we've always thought he was very talented so we put him in the Champagne Stakes and the Royal Lodge later in the year. When they're fit and well and when this pops up at six and a half furlongs, we were more than happy to take our chance. He's a stunning horse with a frame that will make a three-year-old, so he was never going to be overraced at two. But he's always shown Ralph [Beckett, trainer], from very early on, that he's got a lot of talent and I think he's showed it there. He could potentially be a Group horse and at halfway I thought it was maybe looking a little bit sharp, but Hector [Crouch] said as soon as he sat into him, his word was 'electric' - Alex Elliott, owners' representative.

runners.comment: It was never in much doubt under a prominent ride in winning the Group 3 Acomb at York
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 7f on good to firm; looked very nice when overcoming signs of inexperience at Newbury and confirmed that promise in winning the Group 3 Acomb at York, a race that the yard's future 2,000 Guineas winner Chaldean also won en route to taking this and the Dewhurst; likeably straightforward and it was never in much doubt last time after he raced close to the pace throughout; hard to knock.

quotes.quote: Gewan is unbelievable, he took me through the race so easily, I couldn't believe how well I was going inside the two-furlong pole. I loved his attitude when he hit the front. He had a look around but he was never going to get beaten - PJ McDonald, jockey. Gewan is a horse we've liked since he came into the yard. He was very well bought by Billy Jackson-Stops, not for big money. I think PJ was quite taken aback by his attitude and the fact he still had a little more left. I think he'll stay a mile no problem next season but we'll stick to seven this year - Andrew Balding, trainer.

runners.comment: Fifth in a strong maiden on debut before coasting home in front by 8l at Sandown; exciting
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt; caught on the wing when fifth in a traditionally strong maiden at Newmarket's July festival, the form of which could barely have worked out better; duly built on that when coasting home in front by 8l at Sandown (7f, good), in the process posting smart closing fractions; has the size and scope to keep improving and he's an exciting prospect.

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

prev_owners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.comment: 0-5 but placed in two Group 2s over 6f and stepping up 7f is unlikely to pose any problems
runners.spotlight: Kept to 6f thus far and has advanced his RPRs with each run; hardly has the profile of a typical Champagne winner after five defeats, but twice runner-up in Group 2s (comfortably held admittedly) and a slow start did for him in the Group 1 Phoenix Stakes a month ago; battle hardened and stepping up 7f is unlikely to pose any problems, so by no means discounted.

racecards.verdict: Gewan is the one to beat but not many Sandown maidens are won by the 8l margin that OXAGON recorded in late July.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Gewan, 13/8 Oxagon, 5/1 Cape Orator, 9/1 Puerto Rico, 14/1 Cape Ashizuri

racecards.race_name: Betfred Portland (Heritage Handicap)

runners.comment: Beaten a nose in this race 12 months ago but this season has been underwhelming
runners.spotlight: Has gone close in a host of top sprint handicaps, including when beaten a nose in a strong running of this race 12 months ago; he'd be a leading contender in that sort of form but this season has been underwhelming and he faded into last of nine in a Listed race three weeks ago; needs a revival.

quotes.quote: Apollo One has been very unlucky, but he's been giving 15lb to improving handicappers. I think at Doncaster he actually thought he'd won. He doesn't really like that ground but there you go. That's him done for the year and he'll go home and rest now - Peter Charalambous, co-trainer.

I'm not sure that the rain has helped Apollo One as it just blunts his acceleration. He is not the biggest, but you don't have to be. He has got a heart like his mother Boonga Roogeta, who was as tough as nails. He is just a lovely, lovely horse. I'd say we will probably go off to Epsom now for the valuable six-furlong handicap on Derby Day - Peter Charalambous, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Get Your Ladbrokes Daily Odds Boost Spring Cup Stakes (Listed Race) (AW Championships F-T Qual')
quotes.quote: I would think that Apollo One would drop back to six furlongs for the sprint on all-weather finals day after that - Martin Harley, jockey.

runners.breeder: J Hanly, A Stroud & Skymarc Farm
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo; second in Group 3 in the spring has worked out well; could be unexposed
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo whose best effort by some way came when second in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, good to firm) in April; that form has worked out well, with winner Big Mojo landing last week's Sprint Cup and third-placed Ain't Nobody taking second in the Nunthorpe, and he had an excuse (finished lame) when down the field in Group 1 Commonwealth Cup on his only subsequent outing; has since changed trainers (same ownership and stable); may still be unexposed.

runners.comment: Gained seventh Chester win in August; fair run next time but he'll need a career best here
runners.spotlight: Has won on a straight track in Bahrain but best known for his exploits around Chester, winning for the seventh time there in 6f Listed race (good to firm) in August; far from disgraced in big-field handicap at York next time but he'll need another career best to get back on the winning trail.

quotes.quote: Roman Dragon is an absolute star and it was a first-class ride by Jason [Watson]. He read the race supremely well, but the horse was very tough. Turning in I thought we were going to win by even further as we were going so well. He loves it here and he's a sprint handicapper who pulled it out of the bag in a stakes race. I'm not sure he can go to a much higher level than this, but he's turned his love for this track into a massive advantage. He goes so well here and that's worth half a dozen pounds ? it was probably the difference today. Chester made a nice rug for him and he's a history maker - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

Roman Dragon loves Chester. That's now six wins here for this horse, which has equalled the modern-day track record. We had a nice enough draw [five] and everything went perfectly - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

Nick (Hughes, part-owner)'s ambition is for Roman Dragon to become the winningmost horse around Chester and we think he needs six. We're two-thirds of the way there. I'm delighted, he won off 84 last year and 79 here so maybe we can get another before the season's out - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's such a star and has had two good winters in Bahrain. He has a higher rating than he's ever won off, although he did come agonisingly close a couple of times last summer, but I don't think winning off this mark is out of the question. He loves Chester and will go to the May meeting. 09-04-25

He seems better than ever this year and won nicely at Chester early last month over 6f. He returned there 13 days later and put up another good performance when second to Count D'Orsay. There are no meetings at Chester in August, heaven knows why, so we'll wait until September 2 to take him back there over 6f. He has run four of his five races this year at our local track, which comes about because his owners live in Chester and he clearly loves the track and doesn't like many others. 17-08-23

runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

prev_owners.owner: Brian Jordan, B Jordan & Stephen Jordan
Brian Jordan, B Jordan & Stephen Jordan

runners.comment: Seven wins in 2024; encouraging fifth at York's Ebor meeting; solid each-way prospects
runners.spotlight: Won seven times at 5f-6f last season; unplaced all four outings this term but he looks to be building up to something again and was beaten only in a blanket finish when fifth of 20 at York's Ebor meeting; will be fresher than many in this field and he has solid each-way prospects.

quotes.quote: The problem is with William [Buick] he's that good he wins by too far! He's rode me a winner or two over the years and he's obviously a first-class jockey. It was amazing for Jordan Electrics to do what he did carrying so much weight. He's superb to train; at one time he was difficult to travel, but he's mellowing as he gets older. We'll probably come back here for the Scottish Stewards' Cup next - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Unfortunately he picked up an injury so he's going to be off for the first half of the season, but we're thinking he'll be back and it's not career-ending. He's sound but he's going to need time to get over what happened. We'd like to win an Ayr Gold Cup, so we'll target that and possibly the Portland. That's a fickle double, it's usually one or the other. He's a grand horse. Technically at his age he's supposed to be diminishing but he doesn't seem to be. The man who feeds him rides him and he's managed very well. He's a Hamilton specialist. It's probably going to be a bit more difficult for him now, but he's thereabouts in those top sprints. 07-05-25

runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner
runners.comment: 6-17; gained biggest success in large field at York in August; high on the list again
runners.spotlight: Likeable type with a steadily progressive record and a fine strike-rate (6-17); gained his biggest success in 20-runner race at York (5.4f, good to firm) in August, travelling smoothly and just doing enough; does not look overburdened with a 5lb rise and this course winner is high on the list again.

quotes.race: Packhorse Moulton Supports Racing Welfare Handicap
quotes.quote: Trefor is an improving horse who enjoyed the quick ground - Rab Havlin, rider.

runners.breeder: Hellwood Stud Farm & R C Dollar
runners.comment: Followed York Listed win with three well-below-par efforts; revival required
runners.spotlight: Developing something of an all-or-nothing profile and has followed a win in a 5f Listed race at York in May with three well-below-par efforts, most recently back at York when meeting some trouble; slipping in the weights but needs to get back on track.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: We've got options at Ascot and we've got to see if we chance Tropical Storm over six furlongs or go against the older horses over five in the King Charles III Stakes. It's a nice problem to have. One of those races will be his next target - Andrew Balding, trainer.

quotes.race: Julia Graves Roses Stakes (Listed Race) (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Tropical Storm was super. He was a bit out of control at Goodwood but not here and he was fantastic. He got a bit idle in front but when pulling up he took hold of the bit again - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

I have a lot of belief in Tropical Storm. I said before the race that he's probably the best maiden in the country, and that was a super run. Hopefully he can win at this level. He has size, and a big future - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

runners.comment: Arrives in top form after thirds over 6f and 5f; this intermediate trip should be ideal
runners.spotlight: Only two wins from 26 attempts but he arrives here in top form after recent thirds at Goodwood (6f Stewards' Cup) and Ascot (5f); this intermediate distance ought to be ideal and he should be in the thick of things again in first-time cheekpieces.

quotes.race: La Celia Wines Sprint Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Jakajaro was a horse I thought would win a maiden. This is a good horse. He was still green today and hopefully he will go on. He's in the big sales race here in September and that will be in his target - David Marnane, trainer

runners.comment: Six unplaced efforts this term and he's on the back-burner for now; visored for first time
runners.spotlight: Represents the same connections as Jakajaro but has much more to prove after six unplaced efforts this term; beginning to get some help from the handicapper but he needs the visor (replacing cheekpieces) to have a positive effect.

quotes.quote: It's been a big team effort with this horse as he's as quirky as they come. Seven Questions spent the night at the racecourse last night as we know when he gets on the horsebox, he gets very stirred up. So, he had an away night about half a mile from his stable. We saddled him about an hour ago and brought him down - he was trying to kick everyone. He's a talented horse on his day and got a brilliant ride. We'll have to re-evaluate where we go now as he was very much at a crossroads. We'll have to see whether he is a Royal Ascot horse - George Scott, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was a rock-solid two-year-old and was very unlucky not to win a couple of those big races he was competing in. He ran well on his first start in Dubai this winter then slightly tailed off, but you're not going to get everything right all the time. He's come back in good order and he'll be kept busy this season. I imagine we'll aim him at the Holyroodhouse Stakes and he might just need a couple of runs before then to get him in the zone and spot on for Ascot. He gives me every indication that he's trained on from last year. 29-03-24

runners.comment: Had productive AW season but his three turf runs since have been well below par
runners.spotlight: Had a productive AW season which culminated in a valuable win at Newcastle on Good Friday; his three turf runs since have been well below par and, although he had a possible excuse at York last time (hit stall, disputed strong pace), others have far more obvious claims.

quotes.quote: We Never Stop didn't have the best of draws but he has such a high cruising speed I was able to get him up there. The key to him is the first half of the race and getting him switched off - Shane Gray, jockey.

Everything fell into place today really. We Never Stop jumped well, and they went a blistering gallop, so I just sat off the lead a little bit and it worked out really well. I had something to aim at going to the furlong pole, and he had a good look when he got there, but he's done it comfortably in the end - Shane Gray, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: First past the post in this race in 2022 but tailed off on belated reappearance; risky
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner and also first past the post in this race in 2022 (demoted to second after causing interference); well treated if retaining all his ability but he was tailed off in the Shergar Cup on his reappearance after a long absence, having hit his head on the stalls; risky.

quotes.quote: We like Chipstead a lot but he never really produces until the back-end of the season but he's proved us wrong this year. Hopefully he can step up next time out. He was outpaced early on and I said to Fred that he does stay six furlongs. He is in the King's Stand but the Wokingham looks the more likely route and will be our next stop. We'll definitely be going confident and we'll put it to the big boys - Harry Teal, assistant trainer.

Chipstead just keeps on improving. I walked the track and I rang the agent Simon Dodds and asked 'can I win over five down the stands' side here?' He said yes so I told Freddy to stick to that rail - it's the best ground. I think he's on the up and he could be black type soon. There's a Listed race at Doncaster at the end of the month and we thought we could go there if he won today and came out of it well - Roger Teal, trainer.

I'm absolutely delighted. Chipstead's full-brother Oxted won this in 2019, so July Cup here we come! He doesn't tear up the gallops like his brother and just does enough at home, but there's always improvement in horses like that. We were a bit unsure about the ground as apart from his first run he's never run on anything worse than good, but he's bred for it and obviously loved it - Roger Teal, trainer.

Chipstead has hit form again and travelled so well. I wanted to go for a Racing League race at Windsor and he should get in now. He sort of hit form at this time last year and might have suffered with what mine had early on in the season. He loves top of the ground - Roger Teal, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: I'm probably favouring the Wokingham, but if the ground changed we could switch him to the King's Stand, which his brother Oxted won for us in 2021. The ground has gone against him when we've tried him in black-type races, so we haven't learned much. He's never been as classy at home as Oxted, but he does find a lot at the track and is a quality horse. 13-06-23

runners.comment: Ran poorly at York last time and doesn't look to have much in hand of the handicapper
runners.spotlight: Fourth to stablemate Two Tribes in the Stewards' Cup at Goodwood but then ran poorly in another big field at York and doesn't look to have much in hand of the handicapper.

quotes.race: BetUK. Home Of The Acca Restricted Maiden Stakes (For horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Run Boy Run made very hard work of it. I wasn't overly keen on his attitude to be honest but I think some headgear will iron that out - Jason Hart, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

prev_owners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd Xxvii And Partner

runners.comment: Smart as 2yo and has since won three times in Hong Kong; hard to assess back in Britain
