runners.spotlight: Smart 2yo for Kevin Ryan in 2021, winning two 6f Group races in France, and he has gained three 5f successes in Hong Kong since; withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Haydock (just 11-2) last week on return to Britain; impossible to discount but this is a hot race and others are safer.

runners.comment: Going the right way and made it 3-5 last time at Newmarket; 5lb rise could be manageable
runners.spotlight: Going the right way and made it three wins from five attempts when beating ten opponents in the greys' race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) four weeks ago; this is more competitive but his trainer has a knack with progressive sprinters and a 5lb rise does not look insurmountable.

quotes.quote: We had this race in mind after Addison Grey ran well at Chepstow last time despite missing the kick and it's a bit of fun for the owners Opulence Racing, who are big supporters of ours - Matt Stanley, trainer's assistant.

runners.comment: Gives trouble at the start and is often slowly away but arrives in form and not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Tends to give trouble at the start and is often slowly away; put everything together when winning at Goodwood (5f, good) in May and made good late headway into third there three weeks ago; ran well on his only start at Doncaster when third to Big Evs in the Flying Childers as 2yo; needs to get away on terms but he's a lively candidate if doing so.

runners.comment: Last month's win (Newbury) was his third in a fine season; should give running again
runners.spotlight: Having a fine season and his last-gasp Newbury win (5f, good to firm) under Billy Loughnane four weeks ago was his third win of the campaign; no obvious reason why he won't give his running again but he's back up 4lb and in a warmer race; one of three runners for Robert Cowell.

quotes.quote: Rhythm N Hooves has only got a burst for a furlong to a furlong and a half. My first couple of times riding him I struggled to work that out, but I've managed to get the hang of it now so I can conserve him until the last furlong. He's a good horse, he won at Ascot a few years ago and he's a star to ride - Billy Loughnane, jockey.

Rhythm N Hooves was inexperienced coming into this, it was only his fifth race. He learnt a lot at York the last day, that was a big shock for him going from all-weather novices through to these 20, 25-runner handicaps. He has obviously shown a high level of ability here, so hopefully we can either go to more of these high-grade handicaps, but I wouldn't put it past going for a Listed race maybe with him at some point - Archie Watson, trainer.

runners.comment: Back to form on last two outings, third at the Shergar Cup on latest; not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Won at Ripon (5f, good) in April and back to form on last two outings, third at the Shergar Cup on latest; has won at Doncaster and he seems equally effective at 5f and 6f; not ruled out.

quotes.quote: Venture Capital showed a lot of speed last time at Thirsk and immediately after the race my old man felt that sprinting could be his gig and he's shown that there. It was only his first try over six furlongs so I'd like to think there's a lot more to come from him. There's some nice sprints in the summer and he's shown he handles the ground there. We'll see what the handicapper does but he could progress now at that trip. The time was good there and in these big sprint handicaps you need a horse that stays the trip well so that's a big positive. We'll see what comes up but I'm sure by the end of the year there will be bigger and better things on the horizon - Adam Ryan, trainer's son and assistant.

stable_tour.quote: He's very genuine and we've been very lucky with the sire's progeny. He's a homebred and the owner's first winner and I can see them having some good fun with him on turf. He'll get a mile no problem and we'll look for a nice handicap and work from there. 04-04-24

runners.comment: Better with every run this season, just denied at York last Sunday; big player in blinkers
runners.spotlight: Has got better with every run this season, finishing third at York's Ebor meeting (5.4f, good to firm) and only just denied over the same C&D last Sunday; may not have reached his ceiling just yet and first-time blinkers could give him a further lift; commands major respect.

quotes.quote: Air Force One is a horse that joined us last year. He has gotten stronger and had been showing a bit of pace. This was a nice place to start. I think he's a five furlong sprinter - Paddy Twomey, trainer.

runners.comment: Splendidly tough campaigner; arrives on a hat-trick and he's capable of another big run
runners.spotlight: Splendidly tough campaigner with nearly 100 runs under his belt; gained wins number 14 and 15 in 6f events at Ayr and Southwell in August; still well treated on best form, has an impressive record at Doncaster (two wins and four places from nine attempts) and capable of another big run.

quotes.quote: Aberama Gold seems to enjoy those conditions and we're lucky to have got him really. He ended up in Goodwood on this sort of ground, he won at York before that on better ground, but these are his sort of conditions by the looks of it. I don't know if we'll run him again this year. I'm not sure he's up to Listed class. He's a very good sprint handicapper and he's had a great year. I can't imagine he's going into next year well handicapped, but on these conditions, if you handle them it's probably worth a stone. We'll probably look for valuable sprint handicaps on bottomless ground - David O'Meara, trainer.

Owner Evan Sutherland said Aberama Gold would handle the ground better than most and he was right. It was very straightforward, he travelled well, the last half a furlong felt like a long way but the loose horse might have been a help. I have inherited a very good horse off Keith Dalgleish. It is his third start for me now, I can't take a great deal of credit for him. The Ayr Gold Cup will be a good target and between now and then I am not certain as there is a bit of time. He is not just a mudlark but he probably wouldn't want it rattling fast - David O'Meara, trainer.

That was very nice. We've not really done anything with Aberama Gold since he arrived. He ran at Ayr very quickly after coming from Keith's and even now I hardly know the horse yet. He's in the Stewards' Cup next Saturday and if he gets in, why not run? - David O'Meara, trainer.

quotes.race: Heed Your Hunch At Betway Handicap
quotes.quote: Aberama Gold keeps improving and who knows when we will get to the bottom of him. He doesn't do a whole lot when he gets there but he's a nice horse. I always thought a bit of him and he probably just went a bit weak between two and three. He's versatile with ground and track - Shane Gray, jockey.
We had been disappointed with Aberama Gold but giving him time and a strong pace at six furlongs has paid off - Keith Dalgleish, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had a great year in 2023, but not so much last year. He runs himself in and out of form and hopefully this is an 'in' year. If it is, he's well handicapped. He likes dig in the ground and won the Stewards' Cup two years ago on almost unraceable ground. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Ready winner at Newmarket on stable debut but backward steps since; enough to prove
runners.spotlight: Ready winner at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) on stable debut in July but backward steps in his three starts since; enough to prove.

quotes.quote: Emperor Spirit has a very good record fresh. He came to us with a few issues and was freegoing at home. We put in a lot of work at home and bought him a special bridle to let him relax in his work, and I was pleased two furlongs in today that he was relaxing. He'll probably operate at around the 90 mark and will be a horse for those Saturday handicaps - Michael Keady, trainer.

runners.comment: Shaped better than result suggests when sixth in Great St Wilfrid; a very lively outsider
runners.spotlight: Best recent efforts on AW but his turf mark reflects that and he shaped better than the result suggests when sixth in the Great St Wilfrid at Ripon last time, third in his group after meeting trouble; all wins at 6f but he should be just as effective over this extended 5f; a very lively outsider.

quotes.quote: We've not had Fivethousandtoone too long, we've been trying to figure him out. Arguably his best form is at Newcastle, held up, and it all worked perfectly for him today. There was a bit of a headwind and he got plenty of cover - David Allan, jockey.
Today it all came together and hopefully it will be the beginning of a decent season for Fivethousandtoone. He was good last time and better today - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

quotes.race: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap
quotes.quote: Fivethousandtoone has run well without winning. The race worked out well for him today, waiting with him and coming late. That's his level - William Buick, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He was a very smart juvenile, winning over 6f before finishing second in the Mill Reef Stakes and mid-division over 7f in the Dewhurst Stakes. He had a setback earlier this year but that has been resolved and he is now back in fast work. The plan is to go straight to Royal Ascot for the Commonwealth Cup. He is a very smart horse who looks very together this year and is one I am excited about getting back on the track. 19-05-21

runners.comment: Last two runs were underwhelming, including in a tongue-tie last Saturday; plenty to prove
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo for Karl Burke; made a fine start for new connections when second at Chepstow (6f, good) in August but his two runs since have been underwhelming, including in a tongue-tie last Saturday; plenty to prove.

runners.comment: On a hat-trick after 6f wins at Thirsk under Lauren Young; could absorb a 4lb rise
runners.spotlight: From a family that has served her stable extremely well over the years and she's on a roll with two 6f wins at Thirsk in the last fortnight; again well handled by Lauren Young with a front-running success last Saturday; it's hard to know where her limit lies and she could absorb a 4lb rise.

quotes.quote: Eternal Sunshine has been running really well, she was full of confidence and she was carrying no weight at all. She picked up and quickened away nicely - Lauren Young, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She's had a really good season, winning five times. It was frustrating to get beat last time when she got hampered in the Ayr Bronze Cup. She's a homebred and we purposely let her mature. I still think there's improvement in her but we've decided that was enough for the season. Hopefully she'll come back off a nice mark next year and we'll look to follow in the footsteps of American Affair. 02-10-24

racecards.verdict: The in-form pair Eternal Sunshine and Air Force One are respected but the eye is drawn to FIVETHOUSANDTOONE at much longer odds.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Air Force One, 7/1 Addison Grey,  Trefor, 9/1 Jakajaro, 10/1 Jordan Electrics, 11/1 Eternal Sunshine, 12/1 Venture Capital,  Rhythm N Hooves,  Rosario,  Apollo One, 14/1 Diablo Rojo, 16/1 Aberama Gold,  Fivethousandtoone, 18/1 Run Boy Run, 22/1 Roman Dragon, 25/1 Tropical Storm, 28/1 Atomic Force, 40/1 Chipstead,  Seven Questions, 50/1 We Never Stop,  Emperor Spirit,  King Of Light

racecards.race_name: Betfred Park Stakes (Group 2)

runners.comment: Won the Lockinge and the Lennox last term but not in the same form since
runners.spotlight: Smart over 7f/1m on his day; runner-up in this contest in 2023 and won the Lockinge and the Lennox last year; not in the same form since and the switch to blinkers (retained) had no effect last time; the need for a revival continues.

quotes.quote: That was a step back in the right direction, and Audience probably wants quicker ground than today. I was trying to give myself a bit of space in the straight and Quinault kept coming with me, but he finished his race off and in the last furlong he was coming back again. I think the cheekpieces helped, as that was better than he had been doing this year - Rab Havlin, jockey.

quotes.race: Sky Bet City Of York Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: Audience got upset in the stalls and he struck the front of the stalls a couple of times when the other one reared up. Rab said he wasn't himself after that, he wouldn't let down at all - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

Audience has always had the talent but Rab knows him well. He was kept in training as a lead horse to Inspiral, as he was in the Lockinge. He's improving with age so there's hope for us all yet. He's matured a lot mentally. It wasn't a fluke in the Lockinge. The mile was too stiff at Ascot. Rab really knows him. He rides him most mornings - and then he takes him home. We'll go to the City of York without a penalty. We've put him in the Foret - last year's ground would have been perfect. He wouldn't want traditionally Parisian ground. He could well be a Breeders' Cup Mile horse as well - Thady Gosden, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Al Shaqab Lockinge Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: Last year Audience wasn't giving himself a chance, we were running him over sprint trips to start. He was a little bit of a thug. He'd run some great races over seven furlongs last year, really staying on towards the end, and even towards the backend this was always going to be the starting point. We didn't think he'd be good enough to beat Inspiral but he's always threatened to win a big one. It's his first run over a mile and last year he never would have got a mile, he was too like a bull in a china shop. He's come back at the winner a couple of times last year, good horses like Kinross, he's had good horses around him. If he can improve over a mile - which he clearly has - there's some nice races to win with him - Rab Havlin, jockey, speaking on ITV racing.

quotes.race: Cavani Menswear Fashion Face-Off Frenzy Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: It is all down to Leah (Mapston) who looks after Audience and rides him every day. He was not right in the spring but he has come right now. I think the hood helps him a lot. Rab (Havlin) went up that ground this morning and it is completely fresh, virgin ground and that was a little bit of knowing your track. He is in races like the Lennox but he enjoyed the straight track here and seven furlongs is very much his trip - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Made all in the 2024 German 2,000 Guineas; has to overcome 14-month absence
runners.spotlight: Suited by ground softer than good; made all in the 2024 German 2,000 Guineas but struggled in French Group 1 next time; best to assume he'll need the outing on return from 14-month absence, unless the market suggests otherwise.

quotes.race: Coolmore St Mark's Basilica German 2000 Guineas (Group 2) (3yo Colts & Fillies) (Turf)
quotes.quote: It was a hard race and the draw wasn't very favourable to us in ten, so we thought we'd let Devil's Point stride on, catch a breather and go on. We felt we had the best horse in the race so that's how we rode him. This race was always the plan, but I think we could drop him back to seven furlongs in the Prix Jean Prat, and then maybe the Prix Maurice de Gheest [six and a half furlongs] - David Menuisier, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs John Dunlop EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was happy with Devil's Point's run in the Prix Djebel [at Deauville in April]. They just went very quickly and the horse got tired as he was only at 80 per cent, like all of mine first time out. We plan to go for the German 2,000 Guineas [May 20] and, without being offensive, today was a bit like a racecourse gallop in our back garden, so it fit perfectly. This way I don't need to work him again. At least he's seen something else and we've made a few quid and everybody's happy - David Menuisier, trainer.

I'm delighted. Devil's Point came to win the race then kind of flattened out then I'm not sure if he went again. I'm thrilled, it's a Group 1 and second is absolutely marvellous. He was travelling great on the ground. It's pretty special - David Menuisier, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won the German 2,000 Guineas for us last season but then picked up an injury when he ran in the Prix Jean Prat. He's coming back and seems to be doing really well - we took him for a gallop the other day and he went nicely. He's got an entry in the Hungerford, which is an option, but he's not going anywhere until we get some ease in the ground. I'm not prepared to run him on anything quicker than good to soft. The plan is to target the Prix de la Foret and whichever road we take there is fine. 30-07-25

runners.comment: Has largely progressive RPRs but was disappointing last time; headgear is applied
runners.spotlight: Yet to encounter slow ground; lightly raced; has largely progressive RPRs, including at Group level, but was disappointing in the Hungerford most recently; chance of a rebound partly depends on how well he responds to first-time headgear.

stable_tour.quote: He had a little hold-up this spring so I don't think he'll make the 2,000 Guineas. He showed up well on both starts, including when third in the Gimcrack after being drawn on the wing. He had an issue after that race, which is why he didn't run again. He's a big, strong lad and as he gets older things will become easier for him. I'm optimistic that given his size he should progress through the year. 15-04-24

runners.comment: Can be opposed on balance of 2024 form, notwithstanding York Group 3 win in July
runners.spotlight: Front-running 5yo who is versatile ground-wise; took his York record to 3-3 with 7f Group 3 win in July; below that level otherwise this year, most recently in Group 1 back there; opposed on balance of 2025 form.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Criterion Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Quinault is a fast horse and on his dam's side, he's definitely bred to go a bit further. Tom [Morley, joint-owner] continually have this argument whether he's best over a stiff six furlongs or an easy seven, but I think it's probably either. He's just a high-class horse who loves to win. Once he gets in front and rolling along at a place like this, he's hard to catch. We'd like to come back here to the Ebor meeting in August as it was a win and you're in race for it. We'll aim for that and be working back from there. He tries very hard and that's the key to it. It's his first Group win and it means a lot. I took him to Dubai in the winter and ran him in a Group 1, as we've always thought he was capable of this sort of thing - Stuart Williams, trainer.

quotes.race: Starman At Tally Ho Stud Garrowby Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: We thought Quinault wouldn't like this ground but he just wants to win - he definitely tries. He's a special horse for a yard the size of ours, especially as he came a long way in such a short space of time last year. He had a little wobble earlier this year and was a little bit ill after we gave him the flu jab, but he's bang in form now. He's such a special horse. There's a chance he improves again next year and I still think he might be better over seven furlongs, so we've got to explore that avenue as well. He'll have a little bit of a break now as he's done his bit for the summer, but we could travel with him. He's a gelding so we'll go where the best prize-money is that he can win. There isn't much point in running him in Group races just for the sake of it - Stuart Williams, trainer.
I don't think Quinault was in love with the ground but he just tries very hard - Stuart Williams, trainer.

quotes.race: Liverpool Airport Faster, Easier, Friendlier Queensferry Stakes (Listed)
quotes.quote: I'd love Quinault to go seven furlongs round a bend somewhere if I could find the right race for him - I think that would be ideal. We wouldn't be against travelling him later in the year. We might go abroad somewhere, some of the tighter tracks, maybe in America, somewhere like that might suit him - Stuart Williams, trainer.

What a special horse Quinault has been for everybody this year. He didn't run a bad race the last day but he's back on song today - it's a special day. He's got one way of running in that you let him stride on in front, and he's so genuine he keeps finding for you - Luke Catton, jockey.

We had Sendintank who won ten handicaps in a season, including four in a week twice, but that was at a much lower level than this - this is very different. Quinault is a very tough horse and he's got loads of talent as well. Luke Catton has done a fantastic job settling him and it's such a shame he cannot do the weight. The horse was just a runaway when he arrived, he would just bolt. We trained him on a treadmill for the first two months so he never left the yard, before we slowly started to introduce him to the Heath, so there's a lot of work gone into what you're seeing today. He's always had the talent, it's just been a case of letting the horse know it and letting him use it - he's always been fast, that wasn't the problem! He's bred to get further so as long as we can keep him settled in his races that's a route we'll look to go with him - Stuart Williams, trainer.

quotes.race: Oakmere Homes Supporting Macmillan Sprint Handicap (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: This was the biggest occasion Quinault has been to and he got a little bit warm in the preliminaries but he settled nicely in the race and Connor gave him a lovely ride, just managing to get him there on the line. We put him in at Ascot just in case something happened that forced our hand, but my gut feeling is probably not to run. I think he'll get a bit further and I've put him in the Bunbury Cup so that's a possibility - Stuart Williams, trainer.

It was unfortunate Luke Catton couldn't ride Quinault today as he couldn't do the weight. Luke has done a brilliant job settling him at home as he was a tearaway when he came but that is why Godolphin put him in the sale in the first place. He will probably go to York now for the valuable three-year-old six-furlong handicap as long as he comes out of this fine - Stuart Williams, trainer.

runners.owner: Roddy O Byrne And Robert O Byrne
runners.comment: 2-2 at Doncaster, namely soft-ground wins in 2023 and 2024; interesting back here
runners.spotlight: Best effort this term when second to Quinault at York (7f, good) on penultimate start; 2-2 at Doncaster, namely soft-ground wins in the sales race at this meeting in 2023 and Listed event last November; interesting back here, assuming he takes well to first-time headgear.

quotes.quote: There's a Listed race at Newcastle next week [Golden Rose Stakes], but I thought they could go very steady there and then sprint, which wouldn't have suited Room Service, so we took a punt here. He's lightly raced and had a bit of a wind issue that we corrected, but he's still quite a raw horse. Whether he wanted it this soft was another thing too, but he's a very good horse. He's crying out for another furlong too and he proved that at York last time and here. You'd imagine he'll do plenty strength-wise as we know he's got the engine - Kevin Ryan, trainer.

quotes.race: Northern Commercials Iveco E-Daily, Zero Emissions Handicap
quotes.quote: Room Service ran in the Greenham and didn't quite get 7f and he ran a cracker in Listed company there last time. This fast pace and stiff finish suited him down to the ground - Adam Ryan, assistant trainer.

After York I thought this extra half furlong would definitely suit Room Service. And we were very hopeful he'd go on the ground. He's been maturing all year. We've been taking it steady with him and it's paying off now. Whether we run him again this year I don't know but I'd say he'll get a mile well next year - Kevin Ryan, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: All he did was progress last season and he ended up winning the big sales race at Doncaster. He surprised me how well he went through the soft ground that day because he's got the action of a fast-ground horse. He even ran green when he hit the front and you'd be very hopeful he can progress again this season. He missed the Burradon Stakes at Newcastle on Good Friday and is heading for either the Craven Stakes, a 7f conditions race at Newmarket or the Greenham at Newbury. He's got an entry in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and his comeback will determine which route we take. 04-04-24

runners.comment: Gelded prior to Group 3 win at Haydock in sole run this term; may progress further
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to narrow success in the John of Gaunt at Haydock (good; Group 3) in sole run this season, producing a career-best performance and taking record over 7f to 3-7; acts on soft; this 4yo goes well fresh and may be capable of further progress; respected.

quotes.quote: Ten Bob Tony has always gone well fresh. He's very tough and gelding him has probably made him a bit more of a man. He's grown up a lot over the winter and he's getting towards being the finished article - Matty Hicks, assistant trrainer.

stable_tour.quote: He made a brilliant reappearance when winning at Newmarket last year but was probably a little disappointing later on. He's been gelded and I hope he can win a stakes race and then be competitive at a higher level on soft ground. He's a 7f specialist, which is quite tricky, but he's going to start off in the Spring Trophy at Haydock in May. 16-04-25

runners.comment: Returns to the scene of his last success but this is a stiffer assignment
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Listed grade at Salisbury on reappearance; has failed to repeat that form in two major handicaps at Ascot since; returns to the scene of his last success (6f, soft) but this is a stiffer assignment.

quotes.race: Betfred "Double Delight" British EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (Div I)
quotes.quote: Zoum Zoum handled conditions very well and was probably in the best place on the rail. He's very professional and is a horse that we've always liked, so it was good to see him back it up today - Joe Tuite, trainer's assistant.

stable_tour.quote: We always thought he was a giraffe rather than a racehorse and then suddenly late last summer he started showing up at home. He's going to go to the Greenham Stakes. Being a gelding, our options are limited but the Greenham and the Jersey Stakes are on his radar at this point. 15-04-24

runners.breeder: Manor Farm Stud & J E Rose
runners.comment: Interesting on penultimate effort when second behind the subsequent Group 1 Marois winner
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger; has something to find on most of his form but he is interesting judged on penultimate effort, when close second in Group 2 at the Curragh (7f, good to yielding) behind Diego Velazquez who followed up in the Group 1 Marois; that's his clear peak on RPRs.

quotes.race: Irish Racing Writers Association Ron McKnight Memorial Madrid Handicap
quotes.quote: The only time East Hampton encountered ground that resembled soft, he won on it. We brought him to Dundalk the last day but he didn't like the surface so we put him away for this. Obviously six furlongs was as far as he'd gone so the trip was a worry but I was delighted to see the low draw. Luke gave him a fabulous ride, dropped him in and switched him off as much as he could. He will learn from it, he was fresh today. He saw it out well, though and didn't stop galloping. As long as the ground has an ease, we'll keep going - David Marnane, trainer.

runners.comment: 0-7 on turf but was a good third in the Hungerford last time; enters calculations
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-1 on AW compared to 0-7 on turf; however, he has two pieces of very useful form in this sphere, including third in the Hungerford at Newbury (7f, good to firm; Group 2) last time; enters calculations, having shown there's possibly a race of this nature in him.

runners.comment: Champion 2yo last term; drops back from Group 1 level with leading claims on best form
runners.spotlight: Champion 2yo last season, having won the Middle Park and Dewhurst (both on soft ground), and he ran well in the 2,000 Guineas on reappearance; rather a disappointing favourite in the 6f Commonwealth Cup, then couldn't quite get the job done in the 7f Prix Jean Prat having held every chance; drops back from Group 1 level with leading claims on best form.

quotes.quote: Shadow Of Light is going to be a fun horse for the summer and ultimately he'll probably go to the St James's Palace - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

I needed cover, as Shadow Of Light is a six-furlong winner stepping up to seven in soft ground, so I had to conserve where I could. I knew if I showed him daylight too early he'd burn out. I had to follow Oisin where he went, and Ryan and James went on the stands' rail while we were at the far side of the middle. Oisin was cooked approaching the three pole and I had to make my own way from there. Luckily I got in tight with Ryan and James and I just managed to get over to them. Once I did, my fella got really competitive. He hit the rising ground and he was well on top in the end. He's really tough, he responded when I asked him, and what really pleased me the most is for such a quick Middle Park winner he showed stamina on soft ground here. You're nearly winning two races, so it's a very hard thing to do. I can't remember many two-year-olds I've ridden that would be able to do that. He was running on his own doing fine, but once he got a sniff of the competition he found another gear. It was a big performance, and through that you could definitely mark it up - William Buick, jockey.

It's great for the team and nice to have a horse like Shadow Of Light. We'll put him away for the winter and see what everyone else is doing as to whether we look towards the Guineas or stick to the Commonwealth Cup programme. I'd say he's a Commonwealth Cup horse as there's a lot of speed on his page. I think he'll get stronger and they get quicker as they get stronger - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Gimcrack Stakes (Group 2) (IRE Incentive Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: We're happy. It was a big step up and Shadow Of Light has probably learnt a lot and ran his race. He got beat by a better horse on the day. We'll stick at six and he looked quite professional. I said to Charlie the winner looked good for Aomori City, so we'll take that - Alex Merriam, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Promising debut win at Lingfield six weeks ago but he has loads to find on that form
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt who made a promising start to his career with ready win at Lingfield (7f, good) six weeks ago; open to progress but that form gives him loads to find upped markedly in class.

racecards.verdict: Back at Doncaster, ROOM SERVICE is an interesting alternative to top-rated Shadow Of Light. Ten Bob Tony has good claims.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Shadow Of Light, 9/2 Ten Bob Tony, 6/1 Marvelman, 7/1 Room Service, 11/1 King's Gamble, 12/1 Quinault, 16/1 Audience,  East Hampton, 25/1 Devil's Point,  Zoum Zoum, 50/1 Zabeel Alkabeir

racecards.race_name: Betfred St Leger Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

runners.comment: Won 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase and staying-on second in the 1m4f Great Voltigeur at York
runners.spotlight: Blinkered all starts, none on softer than good; won his first three races, culminating in 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot by three quarters of a length from Furthur; off two months before the Great Voltigeur at York (down to 1m4f) in which he lost his unbeaten record but showed further improved form, outpaced in behind horses before rallying strongly when switched, prevailing in a photo for second which also involved Stay True; that suggests better still is likely on today's step back up in trip; player.

quotes.quote: I knew Carmers would stay and I knew turning in Billy [Lee] looked comfortable and I was happy to see the horse come outside him and just not have him at the front and that he was able to get a breather into him round the bottom. He looked comfortable when the bell rang and I was thinking we had a chance. I think staying is his game and he's a pretty good horse. He's done everything we've asked of him and he will have an entry in the St Leger at Doncaster and we'll see, he's done what we've asked him to do today; he's run three times and won three times and hopefully he'll continue doing it - Paddy Twomey, trainer.Race went to plan all the way, jumped well, had a great position, when Harry came around I just let Carmers go on and was able to just hack away but got him rolling early from five out just to get him going forward; he takes a while to build up a head of steam and I know he'd stay well. I wanted to stretch them so I was always going to try and make it a stamina test and you can see him there pricking his ears going to the line, just doing enough. Staying was always going to be a forte of his. He's so laid back at home, he wouldn't pass out the dog at home, he's just that type of character, he saves plenty. I knew when I got stuck into him he'd keep finding away - Billy Lee, jockey.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Yeats Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: He's a horse who des just what he has to do, hence the headgear. I thought Carmers would be in the first three at Ballinrobe but he surprised me how well he won at the line. He came home, licked the pot, rode out this week and didn't care so we decided to come here and see what happens. We knew he'd stay but we didn't want to make it, he's a horse who probably doesn't want to be in front too long. To be fair he kept on well, he's keeping plenty to himself. The Queen's Vase is probably the race for him - Paddy Twomey, trainer, speaking to Racing TV

stable_tour.quote: He's unbeaten and has done nothing wrong. I liked what I saw from him at Navan last time and the Queen's Vase has always been the plan for him. I think the race will really suit him. 13-06-25

runners.comment: Second in the 1m6f Queen's Vase and impressive in the 13.2f Geoffrey Freer at Newbury
runners.spotlight: All this season's races have been on good or firmer; second in the 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot, on the heels of Carmers; flopped behind Scandinavia at Newmarket three weeks later but resumed his improvement to win the Group 3 Geoffrey Freer at Newbury (13.2f) four weeks ago, coming from almost last to first in a steadily run, six-runner race to score by 3l; better still is required but that win was delivered in good style.

quotes.race: Highclere Thoroughbred Racing Geoffrey Freer Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Furthur ran a stellar race at Ascot when he was narrowly beaten in the Queen's Vase and we had big hopes in the Bahrain Trophy, but it probably came too soon for him. He's had a nice break since and that was more like him. He's not an overly enthusiastic horse in the morning, but he relaxed beautifully and Oisin rode him lovely. I'm very pleased - Andrew Balding, trainer.

Furthur wasn't an expensive horse and he's given us a huge amount of enjoyment. Hopefully there's more to come as well. We might look at the Bahrain Trophy if he comes out of this alright. He stays well, and might be a Leger horse later in the year - Andrew Balding, trainer.

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
runners.comment: Form pick after two Derby wins which shouted stamina; may have needed his Voltigeur run
runners.spotlight: Set the pace when winning the Derby at Epsom by nearly 4l and helped to set it when he dug deep to follow up in Irish Derby at the Curragh (again 1m4f on good) later in June; 3l fifth when 4-5 in the Great Voltigeur at York last month looked disappointing, notwithstanding the sprint from early in the straight, but his trainer has stressed that it was a stepping stone to bigger prizes in the autumn; the two Derby wins suggest this trip won't be a problem and they are the best form in this field.

quotes.quote: Lambourn is a straightforward horse, we know his running style, he's behind the bit. He travelled sweetly today and we were in a lovely rhythm all the way down the back and I started to pick it up. Things were going well but Tom was always there breathing down my neck and I didn't feel like I went through the two pole as well as I'd like to. He just kind of stayed on so there's a question mark over whether he actually stayed the trip or not - Sean Levey, jockey.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: They went very slow. Lambourn is a high-tempo, mile-and-a-half horse. Everything was perfect with him; we wanted to get a racecourse run or gallop in him before the Leger or the Arc. The race was kind of a mess really. It was a hack around to halfway, then a sprint up the stretch - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Lambourn is just so laid-back, I can't tell you. Ryan gave him a lovely, uncomplicated, straightforward ride. He knew him to a tee from winning on him at Chester and everything Wayne [Lordan] told him. He's so relaxed, and the horse coming with him [Sir Dinadan] was only going to help him, and you can see he was pricking his ears all the way up the straight. I knew Ryan would be very happy about that as he would have been delighted to have company and would love to get into a battle. He has that personality, that attitude, it's like everything in life when sports people have that attitude and he's that straightforward, genuine and just does whatever you want for you, whenever he can. He's like his dad, if you put him in first gear, he stays in first gear; if you put him in third gear, he'll stay in third gear. He's not influenced by anything around him, but always wants to please and never overdo himself. He could be a King George or an Arc type of horse. The way he races, I'd imagine he'd race that way regardless of the trip. You'd imagine he'd get further but he has lots of class - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Betfred Derby (In Honour Of His Highness Aga Khan IV) (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: Australia [Lambourn's sire] is a great Derby winner, very genuine, and that's what Lambourn is - uncomplicated, genuine and committed. He always has been. His runs this year have been very good - he was second to Delacroix, and then was very impressive in Chester. Chester again - you have to be on your game and you have to stay, and that's the way he was. Ryan loved him in Chester and he obviously can't ride them all. He was always going to be an Irish Derby horse, a Leger horse, and Wayne said he definitely would stay further. He saves plenty for himself and he gives himself a chance to get any trip - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Lambourn looked a thorough stayer and while he was a little on and off the bridle, once the penny dropped he came home really strongly. He got the trip really well and he's gone through the line nicely. We'll just have to see how they all get on in the trials but we were thinking of sending him to Epsom as they learn so much when they go to Chester - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We were very happy with his run when second in the Ballysax behind Delacroix and he's come out of it well. We were delighted with it for his first run of the season and he looks like a horse who will get further. 07-04-25

runners.comment: Backward step last time is forgivable; bang there with three of these in Queen's Vase
runners.spotlight: Progressive first five races, finishing bang there with Carmers, Furthur and Scandinavia when third in the Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot and he came from significantly further back than the first two; that's his only attempt at 1m6f and he took his first backward step when fourth in the storm at Goodwood (1m4f Group 3) on latest outing; that can probably be forgiven but he needs to resume his progress.

quotes.quote: I'm proud and delighted with Rahiebb but gutted to be honest though. Watching it I thought most of the way round he was going to run well and finish strong. With a furlong to run I thought he'd get there but congratulations to the winner, he's a very good horse. I'm proud of ours and I think he's got a big future. He took a while to come to hand and even in his very good run at Royal Ascot in the Vase he did most things wrong that day. He was still learning on the job today. He's a lovely horse, I have to give a shout out to Tony Procter who rides him out every day at home, he's not always easy. He's a lovely horse and I'm looking forward to him next year. I'd have thought that would be it for the year, he got going in February and didn't race as a two-year-old, he's all about next year - Roger Varian, trainer.

Rahiebb has run a really good race, he's a very nice horse. A horse to look forward to, hopefully for the next year or two in that staying division. He's still a baby, he didn't behave great in the prelims, just showing a bit of immaturity. He was slow from the gates, the draw didn't take his chance away, but made it difficult. He hasn't quite got a clear passage, he didn't really get stopped, but he didn't quite get an open runway. I still thought he would win with two to run. Then he was sort of wandering off a straight line, I don't know if it was greenness or ground, hot day, used a bit of nervous energy before the race. I think when you throw everything into the mix, he's a nice horse who performed very well, a bit frustrating we didn't quite bring it home, but a nice one for the future - Roger Varian, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a slow learner but hasn't done much wrong. He won on his debut and has had to give weight and penalties away ever since. He was second behind a very progressive horse called Merchant last time and that was a good run giving 8lb away. He wants a stiffer test and stamina is his strong suit. He's improving all the time and I can see him running a podium finish. 09-06-25

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
runners.comment: Much better with cheekpieces on to win 1m5f Group 3 at Newmarket and the 2m Goodwood Cup
runners.spotlight: Plenty went wrong for him racing wide in the 1m6f Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot but he rallied strongly for a close fifth, just behind three of today's rivals; much better with cheekpieces since, winning a five-runner Group 3 at Newmarket (1m5f) by nearly 9l and outgunning his older stablemate Illinois in their duel for the Goodwood Cup (2m, good); that makes him look the strongest stayer in this field and gives him almost the best form.

quotes.quote: We're delighted. This was Illinois's race, all was planned out, but when Ryan [Moore] said at Newmarket that Scandinavia felt very classy, it was the right thing to do to let them come and run the two of them together and see. Scandinavia looks a very classy stayer, he travelled very well through the race and Wayne [Lordan] gave him a beautiful ride. He was always a very smart horse. The lads actually backed him for the Derby very early in his career, he was considered a very good horse. He won his maiden over a mile and a quarter and Wayne was delighted with him at Ascot next time - he got trapped six wide but he still didn't lie down the whole way and came back at the line to try and win. He's a great stayer with a great mind, he's uncomplicated, and like we saw today he handled the ease in the ground well. You couldn't be more delighted with him really. You would imagine he looks a ready-made Leger horse. Lambourn didn't go to the King George, he is looking at the Voltigeur, and after that an Arc or the British or Irish Leger after that. Scandinavia doesn't have to run again before the Leger if he's going there, so all the options are there - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.Scandinavia has improved all year. He was stepping up to two miles today against the older horses. It's a tough task but I thought he stepped forward quite well. He was hanging onto a little bit even when he got there so I think there's plenty of improvement to come. Today was going to find out how smart he is and he's obviously a very smart stayer - Wayne Lordan, jockey.

I think the St Leger in England, or Ireland, would be targets and then hopefully Scandinavia can develop into a cup horse for next year. We can plot our way to those nice staying races for the rest of the year. We'll see how the horses perform over the festivals at Goodwood and York. He seems to have improved again since Ascot. Hopefully now we can have some fun with him over these longer distances - Paul Smith, co-owner.Scandinavia has been improving with every race and ran well at Ascot. He got into a lovely rhythm today and stayed on really well. I never rode him in a race before although I have rode him at home, so I wouldn't know about the [first-time] cheekpieces. He was impressive today - Ryan Moore, jockey.

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
runners.comment: Threatened to win Great Voltigeur, a run his trainer says was needed; exciting prospect
runners.spotlight: Three of Aidan O'Brien's last four St Leger winners were by Galileo and this colt is from the great sire's final crop; unraced on softer than good; won a 1m2f Leopardstown maiden and short-headed in 11.6f Lingfield Derby Trial; off for more than three months before he upstaged stablemate Lambourn in the 1m4f Great Voltigeur at York, taking over briefly more than 1f out, and although he finished fourth he was pipped for second only on the line by Carmers; there's more for him to prove regarding stamina today compared to all of his rivals, but O'Brien made clear that the York race was needed; after just three races, he still looks an exciting prospect.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Stay True always looked like a lovely Leger horse; you'd be happy with that - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

runners.comment: Two battling 1m6f handicap wins; promising stayer but the St Leger is much more demanding
runners.spotlight: Five races and highly progressive, winning three; penultimate start saw him take a handicap at Sandown (1m6f, good) from Pendragon, whose next race saw him take second in yesterday's Doncaster Cup; latest start was the prestigious Melrose Handicap at York (1m6f, good to firm) in which his battling qualities were again strongly in evidence and he won on the nod; a promising stayer, for sure, and that's twice he's pulled it out of the fire but the St Leger is a much more demanding task.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Melrose Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: I'd say he's a stayer with a future. We'll see what there is for Tarriance in a month's time. Hopefully he's a horse to keep on the team for next year. He might even get a little further, the way he hit the line there. I wouldn't think he'll run in the St Leger - Barry Mahon, Juddmonte racing manager.

quotes.race: bet365 Very British Raceday Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It was only Tarriance's second run and he's still a bit inexperienced. He was in the stalls a long time and didn't get the best of starts. But I knew he stays really well and he was the best horse in the race. He's got a great attitude. He's a big, raw horse and he'll probably get further in time - P J McDonald, jockey.

racecards.verdict: Lambourn and SCANDINAVIA stand out from the rest on their best form. Stay True is fascinating but Carmers is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Scandinavia, 11/4 Lambourn, 9/2 Carmers, 8/1 Stay True, 11/1 Furthur, 22/1 Tarriance, 25/1 Rahiebb

racecards.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (Winner Receives Free Entry To 2026 St Leger) (GBB/GBBPlus)

runners.comment: Promising start when close second of five at Newmarket; plenty of improvement possible
runners.spotlight: 850,000euros yearling, by Camelot out of a German Oaks winner; promising start when a close second of five at Newmarket (1m, good to firm; 11-8 favourite) four weeks ago, doing his best work late on after a slow start; the hood is retained today and plenty of improvement is possible for this 1m Group 2 and Group 1 entry; big chance.

runners.comment: Fared best of the newcomers when third of seven at Lingfield; has to be respected
runners.spotlight: Fared best of the newcomers when third of seven at Lingfield (1m, AW; 9-1) last month, never far away; his stable's 2yos often make a big step forward from debut to second run and he has to be respected.

runners.comment: Bred to be smart but too green to do himself justice on debut; should do much better
runners.spotlight: Bred to be smart (by Dubawi out of a French 12.5f Group 2 winner) but was too green to do himself justice when ninth of 16 at Newbury (7f, good to firm; 11-2) in July; almost certainly capable of much better.

runners.breeder: Ecurie Haras Du Cadran, Ecurie Melanie &
runners.comment: Showed something to build on when fourth of ten at Sandown; not discounted
runners.spotlight: Showed something to build on when about 3l fourth of ten at Sandown (7f, soft; 9-1) two weeks ago, rallying; shapes as if this longer trip will suit; not discounted.

runners.breeder: Macquaire Blds / Lynch Bages / Tim Hyde
runners.comment: Kept on well towards finish when third at Newbury; commands major respect for top yard
runners.spotlight: Kept on well towards the finish when third of ten at Newbury (7f, good to firm; 16-1) last month, giving the impression this step up in distance would be right up his street; top stable has won two of the last three runnings of this race; commands major respect.

runners.comment: Has an attractive pedigree; yard is operating at impressive 31% with its 2yos this season
runners.spotlight: By Iffraaj out of 7f 2yo winning sister to smart 1m-1m2f winner Electrical Storm and half-sister to multiple 1m Group 1 winner Ribchester; that's an attractive pedigree and his yard, which won this race in 2020, is operating at an impressive 31% with its 2yos this season; interesting newcomer.

runners.comment: 150,000gns yearling by Zarak; worth a market check on debut
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros foal, 150,000gns yearling; by Zarak; dam unraced sister to winners Goken (5f/5.5f Group 3) and Gokena (7f); worth market check on debut.

runners.comment: Expensive yearling and has a classy middle-distance pedigree; another interesting newcomer
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling; Wootton Bassett half-brother to 9.4f winner Iseult Of Ireland (RPR 91); dam 1m4f winner (73), sister to Breeders' Cup Turf and Arc winner Found; another newcomer with claims on paper.

runners.breeder: Lady Bamford & The Hon Alice Bamford
runners.comment: 150,000gns yearling; half-brother to a 5f Listed winner; not ruled out on debut
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling, by St Mark's Basilica; half-brother to 5f-7.6f winner Mutaraffa (including Listed; RPR 111); dam placed in France, sister to 7f 2yo winner Grizzle, out of 6f 2yo Listed winner; not ruled out on debut.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (Winner Receives Free Entry To 2026 St Leger) (GBB/GBBPlus)
quotes.quote: Ed Said he liked Pathein said he liked him and to ride him to run well. He got away with the ground. He's still a bit on the weak side but he has a good attitude - Robert Havlin, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's one I like for later in the year. He's a good-bodied colt who has a nice pedigree on the dam's side and cost a few quid at the sales. He's from the first crop of St Mark's Basilica, who won four consecutive Group 1s in 2021 for Aidan O'Brien. 30-04-25

runners.comment: Half-brother to a French Group-placed 1m2f 2yo winner; market support would be significant
runners.spotlight: Galiway half-brother to French Group-placed 1m2f 2yo winner Zarakchic (RPR 96); dam unraced sister to 7.5f-1m1f winner Parigi, out of 12.5f Listed winner; in good hands and market support would be significant on debut.

runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; already gelded and would be a surprise first-time-out winner
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; brother to 7f/1m winner Rocchigiani (including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 111), half-brother to five winners including Ross (6.5f-7.5f including French Listed; 111); dam German 1m Listed winner (101); already gelded and would be a surprise first-time-out winner.

racecards.verdict: Four weeks ago DEL MARO and Nova Centauri made encouraging debuts. Preference for Charlie Appleby's colt today is slight.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Del Maro, 15/8 Nova Centauri, 8/1 Mr Wonka,  Lyneham, 10/1 Merci Monsieur, 12/1 Colourband, 16/1 Morbeh, 20/1 El Loco, 28/1 Seeway, 33/1 Pathein, 66/1 Test Run

racecards.race_name: Mastiff Manor Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Smart at his best but not at best this year; bounce-back needed after three-month break
runners.spotlight: Smart at his peak but has not run to his best this year, only eighth when favourite for a handicap at Epsom on Oaks day; first-time cheekpieces worn on that occasion are left off here; returns from three-month break with his yard in form.

quotes.quote: Botanical was in super nick today. The worry was the drying ground as he was so dominant on quite soft ground at Hamilton, but he showed he copes with it. He was pretty dominant there and while he'll get a bit of a clobbering for that, he's a smart horse - James Doyle, jockey.

quotes.race: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
quotes.quote: Botanical won it really well and he should be a nice horse next year. The handicapper will have his say and will know whether it's a strong race but hopefully he's not too hard on us. He seems like a Class 2 horse and we'll see if he can climb the ladder a bit next year. I think ground is quite important to him, he was beaten on a sounder surface at Ascot the time before. I'm not sure if we'd run again this season - Roger Varian, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He would have liked the ground much slower at Doncaster but I think he ran well back at a mile. We'll be careful where we go with him but he could be flying the flag in plenty of stakes races and we could get the passport out for some slower ground in France or further afield. He might run in the Earl of Sefton in a few weeks if the rain comes. 07-04-25

runners.comment: Listed win on heavy as 2yo but hasn't reached the same heights in similar races this year
runners.spotlight: Smart effort when 4l winner of a 1m1f French Listed race in the mud on his final 2yo start; left Chester Vase reappearance behind when close fifth in 1m3f Hamilton Listed event (good) in July but ran poorly in another Listed race back in France since; no shock were he to revive, particularly if the ground gets testing, but others arrive with less to prove.

quotes.race: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Square D'Alboni is quite a long horse and the back of the stalls just caught his hock at Doncaster last time. We had to leave him alone for a little bit before we could go again. He was in the right frame of mind which was great - Ralph Beckett, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had a very good autumn and the form has worked out well. He's a gelding so we'll have to cut our cloth accordingly. He'll get ten furlongs well and probably a mile and a half. He's done well physically and I'm happy with him. 28-04-25

runners.comment: Largely progressive; better than result (met trouble) on AW latest; respected
runners.spotlight: Bar a blip on fast ground at York in June his profile is essentially progressive, including clear second to a next-time-out winner at Newbury (1m2f, good) in May; shaped much better than result when never-nearer sixth of ten at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last time, left with too much to do after meeting trouble early in the home straight; could still be on a good mark.

runners.breeder: New England Stud & Mount Coote Stud
runners.comment: Seems best on soft/heavy and not discounted should such conditions prevail
runners.spotlight: Soft or heavy ground dominate among her best performances, which she's had this term only when a creditable third at Chester (1m2f) in June; well held at Glorious Goodwood since; each-way claims should more rain arrive.

quotes.quote: That was a good performance, Our Golden One loves the ground. The way she relaxed and quickened away gives her possible options over further - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

runners.comment: Has shown he retains his ability but slow starts are in danger of becoming an issue
runners.spotlight: Usually held up; last three wins over 1m2f at Newbury (the latest a valuable event on heavy off 3lb lower last September) but also successful over C&D (soft) at the start of his career; has shown more than enough to think he retains his ability in three outings this year but could do with cutting out the slow starts.

stable_tour.quote: He was with the Crisfords last season and put up a couple of moderate unplaced efforts. We started him off over 7f and he didn't show much, but when we tried him over 1m½f at Wolverhampton he put up a much better show to be a half-length third. It was when we stepped him up to 1m2f last time at Yarmouth that he showed his true colours when comfortably winning by three and a half lengths. It certainly didn't look as if that would be the case at the start as he fluffed it completely, which was a surprise as he had never shown that trait before. I thought the ground might be too quick for him as he has a round action but he was fine and, having given them a fair head start, he ran on very strongly and looked impressive. I think that trip is ideal for him now and despite an 8lb hike in the handicap I think he'll remain very competitive. 30-06-21

runners.comment: Good thirds on AW/turf this summer and a slow start didn't help latest; Oisin Murphy up
runners.spotlight: In top form when racking up a hat-trick last summer and back at that level when third over 1m2f at Newcastle (AW) and Chepstow (good to soft) this summer; never a threat after a slow start at Southwell last time but more than capable off this mark and Oisin Murphy takes the reins.

quotes.race: tote Guarantee Available At tote.co.uk Racing League 20 Handicap (Trainers Reg' To Racing League)
quotes.quote: Fantasy Believer just wanted to give everyone an even further head start today! He has so much ability but you have to just leave him alone and let him do his own thing. The making of him has probably been stepping up to a mile and a quarter and Ed's [Walker, trainer] team have done an amazing job of getting him back to the form that he's in after such a lengthy break - Saffie Osborne, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He won well at Kempton in April off a mark of 86 and has gone up to 92, but unfortunately that might not be enough to get him into the Royal Hunt Cup. 10-06-21

He's just coming back from a break so he's behind, but he'll be targeted at midsummer handicaps and the Racing League, and we'll see if we can earn some more prize-money with him. He had an amazing season last year and it will be harder off his current mark but he's such a tryer and so game. 16-04-25

runners.owner: Mrs M Lingwood And S Bland
runners.comment: Prominent racer; two more Ripon wins this year but held off this mark last twice
runners.spotlight: Gained fourth Ripon success when making all over 1m2f (soft) in July; 4l second at Chester (1m2f, good) next time and perhaps a drop back to 1m didn't suit when only sixth of seven back at Ripon 19 days ago.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
quotes.quote: Ey Up Its The Boss is a little superstar. The ground came in his favour, but he's never had any bother and is so straightforward. Our horses are beginning to run out of their skin too and lots of ours want soft ground, so we knew when it started raining hard it'd be a massive plus. We didn't fancy him at all as we thought the handicapper had him. We'll make a plan after seeing what his mark is on Tuesday, but he might struggle to come back here as he might be too high in the handicap - Tony Coyle, joint-trainer.

It all went to plan. We weren't drawn too well but I was committed and it was plain sailing. I know it might look like Ey Up Its The Boss was going flat out but he was always getting a breath. I thought I'd sit and squeeze until the two [furlong pole] and he was game - Barry McHugh, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Unexposed handicap newcomer who should stay 1m2f; has to enter calculations
runners.spotlight: Looked a useful prospect when successful on his belated debut at Thirsk (1m, good) in July; a Glorious Goodwood conditions event proved beyond him next time but clear second behind a very useful sort in a novice at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) since; unexposed now venturing into his first handicap over a trip which should be within range.

runners.comment: Progress seems to have levelled off but the drop to 1m2f may help; acts on soft
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m3f and 1m4f in the spring on first two starts for James Owen; his progress has levelled off since but the way he swept into contention when third at Hamilton (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago suggests this drop to 1m2f for the first time since joining this yard could suit this sometimes free-goer; acts on soft.

runners.comment: Has shown the odd quirk but back from short break with yard in top form; remains unexposed
runners.spotlight: Has looked a bit quirky but his close second at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) in June shows he's capable off this sort of mark; never a threat on quicker ground at the Newmarket July meeting but it was a hot 3yo only handicap; returns from two months off, minus the blinkers he's worn on last two starts and with his yard in excellent form; going softer than good would be an unknown but there's still a feeling he might have a bigger performance in him.

racecards.verdict: Regalian is feared with his yard in excellent form but preference is for SOVEREIGN SEA (nap).
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Botanical,  Sovereign Sea, 6/1 Regalian, 7/1 Divine Knight, 8/1 Fantasy Believer,  Sportingsilvermine, 10/1 Mustazeed, 12/1 Square d'Alboni,  Our Golden One, 14/1 Ey Up Its The Boss

racecards.race_name: Alan Rae Smith Errant Handicap

runners.breeder: Whitsbury Manor Stud And Mrs M E Slade
runners.comment: Won on AW on final run in Ireland but down the field all three runs for this yard
runners.spotlight: Won at Dundalk (1m, AW) in his final run in Ireland but he's beaten only one rival in three outings for this yard.

quotes.quote: There was no pace on early but Enough Already travelled the best throughout but I didn't want to get there too early - Ray Dawson, rider.

quotes.race: Read Katie Walsh On Betway Insider Handicap
quotes.quote: Enough Already was a nice animal and lost his way a little bit, but the last day at Kempton he showed a bit of spark to finish fourth. I said to Lee if this horse runs his race he shouldn't be far away and we followed Ryan all the way. It took him a little while to get going but once he got motoring he was away - Dougie Costello, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Paul Deavin, Gerald Chesneau Et Al

runners.comment: Two turf wins of late and acts on AW; has to enter the reckoning
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on turf here (1m5f) in June and bounced back from a lesser run at Doncaster when going in again at Brighton (1m4f) 13 days ago; creditable fourth on AW back in May.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late K Abdullah

runners.comment: Held in ten starts since turf win in March but this multiple AW winner is on a good mark
runners.spotlight: Prolific last year, whereas his only win this season was in March; below par twice on turf since a respectable third on AW at Southwell (1m6f) in July but is well handicapped again.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap
quotes.quote: Destinado is a very tough horse who's been very consistent since he joined James. This is his trip, he broke well and travelled nicely throughout the race - Aidan Keeley, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's a lovely little horse who helped to put us on the map. He went off the boil a bit but ran really well again when he was third at Southwell just before Christmas. Unfortunately, when he won at Lingfield a few starts back, he won by too far, so he shot up in the handicap and we're just having to fight that a little bit at the moment. 01-01-25

runners.comment: All six wins on AW and went close over C&D latest; should go well again
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been on AW; second outing back from a break when narrowly denied a first Lingfield win 14 days ago and can go well again off only 1lb higher.

quotes.race: £30 In Free Bets With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: The Bay Warrior loves the track and the trip, so this was tailor-made for him. I was a little worried as he'd gone up the weights for finishing second, but he overcame it. I think if the grade goes up over these conditions he'll manage it. I don't see why he wouldn't go on the grass, but for now we'll stick to what's working - Mark Usher, trainer.

runners.comment: Will have a big say if headgear sees him recapture form he showed when third on AW in July
runners.spotlight: His third to progressive pair Deep Water Bay and Belgravian over 13.5f at Chelmsford reads very well (the fourth also won a hurdle next time); two lesser runs have followed but major player if first-time cheekpieces help him to recapture the Chelmsford form.

runners.comment: Potential improver in handicaps after break; all the more interesting if backed
runners.spotlight: Smart middle-distance pedigree; didn't get close to that level when reaching the frame in three qualifying runs at up to 1m4f on AW in February/March but no surprise were she to prove a different proposition for her top yard now handicapping after 169 days off; all the more interesting if backed.

runners.comment: Consistent in turf handicaps and likely to be thereabouts again back on AW
runners.spotlight: Very consistent in turf handicaps at up to 1m4f, winning once and runner-up on four other occasions; offered enough to think he's still in form when 2l fourth of six at Newmarket (1m2f) three weeks ago; showed promise on AW at start of his career; ought to be on the premises again.

stable_tour.quote: He's named after where my father trained and we quite like him. He's a big, backward horse who's had only three runs and was third in a maiden. He'll now go handicapping and we'll see how we get on. 30-04-25

racecards.verdict: It might be worth giving TYPHOON DANCER one more chance in first-time headgear as he is well treated on his AW third in July.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Arundel,  Destinado, 4/1 Typhoon Dancer, 9/2 One Million Dreams, 11/2 Kintbury, 6/1 The Bay Warrior, 25/1 Enough Already

racecards.race_name: Diomed Racing At Stuart Williams Stables Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.breeder: New England Stud, A Nicoll & P Vela
runners.comment: Promise when fifth on 7f Kempton debut; likely to progress
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling who showed promise when fifth of ten on 7f Kempton debut 24 days ago; likely to improve.

runners.comment: Makes obvious appeal for a top yard operating at a 35% strike-rate with 2yos in 2025
runners.spotlight: Pinatubo half-brother to French 6f winner Dream Whisper (RPR 92); dam 5f 2yo scorer (99), closely related to 1m4f Group 1 winner Old Persian; makes obvious appeal for a top yard operating at a 35% strike-rate with 2yos in 2025.

runners.comment: Flashed tail and weakened quickly on debut in June; gelded after; check betting
runners.spotlight: In good hands but flashed tail and weakened quickly to finish a well-held fourth of five when 7-2 on his 6f Wolverhampton debut in June; subsequently gelded; the betting is perhaps the best guide to expectations.

runners.comment: Well held in two recent starts
runners.spotlight: More one for low-grade handicaps based on two recent starts.

runners.comment: Well-held fifth on 6.5f turf debut but travelled well until past halfway; should improve
runners.spotlight: Decent pedigree and travelled well to a point when well-held fifth of 13 on 6.5f Newbury debut in a maiden blown apart by the impressive Into The Sky; could improve, possibly considerably so.

runners.comment: Promise in 6f turf novices; off 11 weeks off (gelded); best form of those with experience
runners.spotlight: £140,000 yearling who showed promise when seventh of 11 on his York Dante meeting debut in May and, though unable to justify favouritism, confirmed he has a fair level of ability when third at Newmarket the following month (both 6f, good to firm); gelded ahead of this first start for 11 weeks; has the best form of those with experience.

runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in two 7f AW starts last month; big outsider again
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in two 7f AW starts last month; big outsider again.

runners.comment: From top yard but always behind in two sprint maidens on turf in August
runners.spotlight: From a top yard in very good form but always behind in two sprint maidens on turf in August.

runners.comment: Half-sister to a US Grade 1 winner but yard not known for first-time-out 2yo success
runners.spotlight: Sold for 32,000gns as a foal; makes some paper appeal, being a half-sister to 6f-1m winner King Of Gosford (including 2yo/AW/US Grade 1; RPR 111) and out of a US Listed winner; yard only 2-39 with 2yos in the last five years, though.

runners.comment: Bred to be sharp but yard's last 2yo winner was in 2019
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Epsom Faithfull (prolific 6f-1m2f including 2yo/AW; RPR 97), Lynns Boy (5f AW; 82), Whataguy (5f including 2yo; 82) and La Farfallina (French 5.7f AW/6f turf); dam 5f/6f winner (99); sharp enough pedigree but have to go back to 2019 for the yard's last 2yo winner.

runners.comment: Would be a surprise winner on debut
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns breeze-up buy; half-sister to winners Shifting Strategy (1m; RPR 81) and Secret Sauce (1m; 67); would be a surprise winner on debut.

runners.comment: Should have a future but others appeal more for win purposes
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns yearling; sister to multiple 6f winner Cavalry Charge (including 2yo/Hong Kong; RPR 101), closely related to 7f-1m1f winner Patentar (including 2yo; 111), half-sister to five winners including Khamry (7f; 92); dam 7f/1m winner (70); should have a future but others appeal more for win purposes.

racecards.verdict: Those with experience don't set the bar high so the vote goes to Charlie Appleby newcomer BECCADELLI.
racecards.betting_forecast: 8/11 Beccadelli, 11/4 Romeo Montague, 7/1 Augustus Gloop, 9/1 Comic Strip, 14/1 Manly Fireball, 25/1 Back To Black, 33/1 Canderelly, 50/1 Epsom Ali, 66/1 Magic Melissa, 100/1 East India Breeze,  Yea Boi,  Real Gold

racecards.race_name: Lady Kim Groves 60th Birthday Fillies' Handicap

prev_owners.owner: Ian Wight, Tony Lyons And Ptnr
Ian Wight, Mrs Teresa Lyons And Ptnr

runners.comment: Placed after three-and-a-half month absence at Newmarket (1m); less productive next time.
runners.spotlight: 3-21, those wins coming over 5f, 6f, and 7f; placed on return from a three-and-a-half month absence at Newmarket (1m, good to soft; 6-1) on penultimate start but less productive next time (7f); easing mark coupled with Alec Voikhansky's 3lb claim helpful.

runners.comment: Justified favouritism here (7f, good) in June; reasonable form since; races close to pace.
runners.spotlight: Lingfield regular (turf and AW winner); was always doing enough to justify favouritism over 7f here (good) in June and has posted three reasonable efforts since; likely to sit handy despite a wide draw in stall 7; can she see it out a bit better on this occasion?

runners.comment: Carries 5lb extra for Thursday's Epsom win; should have no issue with returning to AW.
runners.spotlight: Right back to form since fitted with a hood; arrives seeking a hat-trick following back-to-back all-the-way wins at Sandown (1m, soft) and Epsom (8.5f, soft); carries 5lb extra for Thursday's Epsom win and should have no issue with returning to AW (two wins); sets the benchmark.

runners.comment: Wolverhampton winner (8.5f fillies' nov); soft ground possible issue behind Tronido latest
runners.spotlight: Arrived late to win an 8.5f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton (12-1) in May; hit hard by the handicapper thereafter but has boxed away commendably, third at Kempton (1m3f) on penultimate start; soundly beaten by Tronido - soft ground a possible excuse - at Sandown latest.

runners.comment: Never travelling when beaten favourite (last to Tronido) at Sandown (1m, soft) latest.
runners.spotlight: Thrived for the switch to James Owen, winning in a first-time hood at Chester (7f, good) and then finishing runner-up at Yarmouth and Newmarket; never travelling when a beaten favourite (last of nine to Tronido) at Sandown (1m) a fortnight ago; headgear now discarded; 0-3 on AW.

stable_tour.quote: Owned by The Rogues Gallery, she's a filly we bought from the Doncaster Breeze-Up who ticks a lot of boxes. She's probably going to end up being a 7f filly, but will start over six soon. 30-05-24

runners.owner: S & A Mares, W Fisher & Partner
runners.comment: Last year's winner; has been struggling to recapture form lately; difficult to consider.
runners.spotlight: Rated 2lb lower than when winning this by a nose last year; has been struggling to recapture her form since returning to action in June, beating only a single rival on her last two starts at Haydock and Chelmsford (both 1m); difficult to consider.

runners.comment: Placed in three of her six AW starts (7f-1m); continues to ease in weights; tongue-tie on.
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her six AW starts (7f-1m) but yet to make the breakthrough; easing in the weights, a further 2lb lower than when fifth of 11 (beaten approximately 5l) to Tuesday's Child at Kempton 25 days ago; more needed in a newly applied tongue-tie.

runners.comment: On wrong side of the track when a beaten favourite at Newmarket (1m) latest; role to play.
runners.spotlight: Winning handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m, good; from 3lb lower) last month, but found herself on the wrong side of the track when a beaten favourite at Newmarket (1m, fourth; first home on near side) subsequently; has shown that she handles this C&D previously; positive role to play.

racecards.verdict: This isn't a gimme for the hat-trick seeking TRONIDO but she should have no issue with returning to AW and will be tough to peg back.
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Tronido, 4/1 Princess Mia, 5/1 Rogue Dynasty, 13/2 The New Bay Pearl, 10/1 Tempted, 11/1 Daisy Roots, 12/1 Liv My Life,  Lunarscape

racecards.race_name: Arc Celebrates Shared Ownership Handicap

runners.comment: Made all at Southwell (1m) in February; underwhelming lately; first visit to Lingfield.
runners.spotlight: Made all at Southwell (1m; 16-1) in February and also ran well (third; in first-time cheekpieces) there in April; underwhelming since, particularly on last two starts (both 1m); it's possible that this sharper track could suit.

runners.comment: 0-8; struggled in a newly applied visor at Brighton (7f, good; seventh of eight) latest.
runners.spotlight: 0-8; placed at Brighton (7f) and Bath (1m) but her standards have slipped since; struggled in a newly applied visor at Brighton (7f, good; seventh of eight) latest and that equipment is swiftly discarded for first AW appearance since March.

runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in January (debut); three lesser efforts since; set for handicap debut.
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye when runner-up in a C&D maiden (debut) in January but she's failed to better that since; weakened when third of four in a modest Bath novice (1m, good to firm; 11-2) six weeks ago; still low mileage but more is needed on handicap debut.

runners.comment: Beaten 13l+ when third in first-time tongue-tie at Salisbury (1m) latest; others preferred
runners.spotlight: Still seeking her first win (0-9) and her best performances on RPRs came during her juvenile season; last of eight at Windsor (1m) on penultimate start and beaten over 13l when third in a first-time tongue-tie at Salisbury (1m; 12-1) recently; others are better equipped.

runners.comment: Battled when runner-up on recent Ffos Las handicap debut (7.5f); could offer the solution.
runners.spotlight: Sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Kameko (who also won on AW); displayed improved form when a battling runner-up on recent Ffos Las handicap debut (7.5f, good) and could offer the solution on first AW start.

runners.comment: 0-5; hood applied and set to tackle a new trip on return to the AW; monitor only.
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; was returning from a 310-day absence when only sixth of nine on the July Course at Newmarket (7f; 33-1) three weeks ago; hood applied and set to tackle a new trip on return to the AW; monitor only for the time being.

runners.comment: Beaten half a length in a four-runner contest here (7f) in June; stamina the risk over 1m.
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10) but twice went close, at Kempton (7f) and here (7f) when beaten half a length by Royal Accord in a four-runner contest in June; remains fairly weighted but is yet to definitively prove himself over this trip; others safer.

runners.comment: Runner-up last two starts, incl on stable debut at Southwell (1m) last week; a possible.
runners.spotlight: Left Dermot Weld for 21,000gns; ran freely when runner-up at Musselburgh (1m1f; 11-8 favourite) on sole start for Michael Bell, and again occupied that same position on debut for this trainer at Southwell (1m; winner beaten at Newcastle since) last week; a possible.

runners.comment: C&D winner (same mark) in April; form levels have dipped in last three starts (7f-1m).
runners.spotlight: Popular in the market when winning on handicap debut (same mark) over C&D in April; consistent in the immediate aftermath but form levels have dipped on her last three starts (7f-1m) so she arrives with work to do.

runners.comment: 16-race maiden; returns to AW for first time since last October; safer to focus elsewhere.
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; placed at Salisbury (1m; 50-1) on penultimate start but not in the same league as the convincing winner; returns to AW for first time since last October; poorly drawn so safer to focus elsewhere.

racecards.verdict: None of these are particularly convincing but KINGSCLERE could offer the solution. Seconds Out and Elvetham best of the rest.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Kingsclere, 7/2 Seconds Count, 4/1 Elvetham, 15/2 Ventura Dream, 10/1 Warm Glow,  Muy Muy Loco, 12/1 Little She, 14/1 Evanka,  Sharma d'Amour, 40/1 Mount Of Gold

racecards.race_name: Arc All-Weather £1Million Bonus Is Back Handicap

runners.comment: Led late on at Brighton last month; not quite so good at Newmarket since; down in class
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt when getting up late in a 7f handicap at Brighton last month; third of eight in a stronger race at Newmarket three weeks ago (1m, good to firm); career best needed to defy top weight.

runners.comment: Unexposed filly who has shaped well in two handicap runs here; sharper for recent return
runners.spotlight: Good second over 6f on her nursery debut in December; not seen again until her close fourth over C&D nine days ago; may well come on from that and she's one to note.

runners.comment: Picked off late over 6f here last month; headgear left off today; chance despite 3lb rise
