runners.spotlight: 0-9 but she's finished second in a couple of handicaps this season, including when picked off late over 6f here last month (cheekpieces first time, left off today); 3lb rise shouldn't prevent another bold bid.

runners.comment: 0-10 and none too consistent but a repeat of his latest Thirsk 4th would see him involved
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has blown hot and cold this year; only beaten 1.5l when fourth of ten at Thirsk two weeks ago (1m, good) and he'll be in the mix with a repeat.

runners.comment: On the up in handicaps, winning well at Chepstow latest; should stay 7f; 7lb rise to defy
runners.spotlight: On the up since handicapping, second at Windsor and Brighton before a comfortable front-running success at Chepstow (all 6f on good ground); bred to stay 7f and he should have more to come; he'll need it to defy today's 7lb higher mark though; withdrawn at Windsor last month after getting upset in the stalls.

runners.comment: 7f win off 1lb lower (turf) in May; second on AW 16 days ago; contender off the same mark
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Brighton in May off 1lb lower; back to form when second at Chelmsford 16 days ago (7f); runs off the same mark and shouldn't be far away.

stable_tour.quote: He didn't appreciate the kickback on soft ground at Lingfield last time and Neil Callan looked after him in what was a bit of a non-event. He's come out of the race very well, though, and is fresh and bouncing at home. I'm going to run him back at Brighton on July 1 over seven furlongs - as that was the same course and distance he won over two starts ago. 18-06-25

runners.comment: Yet to be placed but has shaped with conspicuous promise and is open to more improvement
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in her four starts, including her handicap debut at Epsom in July, but she shaped with promise in her novice starts and remains open to progress; one to be interested in.

runners.comment: Little solid form; dropping down weights but headgear left off and others look stronger
runners.spotlight: Little solid form and he was well beaten at Brighton 12 days ago (6f, good to firm); goes without headgear today; others look stronger.

runners.comment: Not beaten far in a 6f handicap here last week; 7f should suit; unexposed
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far into fifth in a 6f handicap here last week, just found wanting for pace in the home straight; 7f could suit and she's open to improvement after just four starts.

racecards.verdict: An interesting race of its type in which APPLE OF MY EYE is fancied to build on earlier promise. Friday Again is feared most.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Summertime Blues, 5/1 Cooramook, 7/1 Callout,  Apple Of My Eye, 8/1 Friday Again,  Warrnambool,  Questa Notte, 12/1 Berning Hot, 16/1 Takteek

racecards.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.comment: Some ups and downs but came clear over C&D on Monday; sound chance under penalty
runners.spotlight: Some ups and downs this year but he was a close second of five at Bath (1m) during May in a higher grade than this and appreciated the return to the AW when doing well to get through a gap over C&D on Monday, winning going away; should go very well despite 5lb penalty.

runners.comment: In good form this time last year; has it to prove on handicap efforts this year
runners.spotlight: In good form in novice and maiden races at about 1m on turf and AW this time last year but she hasn't shone in handicaps of late, finding little when returned to 1m on Kempton AW in August; cheekpieces the next step.

runners.comment: Definite signs of ability in C&D maiden; interesting back at this trip on handicap debut
runners.spotlight: From family of bumper winners; showed little in that sphere but clearly has ability, as shown when running well for a long way in C&D maiden (250-1) last month; never sighted over 7f since but it's time to take more interest in her now she's back at 1m on handicap debut; blinkers return.

runners.comment: C&D winner; back in form of late; should go well again; H Doyle up for first time
runners.spotlight: Sole win in C&D novice in 2022 but he's been running well on Southwell Tapeta (1m) of late, a recent third of 13 being fair form in a slightly higher grade; should go well again with Hollie Doyle up for the first time.

runners.comment: All six wins over 7f/6f but stays 1m; close 2nd latest but surface query back on AW
runners.spotlight: All six wins over 7f and 6f, including one on AW, but she kept on stoutly when only just failing on a rare run over 1m at Brighton 25 days ago; well worth persevering with over this trip but nearly all her best form is on turf.

quotes.quote: I was a bit worried about the ground this morning because I was here yesterday when we had all that rain. Bear To Dream hadn't run for a while so I was worried about her getting caught out on that tacky ground, but she was obviously fitter than I thought she was. She's very straightforward. She does plenty at home and Kaiya's [Fraser] has given her a great ride. That's his first winner for us and we've been using him for a bit. He has a real nice way of riding - Michael Attwater, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing

runners.comment: 1lb lower than C&D win in January but he hasn't been in the same form since
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins; now 1lb lower than the latest of them in January but he hasn't been in the same form since, including on this track, and hard to predict revival.

runners.comment: Won AW novice as 3yo; well treated on pick of Tapeta form; dangerous for new yard
runners.spotlight: Winless since landing a 1m novice on Newcastle AW in 2023 but the pick of his Tapeta handicap form since shows he's well treated on yard debut; left the Barrons for £11,000 since last seen; likely to be dangerous if taking to Polytrack.

runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya
runners.comment: Has won at 1m on turf; this year's efforts don't point to a win this time
runners.spotlight: Has won over 1m on turf and the return to this trip should suit but this year's efforts don't point to a win this time.

racecards.verdict: The surface shouldn't be a problem on pedigree for PARISH COUNCILLOR and he is well treated on his debut for a new yard.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Parish Councillor, 11/4 My Ambition, 5/1 Bear To Dream, 13/2 Francesi, 10/1 Bayadere, 14/1 Raqraaq, 16/1 Miss Tinkerbell, 25/1 Haaf A Diamond

racecards.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.comment: Smooth C&D win nine days ago; 4lb rise less of a concern than her wide draw
runners.spotlight: She was having just her third run on AW when running out the stylish winner of a C&D handicap nine days ago; a 4lb rise shouldn't be beyond her but stall nine tempers enthusiasm.

quotes.quote: Glamorous Breeze is just quirky. She's got plenty of ability and was second in a Listed race last season. This year I figured her out, sit, hold still then [go] last 50 yards. It's not she's ungenuine, she just does enough - William Cox, jockey.

runners.comment: Two small-field wins last month; held when meeting trouble latest; needs a career best
runners.spotlight: Won two small-field events on turf last month (5f, good and good to firm); only fifth (met some late trouble) at Goodwood three weeks ago; career best required to take this.

runners.comment: Latest run not the one to judge him on (caught wide) but again the draw hasn't been kind
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; none too consistent but latest seventh (behind Glamorous Breeze) is easy to excuse as he was caught wide from stall 9; hasn't fared much better with the draw today and there is plenty of other pace drawn inside him.

runners.comment: Good 2nd at Windsor last month (5f, good to firm); big run expected from stall 1
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day and he ran well for second in a Racing League handicap at Windsor four weeks ago; has the inside stall today and should make a bold bid.

quotes.quote: Clearpoint just didn't scope as I wanted before Ascot, but he's a pretty smart horse and we like him a lot. He's got a great mind, he's a very talented horse and he'll go to the July Stakes now. I just wanted to get another run into him - I didn't work him this week, I ran him instead. To be honest I think he's a star - a future star, one of the few - Richard Fahey, trainer.

runners.comment: Won this race off 2lb higher last year; retains all his ability and he's greatly respected
runners.spotlight: Got up late when winning this race 12 months ago and he is 2lb lower this time around; 0-11 this year but he retains all his ability and he's greatly respected at a track he runs well.

quotes.race: It's Never Ordinary At bet365 Handicap
quotes.quote: The Thames Boatman needs pace in his races and looking at this race beforehand I knew he'd get that today, so that was an advantage. He's at his best when he has something to aim at, but he made life harder for himself by hanging in the last half a furlong. We do think he's a smart horse, especially on the all-weather - Finley Marsh, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He was brilliant at Lingfield on Saturday, taking his record at the track to 3-3. He just got there over 5f and we might see an ounce of improvement if we go to 6f. He loves Lingfield, so we'll probably stick to there for now. 18-09-24

runners.owner: Ian Wight, Tony Lyons And Ptnr

prev_owners.owner: Ian Wight, Mrs Teresa Lyons And Ptnr

runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but mark fair judged on his 2025 best; sharp 5f a slight query
runners.spotlight: His only win came when a red-hot favourite for a three-runner novice at Ffos Las last summer (5f, good to soft); this mark in range judged on 2025 best but this sharp 5f isn't sure to see him to best effect.

runners.comment: Reliable performer on AW; feasibly weighted if new accessories have the desired effect
runners.spotlight: Reliable performer on AW, winning three of his eight starts (all 5f); not beaten far into fourth at Chelmsford 12 weeks ago; dropped 2lb and returns to action with new accessories fitted.

runners.comment: Two low-key turf runs in spring but on a good mark back on AW and well drawn; contender
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of early speed and he put it to good use at Chelmsford on his reappearance in February (5f; blinkered first time); didn't handle Epsom in April and not at his best at Goodwood the following month; getting back on AW looks a plus and he's well drawn; should have a big run in him with cheekpieces replacing blinkers (formfigures of 1124 in today's headgear).

runners.comment: Two 5f wins this summer but ran poorly latest and has a bad draw this time
runners.spotlight: Made most for wins at Yarmouth and York in the first half of the season; solid effort back at York in her hat-trick bid but she needs to rebound from a poor run at the Ebor meeting and stall 10 won't help, especially given she has hung right in the past.

runners.breeder: Mrs J A Rawding & Miss A J Rawding
runners.comment: Looked good on turf here in June; solid efforts since; needs something extra to win
runners.spotlight: The winner of two of his nine starts, notably an emphatic success on turf here (good to firm) in June; solid efforts in both starts since but he needs something extra to defy this mark.

racecards.verdict: He'll need some luck in running but this could set up for last year's winner THE THAMES BOATMAN (nap). Twilight Fun is feared most.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Glamorous Breeze,  The Thames Boatman, 5/1 Clearpoint, 6/1 Fidelius, 7/1 Twilight Fun, 15/2 Grandlad, 8/1 Diomed Duke, 14/1 Kinetic Force,  Cayman Tai, 16/1 Forager

racecards.race_name: Dovend Electrical Ltd Apprentice Handicap

runners.comment: Not at best here last month but this three-time C&D winner is back on last winning mark
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who is now back on same mark as for 7f success at Catterick (good to firm) in July; behind a couple of these when only sixth here 18 days ago but still considered.

stable_tour.quote: She did really well for us last season, winning three times. She needs a mile but she needs a bend and there's nothing for her at Catterick, so that limits her options there. She won twice at Musselburgh last year and that mile round a bend really suits her. Musselburgh, fast ground and a mile are her ideal conditions. She's back down to a winnable mark of 56, having won twice off that last year. 21-05-25

runners.comment: Seems best suited by stiffer tracks but can't be ruled out off current mark
runners.spotlight: Needs to rebound from a poor run six weeks ago and is seemingly better suited by stiffer tracks than this but has slipped to a good mark and can't be ruled out.

runners.comment: Course winner this summer; needs to better her last couple of efforts, though
runners.spotlight: Led close home to win four-runner race here (1m1f, good) in July and ran at least as well when close second over that trip last month; contender if judged on that form but needs to better her last couple of efforts.

runners.comment: Short of room at crucial stage when placed over C&D last month; high on list
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent this season, and hit the target at Ayr (1m2f, good to soft) in July; short of room at crucial stage when third over C&D (good; just behind On The Bubble) 18 days ago; high on the list.

quotes.quote: I came here today fancying Zebra Star a lot more than than Ski Angel (trainer's later winner). This mare has changed. Clyde Vets were fantastic and we found a sinus problem and operated to sort it at the tail end of last year. It's definitely helped her and she's getting stronger and better all the time, and I think she'll stay further.   She's taken a wee bit of time to grow and has just matured over the winter - Mike Smith, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

quotes.race: Racing League On Sky Sports Racing Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Soft ground is the key to Zebra Star and she loved it out there. Hopefully she'll have a productive autumn and also has the scope for next year - George Boughey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She won very well at Ayr last week. She has gone up 3lb to 60 and is entered back there on Tuesday night. She's grown about an inch and a half over the winter. She had a sinus problem but had an operation and it looks like it's made a big difference. Euchan Falls, one of our jumpers, had it as well and he also ran well afterwards. I see her as a mid-60s horse. 16-05-24

runners.comment: Second in 7f Catterick classified last month; might be vulnerable after recent 3lb rise
runners.spotlight: Has better strike-rate on AW, and the form of last month's second in 7f classified at Catterick (good) isn't anything to go wild about; will be fine back at 1m but might be a shade vulnerable after recent 3lb rise.

runners.comment: Below form last time but made the frame four times in a row at Ayr beforehand
runners.spotlight: Below form on latest outing but made the frame four consecutive times at Ayr (7f-1m) this summer and has gradually edged down the weights; could have a say.

quotes.quote: Golden Valour is much better than his mark. I thought he wasn't helping Donagh up the straight but it was hard work in that wind. There's a valuable handicap here next month which we might go for - Richard O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We ran him over hurdles during the winter but he's had a break since February and will be running on the Flat again soon, where he's still only rated 55. He's a nice-looking, well-bred horse. He might do a bit of both, Flat and jumps, just to sweeten him up. He's been a bit unlucky and I‘m hopeful he can improve from his current mark when he comes out. I'm looking forward to running him. 16-05-24

runners.comment: Went close two starts ago but this 16-race maiden is too inconsistent to rely upon
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; went close in Ayr classified (1m, good) last month but finished well down the field in a subsequent AW handicap (1m2f) and cannot be relied upon heavily; swaps cheekpieces for refitted visor.

runners.comment: Regressive 9yo who made low-key seasonal/stable debut 12 days ago
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner in her prime but struggled on both outings for Ben Haslam last year and made a low-key reappearance for new stable 12 days ago; needs to have taken considerable benefit from that recent outing.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Every Saturday Money Back Handicap
quotes.quote: Sassoon would have been an unlucky loser and you need the luck sometimes, but I think they've made the right decision. He'd popped his nose in front just before the line and then after, and sometimes you need that rub of the green. He's a different animal on really heavy ground. He comes alive at this time of year and it's like chalk and cheese with him when that ground is there. Funnily enough he won a very similar contest nearly exactly three years ago, before he went and won at Redcar the week after. I think we might stick to that plan again - Ben Haslam, trainer.

runners.comment: Yet to win for new stable this year but ran well in defeat over C&D last month
runners.spotlight: Yet to win for new stable this year but put in good front-running shift when close second over C&D (good) last month and has obvious claims if in similar form again.

runners.comment: 11-race maiden who has not shown much on her three starts for Katie Scott this summer
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who has not shown much on her three starts for Katie Scott this summer (1m-1m4f); urgently needs to turn a corner.

stable_tour.quote: She is owned by Summerstorm Bloodstock, who also have Luna A Inbhir Nis with us. She was at Jim Goldie's for her last couple of runs and was just a bit disappointing, so they wanted to give her a change of scenery to see if it perked her up. She's had a wind operation and she's just back in full work. Another three or four weeks she'll be out running again and we'll see if it's helped her or not. 17-07-25

runners.comment: Without a win since May 2023, and her form over the last two seasons is not good enough
runners.spotlight: Without a win since May 2023, and her form over the last two seasons does not suggest she can end her drought.

racecards.verdict: Mike Smith's ZEBRA STAR might have been a shade unlucky not to go close over C&D 18 days ago and could be the answer.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Zebra Star, 4/1 On The Bubble, 11/2 Martin's Brig, 7/1 Without Delay,  Shahnaz,  Golden Valour, 8/1 Spring Festival, 10/1 Scarriff, 25/1 Yellow Lemons, 33/1 Sassoon, 50/1 Royal Countess

racecards.race_name: British EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Needs improvement for the new trip
runners.spotlight: Has something to find on his 5f efforts and thus needs improvement for this longer trip.

runners.comment: Finished third upped to 7f at Catterick last time; again has place possibilities
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who finished third upped to 7f at Catterick (soft) most recently; again has place possibilities.

runners.comment: Encouraging debut in C&D maiden last month; finishing third; warrants respect
runners.spotlight: Encouraging debut in similar event over C&D (good) last month, finishing third of eight; open to progress and warrants respect.

runners.comment: Ties in with Suddenly I See on C&D run last month; pipped in 7f seller at Salisbury Friday
runners.spotlight: Ties in with Suddenly I See on C&D running last month (third start) and is one of the main form players; stuck to his task well when narrowly beaten by a 66-1 shot in 7f seller at Salisbury on Friday.

runners.comment: Both starts over 6f, better effort when fourth at Pontefract latest
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f, better effort when fourth at Pontefract latest; needs to show further progress upped in distance.

runners.comment: Absent and gelded since showing ability at Hamilton three months ago
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since showing ability with third of six at Hamilton (6f, good) three months ago; check the market signals.

runners.comment: Turf debutant who has frame possibilities judged on his AW efforts
runners.spotlight: Turf debutant who has frame possibilities judged on his 7f AW efforts, fourth at Chelmsford latest.

runners.comment: Gradually moving in right direction but his form is modest; headgear fitted
runners.spotlight: Gradually moving in the right direction but his form is modest, third in Class 6 nursery here (1m) most recently; headgear applied.

runners.comment: Both outings over C&D; fighting chance provided he's back to debut form
runners.spotlight: Both outings over C&D, better effort when close third on debut; fighting chance provided he's back to that form.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a nice race on his debut at Musselburgh when beaten half a length. He went back there four weeks later and didn't handle the ground at all. We were a bit disappointed with the run, but he'll be better suited to a bit of ease in the ground or the all-weather. He's a really nice horse. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Showed ability in Beverley maiden; possibilities if building on that effort
runners.spotlight: Showed ability with fourth of 13 in Beverley maiden (7.5f, good to firm) and has possibilities if building on that effort.

runners.comment: Eyecatching fifth in AW maiden; interesting with improvement plausible
runners.spotlight: Eyecatching fifth of 11, amid traffic issues, in Wolverhampton maiden (7f, AW); interesting with improvement plausible.

racecards.verdict: Suddenly I See warrants respect despite being drawn wide. SASHAY AWAY, on the inner, looks an interesting alternative.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Suddenly I See, 9/2 Supreme Clarets, 6/1 Spirit Of The Wood, 8/1 Lyrical Song,  Welbury,  Sashay Away, 10/1 Hakin Adraar,  Kipepeo, 12/1 Panthere Noir, 16/1 Vincent Gambini, 66/1 Breeze Star

racecards.race_name: Royal Regiment Of Scotland Trophy Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Record is 1-10; creditable second at Thirsk last Saturday; may remain competitive
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-10, the success in a maiden; however, creditable second in Thirsk nursery (5f) last Saturday and may remain competitive.

runners.comment: Returns to a more realistic level after running in sales race but is quite exposed
runners.spotlight: Returns to a more realistic level after running in sales race but is quite exposed, notwithstanding a maiden win two starts ago.

runners.comment: Runner-up off this mark at Musselburgh and Ripon since handicapping; solid chance
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off this mark over 5f at Musselburgh (good) and Ripon (good to firm) since switched to nursery level; solid chance.

runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
runners.comment: Well held on AW last time; record of 331 on turf; may do better still in this sphere
runners.spotlight: Well held over 6f on AW last time; record of 331 over 5f on turf otherwise and may do better still in this sphere; not ruled out.

runners.comment: Has shown best form over C&D; could go well, provided he takes to headgear
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; has shown best form over C&D; returns to Musselburgh and could go well, provided he takes to first-time headgear.

runners.comment: Shapes as if this drop in distance will suit on nursery debut; interesting contender
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability over 6f, notably when second at Chester, while shaping as if this drop in trip will suit on nursery debut; interesting.

runners.comment: Ties in closely with Straight Ahead on Ripon running last time; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Ties in closely with Straight Ahead on Ripon running (nursery debut) last time; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.

runners.comment: Improved form the last twice, winning over C&D then third on AW; respected back here
runners.spotlight: Improved form in nurseries the last twice, winning over C&D (good) then close second at Southwell (6f, AW); respected back here.

quotes.quote: It wasn't a surprise. We've always liked Davorge Nation but he just took a little bit of time to come to hand. In the last few weeks things have gone right for him and he's done that well. He got a little bit stopped in the run last time and we thought this might suit him better. The runner-up made us run right to the line and I don't know what was behind him, but you can only win. He likes this quick ground, so we'll keep running while we can -  Declan Carroll, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Best effort when 1l second to Davorge Nation over C&D (sole nursery attempt)
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; best effort when 1l second to Davorge Nation over C&D on penultimate start (sole nursery attempt); in the mix.

racecards.verdict: Nursery debutant BLAZING is particularly interesting with progress plausible. C&D winner Davorge Nation is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 U Turn,  Blazing, 5/1 Davorge Nation, 6/1 Straight Ahead, 7/1 Rotokura Belle, 8/1 Havin A Flyer, 9/1 Brimfulofattitude, 12/1 Arduis Invicta,  Kodigara

racecards.race_name: Peterhead Fish Market Handicap

prev_owners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Jonathan B Munz

runners.comment: Beaten a long way in blinkers on handicap debut at Newcastle on Thursday evening
runners.spotlight: Cost 500,000gns as a yearling and has Group 1 winners in his family, but has shown only modest form and was beaten a long way in blinkers on handicap debut at Newcastle (2m) on Thursday evening.

runners.comment: Holding form well in handicaps, second at Catterick and third at Ffos Las; solid candidate
runners.spotlight: Holding her form well in handicaps, following clear second at Catterick (1m4f, soft) with very close third at Ffos Las (1m2f, soft); handles quick ground too; solid candidate.

runners.comment: Standout run came when second at Carlisle in July; ran poorly on faster ground next time
runners.spotlight: Standout run came when 1l second of ten in handicap at Carlisle (1m3f, good to soft) in July; ran poorly on faster ground when favourite over same C&D next time; needs to bounce back.

runners.breeder: Sas Regnier & San Gabriel Inv Inc
runners.comment: Saw off several challengers when gamely making all at Newcastle; should go well again.
runners.spotlight: Saw off several challengers when gamely making all at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) two weeks ago for his first success; should be just as effective over 1m4f; up just 2lb and should go well again.

stable_tour.quote: We've had him for a year. He's shown bits of promise here and there and his last run was quite good when third behind our own Straya. I don't think he's any kind of worldbeater but he might go up in trip and improve for that. He ran well over 1m2f at Ayr last week, and I think I'd like to step him up to 1m4f, but he's only three and so there's not a lot of opportunities to do that at the moment. We'll just to have to sit tight and wait, especially if we don't want to be travelling hours and hours down the road. He'll probably be out again when we get a bit of rain. 17-07-25

runners.comment: Placed a few times but now 0-15 and recent efforts have been disappointing
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times but now 0-15 and recent efforts have been disappointing; difficult to recommend.

runners.comment: 0-16, unplaced all starts; needs first-time headgear to have a transformative effect
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden, unplaced all starts; tailed off on latest outing and needs first-time headgear to have a transformative effect.

racecards.verdict: The most solid contender is the only filly, PENN AVENUE (nap), and she is taken to win for the second time.
racecards.betting_forecast: Evs Penn Avenue, 9/4 Falaise Blanc, 11/2 Sidney Stratton, 14/1 Bebside, 16/1 Woodshaw, 20/1 Himself

racecards.race_name: Scots Association Musselburgh Gold Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Ran respectably at the York Ebor festival last time; still on a workable mark
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in higher grade at the York Ebor festival (2m) last time and remains favourably treated, having gained sole Flat handicap win off this mark; not ruled out.

quotes.race: William Hill More Top Prices Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div I)
quotes.quote: I'd say Maghlaak is going to be a decent horse, jumping will toughen him up. His bit of class showed there late on and he did that well.  We bought him as a dual-purpose horse and he has a great pedigree - his dam won the Guineas - Adrian Keatley, trainer

quotes.race: Golf At Goodwood Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Maghlaak had a lot of weight, so did it well. I've no idea about plans - Jim Crowley, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He finished well to win at Goodwood on his comeback, but he was drawn ten of ten at Redcar last time and could never get any cover. It was a non-event and I'm putting a line through it. He's a pretty talented horse, who likes to get his toe in, and I think the mile and a half would suit him well if he makes the cut. 13-06-24

He's been a good servant for us. He ran a good race in the Group 3 Silver Cup at York and then went so close at Market Rasen. I think if we keep stepping him up in trip, we'll get similar or better results with him. We'll look at the staying race at the Shergar Cup meeting and continue to mix it between hurdles and the Flat. He might jump fences in time. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Heavy defeat when last seen but generally consistent at up to 1m6f otherwise
runners.spotlight: Absent since heavy defeat in major 1m4f handicap at Haydock ten weeks ago; generally consistent otherwise and may rebound, provided he sees out this new trip; stays a testing 1m6f well.

quotes.quote: That's a great prize to win. Minstrel Knight has been very consistent and he loves this type of ground. He's done that really well. He's in the Horses In Training sale but I need to talk to the owner - Ed Bethell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was progressive last year and I think he'll be the same this season. He ran a great race first time up at Hamilton, where he travelled well from off the pace and looked as if he was going to do some damage but made quite a big move at a tough time and got tired. He's pretty ground dependent, he needs it soft, and is entered in the Old Newton Cup and a £100,000 race at the Newmarket July festival. If it came up soft, the Ebor could be a fun race for him. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Successful in last three turf attempts, latest over C&D; improving mare; big player
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive for current yard and successful in last three turf attempts (1m4f-2m2f, good), beating Simple Star over C&D latest; may not have reached her ceiling yet; major player.

runners.comment: Ran well behind Paper Doll when bidding for a Musselburgh double; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Plentiful form at Musselburgh includes sound performances the last twice (good), winning over the extended 1m4f then second to Paper Doll over 2m2f; likely to remain competitive.

quotes.quote: We were very disappointed last week at Carlisle so Doreen [Monteith, owner] said let's try Simple Star back over hurdles. He was a little star for us last year on the Flat. He came back this season and ran a great race at Musselburgh and then I think it was trainer error putting him into tracks that started with a downhill and he just got too keen. Hopefully, he'll be back at Musselburgh at the end of the Flat season - Dianne Sayer, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Opened his account in Goodwood event last week; may build on that win; respected
runners.spotlight: Opened his account in Goodwood contest last week when attempting 2m for the first time; may build on that success and show additional progress upped further in distance; respected.

quotes.quote: That was a good performance. The three-year-olds over this sort of distance get a good weight allowance and we thought that would really matter in this kind of ground. Novelista is a big, immature horse who stays every inch of the trip and he deserved a win - Mark Johnston, trainer's assistant. Novelista deserved that today. He's probably still a bit weak and is probably better on a flatter track but that should do his confidence the world of good - Hollie Doyle, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.breeder: S A R L Al Shahania Stud France
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival last time on debut for new stable
runners.spotlight: Five wins for previous yard; dropped away and failed to beat a rival last time on debut for new stable; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a return to form is expected.

quotes.quote: Dreams Adozen handled the ground. It's absolutely bottomless but she ploughed through it, maybe the others didn't go in the ground - Joe Fanning, jockey.

runners.owner: J S Morrison & Ms Carol Morrison
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season and finished last in small field most recently
runners.spotlight: Bagged a big bonus for winning three Sunday Series events in 2024; inconsistent this season and finished last in small field most recently; admittedly very well treated on best form.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap
quotes.quote: Letsbefrank is getting very lethargic in his races and he was never on the bridle. He's tough and game, but the Johnston horse got a soft lead and you know they'll always keep going. But this horse keeps digging, he's tough. I'm lucky I'm the one pointing him, but this has been the plan for Jim and his team since the winter. He's had all the races mapped out, credit to him. To win one race is hard but to win three takes some doing - Paul Mulrennan, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's been frustrating at the start of this year. We did such a good job with him last year, so it just swings and roundabouts. We've given him a couple of runs and he was a little bit better at Thirsk, but he was still playing games. I've taken my foot off the accelerator a bit and we're treating him for ulcers and various health things. He had quite a hard season last year so maybe we're paying for it, but I'm sure he'll bounce back. He's still got an engine. 07-05-25

runners.comment: The manner of his 1m5f win last week suggests returning to further will suit
runners.spotlight: Regained the winning thread at Hamilton (good to soft) last week, needing every yard of the stiff 1m5f; bit to prove over 2m2f but the step back up in trip seems worth exploring.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Chatteris Fen Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Perseus Way is a very nice horse and I'm pleased that he has done it well today. His jumping is getting better all the time, but it needs to. He is getting slicker and quicker and he is starting to enjoy his racing. It was a good run in defeat at Chepstow last time and I felt that Chepstow was probably soft enough for him. We rode him too far back that day and that is why we rode him handier today. I don't think he is good enough to run in the Triumph but I think he could have a strong chance in the handicap - Gary Moore, trainer.

Perseus Way has not run for months because we've been waiting for the ground. Jim said he'd get as far as you'd want. There is talk Gary Moore could have him for jumping at some stage. We were waiting to see what he'd do today because if he was well beaten, he might have gone to Gary's sooner! We just got away with the trip today as he's a grinder who will appreciate more give in the ground - Owen Burrows, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Reference Point Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was very pleased with Perseus Way. I hadn't sat on him at home and it was a proper slog on this ground, but he was quite fit and was able to keep going. He coped with conditions well. He's definitely a nice type for next year, he's big and raw and should furnish away over the winter - James Doyle, jockey.

runners.comment: Beaten in races won by Paper Doll and Perseus Way the last twice
runners.spotlight: Triple course winner but may have to settle for a supporting role, having been beaten in races won by Paper Doll and Perseus Way the last twice and being 3lb out of weights.

stable_tour.quote: He won at Musselburgh over 2m2f in late August and has finished third the last twice- at Chester and Ayr on Tuesday. He's probably going to have another couple of runs this autumn as he seems to handle soft ground. He's a young horse who's improving. He ran on good ground at Ayr on Tuesday and it would be good if we can get another run with him on soft ground before the end of the season. I doubt we'll run him on the all-weather this winter and we'll probably wait until next year with him. 02-10-24

racecards.verdict: The sole 3yo in the field, NOVELISTA, is taken to follow up his Goodwood success. Paper Doll is second pick.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Novelista, 7/2 Paper Doll, 11/2 Simple Star, 7/1 Maghlaak, 9/1 Minstrel Knight,  Perseus Way, 14/1 Dreams Adozen,  Letsbefrank, 16/1 Trojan Sun

racecards.race_name: Lothian Supply Company Handicap

runners.comment: Won five on the spin and went off too fast in a higher grade last week at Thirsk
runners.spotlight: Shot up the weights for winning five on the spin but went off too fast in a higher grade last week at Thirsk (having been raised 7lb); doesn't have to lead and could bounce back.

quotes.race: Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Jannas Journey is an amazing filly and seems to be improving. It wasn't the most competitive race, but you can't take anything away from Jim - he's worked wonders with her - Danny Tudhope, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Running well but has a bit to find with In A Hurry on latest run; each-way claims
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who arrives here in decent nick, though has a bit to find with In A Hurry on latest run; each-way claims.

quotes.race: ICM Stellar Sports Earl Grosvenor Handicap
quotes.quote: Red Mirage jumped from the gate really well and I wanted to hold a forward position with him and he got a bit lit up doing that. He then relaxed and when I took it to him, he really did point his toe out nicely - David Probert, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's had soundness issues but has always worked well and won at Kempton in October. He did his first fast work last week and will run in a novice in a couple of weeks. 18-04-21

I was gutted he didn't win in his first season with us. I'm not sure whether he was ever in red-hot form or whether he'd been through two good yards and there was nothing left in the tank. He's pleasing us in training, goes on soft ground and will be ready to run early, so we'll know quite soon. He's competitively handicapped. 20-03-24

runners.comment: Never ideally positioned back over 6f last time and returns to 7f off a dangerous mark
runners.spotlight: Only 2-21 but was never ideally positioned back over 6f last time and returns to 7f off a dangerous mark.

runners.comment: Below par this year and well held by the winner in a small field at Hamilton recently
runners.spotlight: Hasn't found her best form so far this year and was well held by the winner in a small field at Hamilton recently.

runners.comment: Maiden winner over this trip in Ireland and bang there in 7f handicaps for current yard
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over this trip in Ireland and bang there in two 7f handicaps for current yard, on good ground and the AW; 3lb rise is annoying but needs respecting.

stable_tour.quote: We picked him up recently at the sales for Ursa Major Racing. He came out of Kevin Coleman's yard. He's got nice form in Ireland - he won at Dundalk last season and finished third on his last run at Down Royal in June behind Summer Snow. He's a nice, big horse so it will be exciting to get him out on the track. 17-07-25

runners.comment: Should be well handicapped off this mark but last three outings have been underwhelming
runners.spotlight: Should be well handicapped off this mark but last three outings have been underwhelming; behind two of these last time at Carlisle.

quotes.race: Spreadex Racing In-Running Spread Betting Handicap
quotes.quote: There is quite a stiff side and head wind now, but he still picked up lovely. I know he's also won at Redcar and Catterick, but he probably has more strengthening to do and confidence to gain - Faye McManoman, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Looks handicapped about right but can have some say in current form
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; last three runs over 7f, winning a Thirsk maiden and since placed in handicaps at Catterick and Carlisle; looks handicapped about right but can have some say.

runners.comment: Won a 7f handicap at Catterick in July but has finished last since then, over 1m and 6f
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Catterick in July but has finished last since then, over 1m and 6f.

stable_tour.quote: A filly who has pleased us this year. She won at Catterick in July and is probably better over 7f round a bend. She's got ability and there are more races for her. She's very tough. 10-09-25

runners.owner: Paul Corrigan & Miss L A Perratt
runners.comment: Some near misses this season but not one to be making excuses for
runners.spotlight: Had some near misses this season but not one to be making excuses for as he has a modest strike-rate; well held at Ayr last time and too unpredictable to fancy.

runners.comment: Scopey gelding who might be getting the hang of things after winning over C&D
runners.spotlight: Scopey gelding who might be getting the hang of things after winning over C&D (good) 18 days ago as he was nicely on top at the finish; 5lb higher.

runners.comment: Has won here but better at Carlisle and needs further than 7f these days
runners.spotlight: Has won here but better at Carlisle and needs further than 7f these days, as latest run seemingly confirmed.

racecards.verdict: If there's an improver lurking amongst these then it's WADACRE MAESTRO who got off the mark last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Black Storm, 5/1 Wadacre Maestro, 6/1 In A Hurry, 7/1 Red Mirage,  Ziggy's Condor, 8/1 Jannas Journey,  Vince Le Prince, 10/1 Rock Melody, 20/1 Invincible Ruby,  Judgment Call, 25/1 Detective

racecards.race_name: Jewson Edinburgh Craigentinny Handicap

runners.comment: Winner and second on last two starts; should be in the thick of things again
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 16th attempt when readily seeing off nine rivals in classified event at Wolverhampton (5f) and ran just as well when second in similar contest at Thirsk (6f, good); should be in the thick of things again.

runners.comment: 5f AW winner at Newcastle in August; handles turf and not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Arrives in reasonable form with a win and a fifth over 5f at Newcastle on her last two outings; unplaced all seven turf starts but she does handle grass; not ruled out.

runners.comment: 12-race maiden; followed creditable third at Thirsk with below-par run at Newcastle
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who has each-way claims on his third of six at Thirsk (5f, good) in July but was well below par at Newcastle the following week; perhaps he's better on turf than AW but he needs to bounce back.

runners.owner: William Burns & Miss L A Perratt
runners.comment: All nine wins have been at Newcastle and he's 0-35 on turf; others make more appeal
runners.spotlight: All nine of his wins have been at Newcastle and he's 0-35 on turf; soundly beaten when last seen at Ayr last month; others make more appeal.

runners.breeder: D D & Mrs J P Clee
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; second here 18 days ago; arguably the pick of Linda Perratt's four
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner and ran well when second of eight here 18 days ago with three of today's rivals behind; arguably the pick of four for Linda Perratt.

runners.breeder: Oghill House Stud & Joseph M Burke
runners.owner: John Murphy, W F Perratt,Jackton Racing
runners.comment: In fair form when last seen but off three months and has been unplaced all six turf runs
runners.spotlight: Made breakthrough at the tenth attempt at Newcastle (5f) in March and wasn't disgraced after three months off at Wolverhampton in June; however, he's now had another break and he's been unplaced on all six turf runs.

runners.comment: Two C&D successes; below last winning mark and should be thereabouts if getting the breaks
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, and shaped better than the bare result (slowly away and short of room) when fourth here 18 days ago; below her last winning mark and should be thereabouts if getting the breaks.

stable_tour.quote: The owners have been very patient as they bought her as a yearling. She went to John Quinn but never raced as a two-year-old. She ran three times as a three-year-old without winning and the owners brought her here along with Bloomin Robbery. She's now won three times for us, including at Ayr last time out. She's gone up 5lb for that to 53, but the way she won you'd like to think she's got a bit more in hand and I see her as a high 50s or low 60s horse. She was ridden last time by Duran Fentiman,  who rides for Tim Easterby. He's an underrated jockey - very strong and capable. 16-05-24

runners.comment: Unexposed after just four runs; shaped well over C&D on reappearance; interesting
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after just four runs; looked green as a 2yo and appeared a much improved model when third of eight over C&D (good ground) 18 days ago, fading after eight months off; the hood (omitted on return) goes back on today; in good hands and has far more potential than most of today's rivals.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs J K Powell & Mrs A Doyle

runners.comment: Only fifth on most recent start here when behind Sixcor, Crackinthunder and Ski Angel
runners.spotlight: Most recent win came at 50-1 over C&D last summer; second here last month but only fifth on most recent start when behind Sixcor, Crackinthunder and Ski Angel.

quotes.race: Bob Veitch 90th Birthday Bash Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: We thought we'd take everything off. Hard Nut had threatened to win a few times, but if there's a bit of trouble he'll be the one to get in it, whether it's traffic jams or receiving a bump. We always thought he was ready to win, but he just never got his head in front. He looked like he enjoyed that and hopefully that will give him a bit of confidence now and he can go on - Linda Perratt, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Mirage Signs EBF Novice Auction Stakes (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: It was a good performance from Hard Nut. I don't think it was much of a race, but he did everything right and showed plenty of speed. He finished out his race well. I was worried about the last furlong, but he stays six. The ground was quick enough and he'd take some ease - Sean Levey, rider.

runners.comment: Veteran; 0-30 since October 2023 and in rear on last four outings
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has not won since October 2023 and has finished in rear on his last four outings; others appeal more.

quotes.quote: John Kirkup was due a win as he's been unlucky all season. He got his ground and he always shows his best - David Thompson, trainer.

John Kirkup loves these conditions and it was a good idea to drop him back to five furlongs on this stiff track. He travelled into it well and got the splits at the right time - Connor Beasley, rider.

John Kirkup could be one for the Two-Year-Old Trophy at Redcar and he's likely to run in another nursery before then. He can go over five or six furlongs but wants give in the ground - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: Edmundson Cabletech Carlisle Novice Auction Stakes
quotes.quote: John Kirkup got shuffled back and to finish like he has is a fair effort particularly with his penalty. He won't run without a bit of cut in the ground - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing UK In HD Novice Stakes
quotes.quote: John Kirkup ran with the choke out first time at Ayr and we've brought him back to a stiff five furlongs and got him behind horses and he's dropped his head. He's quite a nice horse and has done it nicely on ground he has enjoyed - Michael Dods, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Another old warrior with a decent wins to runs ratio and he goes particularly well for Connor Beasley. The thing with John is that you can not have the ground soft enough for him. We took a gamble running him at Ayr last month as the rains were forecast, thankfully it arrived and he won for the eighth time. However, last time out at Thirsk it was good ground which is too quick for him. 10-08-21

runners.comment: Unplaced all seven starts in Ireland; needs blinkers to make a big difference
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all seven starts in Ireland, achieving poor form at best; needs blinkers (has worn a visor) to make a big difference.

racecards.verdict: Crackinthunder is unexposed and could overcome a wide draw but just preferred is the in-form SANBONA.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Crackinthunder, 4/1 Sanbona, 9/2 Sixcor, 11/2 Ski Angel, 7/1 Dorothy May, 9/1 Shatin Venture, 12/1 Beerwah, 16/1 Pockley, 20/1 Carmina Burana, 25/1 Hard Nut,  John Kirkup

racecards.race_name: Ballylinch Stud Irish EBF Ingabelle Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)

runners.comment: Limitations have been exposed in Pattern company since a C&D maiden win, handles soft
runners.spotlight: Won a barrier trial at Naas in May; put that experience to good use when landing a C&D maiden on soft; twice fourth in small-field Group races won by the Aidan O'Brien-trained Composing; ground not an issue, but limitations have been exposed.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I think Brownstown is a smart filly and has a bit of class, she's been doing everything right at home. She's a big, long-striding filly, picked up well from the 2f to the 1f marker, just got a bit tired inside the final furlong. Nicer ground will help her, she'll come on for that and she has the size and scope to keep improving - Gary Carroll, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Made winning debut at Naas, well-regarded and might not be out of place in this company
runners.spotlight: Did well to overcome inexperience to make a winning debut in a fillies' maiden at Naas late last month; well-regarded and might not be out of place in this company despite the relatively short gap between races.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Caught U Sleeping is gorgeous. We thought she was good from the time we got her. She's a little bit fizzy and I was a bit afraid that first time out she might boil up a bit too much but she didn't at all; she was very relaxed. I'd say she will go forward. Dylan thinks she will and I think she will, from what I have seen from her at home. I would say we'll probably go looking for black type with her - Noel Meade, trainer.

runners.breeder: Ecurie Des Monceaux & Skymarc Farm Inc
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
runners.comment: Defied market weakness to win on her debut, superb pedigree, potentially very smart
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly cost E1,700,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to eight winners, including Magic Wand (Ribblesdale and Australian Group 1), and Chicquita (Irish Oaks); made a pleasing debut when beating stablemate and favourite Minerva at the Curragh; an element of doubt is introduced by Minerva's eclipse behind Kensington Lane at Gowran.

quotes.race: Ballylinch Stud Irish EBF Ingabelle Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: She was a little impatient in the stalls and Diamond Necklace didn't jump out properly and after that I had to change my plans as a I was quite far back. She responded really well when I asked her to quicken, she does it so easy. It would be lovely to see her on Arc weekend- Christophe Soumillon, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

quotes.race: Grand Hotel Malahide Irish EBF Fillies Juvenile Race
quotes.quote: Diamond Necklace is a lovely filly with a great pedigree. Her dad didn't win a maiden for another three weeks so that's St Mark's Basilica for you. He went on to win the Dewhurst and how he improved from two to three was incredible - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

runners.comment: The only filly in the field with no previous experience, huge task in the circumstances
runners.spotlight: sixth foal; half-sister to two winners; dam 1m2f winner; half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner James Garfield; the only filly in the field with no previous experience; huge task in the circumstances.

runners.comment: Has improved with experience, opened her account on the fifth attempt,  extra needed now
runners.spotlight: Has improved with experience; was having her third outing when second to the subsequent Ballyhane Stakes runner-up at Down Royal; made most before being overhauled late by subsequent Group 2 runner-up Geryon at the Curragh; relegated the disappointing Minerva to fourth when winning readily at Gowran.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: Kensington Lane been improving slowly. Her form is rock-solid. At Down Royal she had a good look when she got there and was just collared by one of Joseph O'Brien's, who has won twice since. We've always liked her and I'd say there's a little bit more improvement in her - Gavin Ryan, jockey.

runners.comment: Slowly away before running on to some effect on debut over C&D, takes a big jump in class
runners.spotlight: Slowly away before running on to some effect into fourth on debut over C&D last month; form took a knock with the defeat of runner-up Josh's Joy at Cork on Wednesday.

runners.comment: Looked raw when winning first time out at Galway, the value of the form is questionable
runners.spotlight: Looked raw first time at Galway and deserves plenty of credit for getting the better of the twice-raced runner-up; has the makings of a long-term prospect, but the form is of questionable value at this level, since the second was a beaten favourite from a mark of 79 in a Killarney nursery on her next start.

quotes.race: James's Gate Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Mighty Danu was very green but we liked her and she went to the line well. She is a big, tall filly and there is plenty of improvement in her. She is going to make a nice three-year-old. I thought she had a place chance, I knew she was going to be very green, I wasn't sure that she was going to be street wise enough to win. Gary [Carroll] gave her a nice ride and gave her a chance. I am not sure where she will go next, possibly a winners race or she could be sold as well - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

runners.comment: Struggled in a C&D Group 3 in July, in receipt of 8lb when second at Roscommon, tough task
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden winner over 1m, rated 87 after four runs; finished last of five behind Composing in a C&D Group 3 in July; more like it when second at Roscommon, though she was getting 8lb from the winner.

runners.comment: Fair fourth behind an Aidan O'Brien-trained winner at Goodwood, yet to race on soft
runners.spotlight: Overcame trouble to win on debut over 6f on good to firm at Newbury in June; never-dangerous seventh when an outsider 6f Ascot Group 3; got closer when fourth of seven behind the Aidan O'Brien-trained Precise in a 7f Group 3 at Goodwood.

stable_tour.quote: She's a lovely filly who's owned by Opulence Thoroughbreds. She's gone through a little bit of a growth period during the last month but she's shown me a lot of speed early. She's my first Havana Grey and looks to possess quite a bit of ability. We're excited for her this summer. 02-04-25

runners.comment: Workmanlike when landing short odds at Lingfield, takes a big jump in class, tongue-tie
runners.spotlight: Bigger price of her trainer's pair on debut at Fairyhouse; shaped with some promise and built on that when a clear second at this venue; only workmanlike when landing short odds at Lingfield; takes a big jump in class with a tongue-tie fitted.

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
runners.comment: No sign that she can match the ability shown by stablemate Diamond Necklace
runners.spotlight: Apprentice-ridden second string first time out over 1m here in July; second and third have let the form down, and she failed to improve when unplaced over C&D on second start; no sign that she can match the ability shown by stablemate Diamond Necklace.

racecards.verdict: It appears that DIAMOND NECKLACE was more forward than anticipated when winning at the Curragh and there should be better to come
racecards.betting_forecast: 10/11 Diamond Necklace, 11/2 Sukanya, 8/1 Caught U Sleeping, 10/1 Kensington Lane,  Mighty Danu, 12/1 Brownstown,  Red Autumn, 16/1 Killashee Warrior, 20/1 Teewinot,  Venosa, 50/1 Inbox

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Sovereign Path Handicap (Premier Handicap)

quotes.quote: You don't expect to be winning these races with the penalties. We were a bit disappointed with Mutasarref last time at the Curragh, but that's the Curragh. I don't think he's ever run his best race there but he does it here. You think the penalties are going to stop him and he just keeps doing things like that. Horses like him make you enjoy the game. The plan is to come back here on Champions Weekend for the Group 2 Boomerang Stakes, which is beyond him, but it will give us another day out - Ger Lyons, trainer.
Mutasarref came back from an injury and as Colin said he's back to himself. That's his grade and seven furlongs is his trip. A mile is just a tad beyond him - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Bahrain Turf Club Knockaire Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Mutasarref has been on the wane all year but the ground here helped his old injuries and his old bones. He has been a very lucky horse for us and on his day he is still a good horse - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Hollywoodbets We're Painting Ireland Purple Handicap
quotes.quote: Mutasarref is a stakes horse in a handicap. I don't know if he's a Group 3 but he's definitely a Listed. We'll go to Champions Weekend with him now - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Jet & Peggy O'Carroll Memorial (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Mutasarref looked like he should go and win today. We'll look for a handicap for him. We thought he would handle the ground well and he did - Kris Weld, assistant trainer.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.owner: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Sovereign Path Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: When the draw came out 20 I said 'oh no', Our minds were made up that we had to drop in. The pace was strong, one of the fractions was 10 seconds. Maybe the couple of front-runners holding up earlier in the day encouraged the lads to go forward. It suited Dance Night Andday and Ronnie was unbelievable on her - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies & Mares Handicap
quotes.quote: Dance Night Andday cdame out of Naas really well. She's an improver and a very relaxed filly. She just pricked her ears and idled when she hit the front. We will probably dip our toe in Listed company now and see where we are - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer

quotes.race: Whistlin' Donkeys Playing Live After Racing Handicap
quotes.quote: Dance Night Andday ran well in her maidens. She worked well during the week and we thought she would run a nice race - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer

quotes.race: COLM QUINN BMW Mile Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Dunum comes alive at this track and Seamie [Heffernan] gave him a peach of a ride. He usually jumps smart and Seamie didn't want to give away his position. The horse enjoyed himself, he loves the downhill into the dip and the uphill finish. He battled well, it's brilliant. We'll see how he is later in the week and decide whether he runs in the Ahonoora on Sunday so we might see him back here. He's a Galway specialist - Natalia Lupini, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF "Ahonoora" Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: I was probably in front long enough, Dunum wasn't doing a whole lot, but, as big handicaps like that go, it was plain sailing. I could hear plenty of shouting behind me and I could tell a few might be getting boxed in, so I said I would commit and take a couple of lengths out of them. It worked. He gets a mile well, I just didn't think they went quick enough for him here on Tuesday and he was a bit keen. Even today he grabbed hold of it coming down the hill, but he had something in front of him just to keep him settled. He's a very uncomplicated horse, he just needs a good gallop in front - Billy Lee, jockey.

quotes.race: Hollywoodbets We're Here To Take Over Handicap
quotes.quote: Dunum was in good form so we decided to enter him. It's great to have a runner here let alone a winner. He had improved at home and we thought we had a bit more in hand today with the good draw - Natalia Lupini, trainer.

quotes.race: Gowran Festival Of Speed August 21st Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: Dunum likes it around here and Wayne gave him a powerful ride. He likes some ease in the ground and we can aim for Galway - Natalia Lupini, trainer

runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

quotes.quote: Mississippi River always runs up to a nice mark. Seven is a lovely trip for him, he is in the handicap at Leopardstown next Saturday, so we will see how he comes out of it, if not, we will find another little race for him and probably try him in stakes company from seven to a mile. He'll have a winner's penalty, but in fairness, he has good form around Leopardstown and it is a good pot so we'll see how he is - Chris Armstrong, Aidan O'Brien's stable representative.

stable_tour.quote: It was a shame not to get it done in the Victoria Cup but he's had a look at the track. He's gone up 3lb for the good run in third but it looks the ideal prep for this one. The style of the race suits a hold-up horse as there should be plenty of pace to aim at and he ticks a lot of boxes. He's a big bullet to fire at the meeting for us in our first year training.  13-06-25

quotes.quote: Hurricane Ivor loved it. I was a little worried about the ground but he was great. It's the first time he has won over seven - Jessica Harrington, trainer

quotes.race: Dubai International Airport World Trophy Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Hurricane Ivor could go to France, you never know, he's done nothing but improve. He takes a while to get going but this was half a furlong shorter so he's done well to win. He's a lovely, straightforward horse and just does what you ask him to do - not much more, though! - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

That was great, Hurricane Ivor is such a star. He's a charming horse who is such a pleasure to have around the yard and he's done nothing but improve all year. The trip suited him and so did a bit of dig in the ground. I don't think he'll run in the Ayr Gold Cup. We'll probably look at a black type race, it was a good performance under top weight today and he deserves it - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

Hurricane Ivor has done very well. He ran poorly first time but had been training much better since and this was a little bit lower grade. He needed every yard here, and a flat six or a stiff five will be his game. I thought the ground might be too soft for him today, but it wasn't. I'm sure there will be an appeal from some quarter, probably from us. Most people seem to think we won the race but it's pretty impossible to know, really - William Haggas, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a star. He had two quick runs at the end of March and we gave him a small break afterwards. He never lets us down and at the age of eight he seems to be getting better. He runs at Tipperary on Thursday and the conditions of that race look perfect for him. So does the ground. 23-04-25

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Derby Festival Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Mick [Halford] recommended us and I've known Paul Rooney for a long time. He's one of the biggest gentlemen in racing. Slieve Binnian ran really well on Guineas weekend and we put him away for this and it came together. He's a lovely horse and could be a Galway Mile horse and will need to go up a few pounds to get in, so we'll see what the handicapper does with him. I'd like to have a go at Galway. He hasn't had many goes at a mile. He took a while to get going the last day but came home well and sharpened up a lot from it - David Marnane, trainer.

quotes.race: Gateway Hotel Dundalk Race & Stay Handicap
quotes.quote: Slieve Binnian did that well. He jumped and travelled and hit the line well, he is definitely on the up. He jumps quick and you can put him anywhere in a race - Ronan Whelan, rider.
Slieve Binnian was in such good form all week that I let him take his chance. He's a lovely, gebuine horse and that's a good trip for him - Michael Halford, joint trainer

runners.owner: Mrs H M Keaveney & Mark Keaveney

prev_owners.owner: Mrs H M Keaveney & M Keaveney

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Derby Festival Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Blues Emperor is very brave and when he gets his head in front he is hard to get past.

quotes.race: Tote Always SP Or Better Handicap
quotes.quote: Blues Emperor is a bonny little horse. We wondered would he handle this better ground but he is very tough and genuine. Hopefully he will make into a Premier Handicap horse - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

Blues Emperor has probably done nothing but improve. He ran well at Dundalk and very well at Cork the last day. Ben (Coen) said he got into a pocket but he's very genuine, he tried his hardest when he got into the gap. We'll probably give him one more outing this season - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

quotes.quote: Chicago Fireball is a versatile horse. I probably asked him to pick up soon enough but I was not expecting him to pick up as well as he did. He's an improving horse - Ben Coen, rider.

Daamberdiplomat has been very consistent all year. I thought he ran well at Fairyhouse last time. Ben [Coen, jockey] rode him well today. I think it was the ride that won the race. He got him in a lovely position. We put the cheekpieces on him just to give him that extra bit of help. He's very competitive off 87 but when he goes to 93 or 94, he needs to run five more times to get back to 87. So there is no secret about him. He was on his winning mark today and I think it was the ride that won it - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

quotes.race: Christmas Party Nights At Dundalk Stadium Handicap
quotes.quote: Six furlongs and coming off a good gallop on this surface really suits DAAMBERDIPLOMAT. He does get seven but a proper strongly-run six is ideal for him. He's good fresh so we liked his chance coming here this evening - Ben Coen, rider

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I did not feel Daamberdiplomat had progressed at home but that was pretty impressive. He settled better dropping back in trip, I am not sure where we will go next but we will be in no rush. We might look at the Killavullan later in the year - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

Noble Truth was much more professional, he has so much energy and he managed to channel it in the right direction. He has lots of speed and today I was able to ride him a little bit, whereas in the past I've been a passenger on him. I wanted to go right on the stands' rail but he was looking at the crowd and shying away from it a little. Frankie [Dettori] came upsides on my inside and headed me but that really helped my lad really get motivated - William Buick, rider.

quotes.race: Betfair King Charles II Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Noble Truth is a bit of handful in the mornings and I think Nathan who rides him out is glad he's running to give his arms a day off - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

It's nice to get Noble Truth back on track. He won nicely at Newmarket and disappointed last time for no apparent reason. He settled nicely today and I'm pleased with him. I just had a quick chat with Charlie and there are no immediate plans but we'll stick to seven furlongs - Alex Merriam, assistant trainer.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Bloodstock Pro British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Once Noble Truth hit the rising ground, he finished strongly. We don't have any immediate plans as he's been quite headstrong at home and we're taking each race to see which way he will go. This is traditionally a strong race. We might look to potentially work back from something like the Champagne Stakes in September, which will give him some time to hopefully show us the right things. I think we'll see him over a mile by the end of the season. He's a nice, scopey horse and could be one for the Vertem Futurity Trophy - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A horse with a good deal of talent who we bought last summer. He's a very hot character who pulls hard in his work and in his races - it's a case of keeping a lid on him. He trains on his own at home but he's been grand. He has some good form as he was second in the Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere and won the Jersey Stakes the following year. He's been off the track for a while now and is nearly ready. His trip is probably seven furlongs but I'd like to start over six to blow the cobwebs away. 19-04-24

prev_owners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Orchidaceae won well at the Curragh so we were quite hopeful coming here. Everything went to plan and it was very straightforward. She loves that ground and we will have to look at stepping her up in grade - Andy Oliver, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Orchidaceae is one that Andy liked and he thought if I got a bit of cover today she might come home well. I would have no problem going another furlong - Andy Slattery, rider

runners.owner: Teme Valley & Newsells Park Stud Ltd

quotes.quote: He travelled well, quickened up really well and had a little look when I got there but Tribal Nation is a gorgeous horse. I was a bit far back the last day in Gowran and had a lot of ground to make up but coming back to seven furlongs suited him today. He showed a good attitude on the ground in the last furlong and I think he is a nice horse who will only get better - Dylan Browne McMonagle, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: He had a good third at Naas most recently and should love the straight mile in the Britannia. 13-06-25

prev_owners.owner: M O'Callaghan Racing Limited, J Fleming & M O'Callaghan

quotes.quote: I Am Superman hated the ground, just being a big strong horse he was able to get through it and get home. He is a very good horse on his day, and the end of last year I had the Buckingham Palace in my head. He is a Group horse and a straight seven at Ascot on fast ground where they go a lick will suit him - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

quotes.race: Family Fun Day August Bank Holiday Race
quotes.quote: It was a bit of a confidence booster for the horse and a bit of a relief for myself, because we know he has the ability - he has loads of talent - but at Ascot the whole occasion got to him and he raced too enthusiastically. He blew it by half way, whereas today they went a real good gallop and he travelled well. He did it very professionally. He raced a little bit in Leigh's hands early but he just needs to learn to race a little more - he's still learning. There's plenty more to come from him, he's just gone through the motions in what looked a good enough race on paper. After Ascot, today was about picking up the pieces and we'll figure out a plan from here - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer of I Am Superman

I Am Superman is a horse we always thought a lot of and he's making shapes of being a very nice horse. He made them look like handicappers. We might run him in the Tetrarch and if he came through that we can think about running in the Irish 2000 Guineas - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

I thought that was good performance carrying top weight and from that draw. He wasn't the quickest away but Leigh kept him out wide on the fresh ground down the back and at the apex of the bend was able to slot in in front and set his own fractions. He won well and he said he was just idling in front. I'm delighted with him and delighted that he's come good because we did think he was a nice horse last year. Hopefully he can progress well - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer

quotes.race: Gateway Hotel Dundalk Race & Stay Maiden
quotes.quote: Pier Pressure travelled into it nicely, He was still green and had a good look when he got there but he won snug enough - Jack Kearney, rider

We were very confident coming down of a good run. I'm a restricted trainer and have had my licence four years. Summer Snow has been tearing up the gallops at home and to win, you can't expect it up against the big guns. I've always been breaking and pretraining and giving away my horses. I just had too many and couldn't give them away. Pat [Kelly, owner] gave her to me when she was rated 68 and she was rated 81 today. She's going through the ranks - Peter Lawlor, trainer.

That's Summer Snow's second win for me now and we'll see what the handicapper gives us. I'd say we will still be in the 70s and I might look at Derby weekend, where there's a 0-80 for her. We'll see how she comes out of this - Peter Lawlor, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Extra Places Every Day Handicap
quotes.quote: I've won on Glenfinnan before so was probably entitled to have another go on him. He did well to finish off like that considering that he was a bit keen through the race. I'd say a stiff six would be fine but he did get the seven well. The ground not being too fast probably helped him - Oisin Murphy, rider.

Today worked out well on Glenfinnan, who has plenty of pace. He enjoyed travelling quite strongly and, when I switched out, it always felt he'd hit the line in front. He galloped out strongly and I hope he can finish the season on a high. The stiff 7f probably suited, but I don't know what races Andrew has in mind for him - Oisin Murphy, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He ran really well in the Wokingham, where he finished fifth behind Unequal Love. We thought he was worth perservering with over 6f, but at Goodwood in the Stewards' Cup last time the track was probably on the quick side for him and he finished well down the field. He's by Harry Angel and probably a little bit of cut would do him good. It may also have come too quickly for him after the Wokingham - he had another run at Ascot in between too. He seems to be coming back to form now, though. He's entered in the Ayr Gold Cup but we might give him a run over 7f first, possibly at Thirsk on Friday. 28-08-24

racecards.verdict: Johnny Murtagh holds a strong hand, CHICAGO FIREBALL narrowly preferred to the top-weighted Rahmi
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/2 Gleneagle Bay, 13/2 Rahmi, 15/2 Dunum,  Chicago Fireball, 8/1 Dance Night Andday, 10/1 Hurricane Ivor, 12/1 One Smack Mac,  Slieve Binnian,  Tribal Nation,  Pier Pressure,  Nakasero, 14/1 Blues Emperor,  Glenfinnan, 16/1 I Am Superman, 20/1 Daamberdiplomat, 25/1 Noble Truth

racecards.race_name: KPMG Champions Juvenile Stakes (Group 2)

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: We thought A Boy Named Susie was good as he did a really, really good piece of work up the Curragh a few weeks ago. This auction race looked a tough ask as he was giving away weight to winners and it is a tough track first-time-out but he is obviously just smart. For an auction race at Killarney, it has thrown up Iridessa, Luxembourg, Lambourn and a few Group 2 winners as well so it's a nice start for him. They hacked and then sprinted but I think he could be a proper one. He has plenty of gears for a big horse - Donnacha O'Brien, trainer.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.quote: Action is quite a big horse, he stays well and ideally I would have loved to have gone a stride quicker but he was having such a look in front I was letting him find his feet and get comfortable. I thought the last 50 yards he hit the line well. He is going to learn plenty from today - Wayne Lordan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

Benvenuto Cellini is a horse that stepped forward and riding him around there in front he was having a good look. He got a little bit lonely in the last furlong and was half leaning off the rail, but he's a very nice colt. I think mentally he's going to improve, he's just a little bit immature. He's a nice colt for us as the year goes on. He feels like a horse who is going to stay well. He was only doing his own thing out front but when I got after him he quickened and he's going to stay, so that will bring a bit of class into him when he goes up in trip - Wayne Lordan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: With that run under his belt. Benevenuto Cellini will be a very hot favourite in a maiden next time - Aidan O'Brien, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

prev_owners.owner: A K Whelan & Reeves Thoroughbred Racing

racecards.verdict: All five trained by the O'Briens, with BENVENUTO CELLINI taken to repel stablemate Montreal
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Benvenuto Cellini, 2/1 Montreal, 11/2 Hardy Warrior, 13/2 A Boy Named Susie, 25/1 Nil Bua Gan Dua

racecards.race_name: CMG Group Stakes (Group 3)

quotes.quote: Al Aasy is terrific isn't he? He's pretty reliable in this grade when things set up like that. They didn't go very fast today - he usually likes a stronger pace - but they picked up well up the straight and he found a gear. He actually ran on strongly today - he usually wins by half a length or a neck. He's been called some names in the past but I wish people would respect him for what he is - he's a very talented horse but you just need to wait, and wait and wait. I think he enjoyed a bit of cut in the ground today as well - William Haggas, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

We knew Al Aasy was in good form and he has looked great all winter. He is paying us back for looking after him in his earlier years and is just a lovely horse to have about. I think Jim [Crowley, jockey] has really got the hang of him by just waiting and waiting and the mile and a quarter pace suited him better. I was worried he might be too fresh but Jim gave him a beautiful ride. I think a both a stiff mile and a quarter and a mile and a half suit him. We'll look around and find something for him - Maureen Haggas, trainer's assistant.

Al Aasy has always been talented and has probably been better since being gelded. It was great for him there, it was easy. He's a dude at home now. It's only four weeks since he had his first run here, with Goodwood in between, which is a big ask, especially having had some time off like he has but you couldn't have passed up an opportunity like this. He's seven-years-old and it's time to get to work really.  He enjoyed it today. It was a good pace, which he enjoys. He's run over all sorts of trips, from a mile and a quarter up to a mile and five furlongs. He likes a good pace. He used to be very keen at home. He's not as keen but can still be strong when he wants to - Maureen Haggas, wife of trainer William.
