It was a messy race. We went very steady. I got a bit closer at the top of the hill and then I was quite happy when he was in a pocket. William [Haggas, trainer] said to ride him for luck and, if it opens up, it opens up. We just needed that gap and I was very fortunate to have got up the rail. There wasn't a lot of room up the rail but Al Aasy was very brave. Once he was in there, it was game over really. He's just as good over this trip as he is over a mile and a half. He's been a bit unlucky because that's his run style. He's been called a few names in the past for not being a battler. He's just one of those horses who has to be produced at the last minute - Jim Crowley, jockey.

Al Aasy has always been a talented horse and bringing him back to a mile and a quarter has been a good thing, he gets the trip well and he travels really well. He looked as though he was enjoying himself today. The horse has been unfairly criticised, since he's been gelded we've never really classed him as ungenuine, he's been unfortunate once or twice. Although he's old he's a bit like Hamish, there aren't many miles on the clock - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

quotes.race: bet365 Stakes (Registered As The Steventon Stakes) (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: That's very satisfying for everyone involved with Al Aasy. They badgered me to run him over a mile and a quarter. I wouldn't do it but I finally succumbed and clearly, now aged six, that's his trip. They go a bit faster and that helps him to relax. He did it comfortably today. Physically, he's been a battle, and mentally he's not straightforward either. He's had a couple of injuries but he's coming. He still doesn't look right in his skin but he's coming. He'll go for the Rose of Lancaster at Haydock, a Group 3 over a mile and a quarter on August 12, next - William Haggas, trainer.

You could say that Al Aasy should have won the way he won because he's only just been beaten in a Group 1, but I thought Dane [O'Neill, jockey] gave him a brilliant ride and he did it nicely. He had a nice, easy run around and it'll have done his confidence the world of good because that's what he needs. That's half the battle with him really because he's got stacks of talent. In terms of where we go next, that'll be a discussion we have but he's got options - Maureen Haggas, trainer's wife.

quotes.race: Qipco Champion Stakes (British Champions Middle Distance) (Group 1)
quotes.quote: I'm sorry that Al Aasy never ran at all - William Haggas, trainer.

quotes.race: Al Rayyan Stakes (Registered As The Aston Park Stakes) (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Al Aasy won very well and liked the ground. He settled well and I can't really say anything else because he looks like that at home. He's a talented horse. He's done well this year and we'll find out in the Coronation Cup, which is where he'll run next. He's in everything and we've put him in the Arc, but that's a long way to go. I fancy a mile-two, but I'm not sure, and there are some fantastic races at a mile and a half. If he's going to run in the Arc he'll need a break and I wasn't planning to run him after the John Porter until Epsom, but he was too well - William Haggas, trainer.

William has always liked Al Aasy - he's a beautiful horse, and he's bred to be a good horse. He ran really well first time last year and we were really disappointed when he ran like he did at Newmarket and we don't know why, perhaps it was rustiness. He did what he needed to do last time and he's improving and learning. When he won last time this was the race William wanted to go for, but I don't know what's next. I'd think a well-run mile and a half would suit him best, he's not slow, and ideally faster ground than today. I think the Great Voltigeur would be the likely next step - Maureen Haggas, wife of William Haggas, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Mr & Mrs H Morriss & Miss K Rausing
Mr & Mrs H Morriss & Miss K Rausing

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boost Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was expecting Sons And Lovers to finish in the first three. He is a lovely horse. He is still a bit raw and he has got bit of maturing to do. He is a lovely mover but I was worried the ground might be too quick - Jane Chapple-Hyam, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's had two really good runs for us. We've been working towards the Wolferton Stakes and he's had a good preparation. 13-06-25

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai International Stakes In Honour Of Kevin Prendergast (Group 3)
quotes.quote: You can set your clock by Trustyourinstinct. He turns up and runs his race everyday. He is a multiple stakes winner and he loves the Curragh. He won at the track on Guineas weekend and he backed it up here up in class and down in trip with a penalty. It wasn't an easy task today. I thought he was impressive and I think that run is right up there with his best. When he won the last day he needed to fight  out the finish but today he showed a turn of foot off the turn and Dylan [Browne McMonagle, jockey] said he felt really sharp off the bend. It was a smart performance and he is going the right way.   I imagine the horse will travel somewhere in the Autumn whether it is to Kentucky for the mile and half, million and half dollar race or to Australia. To be quite honest a horse like him has loads of options for good pots and he's a gelding so we will be looking all the big prize money options in the Autumn. He won over hurdles but he wasn't the most natural jumper. He has really found his niche now - Joseph O'Brient, trainer.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Orby Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Trustyourinstinct is a lovely horse, very consistent and loves it here at the Curragh. I'm delighted to have a nice winner for JP. He's a home-bred of JP and Noreen's and everybody will get an extra kick out of that. He always turns up and runs his race. He was behind Los Angeles here the last day, it was a very credible run, and dropping down into Listed class today he's won impressively. He's well enough handicapped over hurdles but he doesn't really jump with great fluency. He'll probably stick to the Flat. He's a very solid Listed/Group 3 horse and he pays his way every year. We've ran him internationally a few times as well and we might look at that again at a later date. He particularly loves the Curragh and it's not a bad place to have a rapport with. - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

We weren't sure about the mile-and-a-half trip as his best runs had been at ten but Trustyourinstinct really got the trip well. He qualifies for a lot of big pots around the world as we get into the autumn so he could potentially end up in a race internationally - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

Trustyourinstinct was a classy enough horse on the flat and it's probably taken him a while to get his act together over hurdles. It was a weak race and so we just kept it simple - Simon Torrens, rider

quotes.race: Bet Builder At Novibet Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Trustyourinstinct looks a nice horse. We thought he'd run well and he'll have learned a lot. He was green in the middle of the race there but I thought he went to the line well. It was a good performance on debut - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He showed some really good form last year, winning a Group 3 at Leopardstown over a mile and a half and just touched off in a Group 2 in Italy in October. We were happy with his run when third in the Devoy Stakes, but the set-up of the race wasn't ideal for him. Depending on ground, he could go for the Mooresbridge next. 24-04-25
He won two races on the Flat for us and he will start off in the next month in a juvenile hurdle. We're looking forward to getting him out over hurdles and his schooling has been good. 27-11-23

prev_owners.owner: Mrs J S Bolger & Frederik Tylicki

quotes.race: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Declan [McDonagh] had Fleetfoot in a lovely position at the top of the hill and he hit the line hard today. That's what he's showing us at home all the time. He's in the July Sale next week and that is where he will go next - Ger Flynn, trainer's representative.

quotes.race: Punchestown New Track On Track Race
quotes.quote: Fleetfoot hit a little bit of a flat spot early in the straight but Rory always thought he would get there. The ground is probably a little bit holding for him. We like him a lot and he is going the right way - Ger Flynn, travelling head groom
Fleetfoot had good form last year, he got mugged by some very good horses, but he is a better three-year-old. Hopefully he will be back here in September - Jim Bolger, trainer.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.race: TOTE Irish EBF Devoy Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Sunchart has never run a bad race at this track so it's a pity there's not more races like this here and he loves an ease in the ground. It's great to have a servant like him. It took him a long time to win a race and he's now won two Listed races one year after the other. We might go travelling this year, somewhere like Germany where he'll get soft ground over a mile and a quarter or mile and a half. We're going to have to as he's an eight-year-old now. He could go back over hurdles and the ground suits him over hurdles as it's watered - Andy Slattery, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Devoy Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Sunchart deserved that more than any of us. Pat has kept persevering with him. He is a hard horse to place but he likes this track and likes soft ground. It was a good opportunity, he had the highest rating - Andy Slattery, trainer.

quotes.race: Athassel House Stud Irish EBF Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Ben said he improved from Listowel and he'll improve again as he was green enough. He was green enough in the parade ring today as well and I think we'll give him another run in the Eyrefield Stakes. He has a fancy entry in the Epsom Derby next year and we think a good bit of him. He'll be a better three-year-old and I just think he'll need another run. The Eyrefield will give him a bit more education and I think he'll need it - Andy Slattery, trainer

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

quotes.race: Kara Quinn Memorial Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Acapulco Bay was very green in front. Ryan said he never picked up the bridle at all and felt he was in second gear - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well when second at Naas over a mile last month and should come forward for that run. He'll definitely get a mile and a quarter and could get further. 07-04-25

quotes.quote: Convergent has run a lovely race and is very much a work in progress. He's a horse for next season as well as this one. He's been running over shorter trips and was keen over the first couple of furlongs, then he got into a rhythm. Turning in I thought we'd win, before I thought we'd get run out of it, and then he's stayed on again. We know he stays a mile and a half well now and we're very happy for the future. I couldn't be happier without winning - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: I was pleased with his first run in Group company at Chester. He ended up making the running, which wasn't ideal, but stayed on strongly. He'll get an entry in the Hampton Court but is more likely for the King Edward VII over a mile and a half. I rate him highly but he's very much one for the future and he wouldn't want the ground too quick.  12-06-25

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

quotes.race: Boodles Raindance Dee Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Ryan [Moore] gave  Mount Kilimanjaro a great ride, he was very patient. The pace was very honest and he didn't panic and the horse quickened up well and showed a nice attitude. He'll come forward from this and we're delighted with him. A Derby of some sorts - an Epsom Derby or the French Derby - are the options for him. We'll see how the trials go and where they'll all go - Paul Smith, representing owners Coolmore.

Mount Kilimanjaro ran a lovely race, I was delighted with him. We knew after Longchamp he got the trip well and Ryan said he could be a French Derby horse so we thought we'd let him take his chance in a Group 1 - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had some very good runs last season and won well in France before finishing second in the Group 1 at Saint-Cloud. We might run him in one of those trials at Chester. He was beaten at Dundalk on his return but we felt we might have started him a little too early. He looks like he'll get a mile and a quarter well and handles an ease in the ground. 07-04-25

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

stable_tour.quote: I was very happy with his run last time. I was disappointed to lose it in the stewards' room but he deserves his chance now. The Hampton Court Stakes trip will suit him.  13-06-25

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Stay True always looked like a lovely Leger horse; you'd be happy with that - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Neville O'Byrne Memorial Irish EBF Fillies & Mares Handicap
quotes.quote: She's very nice and is all about next year. I was happy to see some rain last night and I think It's A Heartbeat will be better on easier. There is a race in France that we might look at over a mile and a half and I have her in the Princess Royal at Newmarket. I think the track would really suit her there, a nice even pace and come into it at the end. There are also plenty of options here - John O'Donoghue, trainer

racecards.verdict: Last year's winner Trustyourinstinct is respected but AL AASY (nap) looks a tough nut to crack
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Al Aasy, 7/2 Convergent, 5/1 Trustyourinstinct, 13/2 Sons And Lovers, 12/1 Sunchart, 16/1 Omni Man,  Reyenzi, 20/1 It's A Heartbeat, 25/1 Fleetfoot,  Acapulco Bay, 50/1 Mo Ghille Mar

racecards.race_name: Coolmore America 'Justify' Matron Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)

quotes.race: Balanchine Presented By DP World (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Today there was much more pace early on. I was happy to sit in just behind the leaders. When we hit the straight, I asked Choisya to pick up and she did so very well. It's always a huge advantage for jockeys when horses can be in different positions and today she won because she's very versatile - Mickael Barzalona, jockey.

quotes.race: Cape Verdi Stakes (Sponsored by Al Tayer Motors) (Group 2) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
quotes.quote: I got an easy lead in front; it felt like a French race. When I asked Choisya to pick up she did it very well. It would have been better to have a lead but all of the others had the instructions to take back; sometimes it is lucky to have horses who are versatile like she is - Mickael Barzalona, jockey.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Dick Hern Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Choisya did a lovely piece of work the other day and she's brought that here. She went through the gears really well and she travelled great. The good gallop in the early part of the race definitely helped her out, she relaxed well. I was delighted with her performance - Harry Davies, jockey.

Lovely run, drawn wide so switched tactics up a little bit today and dropped her in a bit, which seemed to work really nicely. We went an even gallop. There was a bit of scrimmaging in front of me and I managed to stay clear of that. Choisya got there quite easily. She's an improving filly, she's tough. We weren't sure about the ground, but she clearly went on it - Jack Mitchell, jockey.

quotes.race: Betting Better With Sky Bet Sankey Handicap
quotes.quote: It was a bit difficult as Choisya was hanging into the other horse. She didn't handle Goodwood last time and the return to a flat, galloping track worked a treat - Harry Burns, rider.

quotes.race: Prix Rothschild - Fonds Europeen de l'Elevage (Group 1) (3yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Straight Course) (Tu
quotes.quote: I'm absolutely delighted to have a Group 1 winner for Wathnan, they've been huge supporters and long may it continue. I'm delighted for Danny [Tudhope] as well. I was a little surprised James [Doyle] went the way he did [riding fellow Wathnan runner Crimson Advocate]. I know Crimson Advocate beat us at Ascot but we needed that bit better ground and the cheekpieces made a bit of difference. Also, Fallen Angel's work has taken a step forward since Ascot and especially over the last two weeks. I wouldn't say we were confident but we were bullish going there. She doesn't want any firmness in it like she had at Ascot where, coming round the bend, her head just came up a bit. She's such an honest, game mare and she was battling back in the last 100 yards then, as she did today. She only does what you ask her to do and Aidan's filly coming to her helped her. What I'm really pleased about is that she's emulated Laurens in winning a Group 1 at two, three and four now. The Matron I would imagine is the next port of call and hopefully we'll have some nice ground at that time of year. There is the mile and a quarter Prix Jean Romanet but we'll enjoy today, get her home and see how she is, and then I'll speak to connections, but the way she's won that I'd be happy to stick at a mile - Karl Burke, trainer. Fallen Angel is a superstar. I haven't ridden her since last year when we won the Irish 1,000 Guineas. She means a lot to me and the team as well, that's three Group 1s now as a two-year-old, three-year-old and now as a four-year-old. It's amazing and I have to thank the Wathnan team for giving me the chance to get back on her. She has a good mind on her, a great mentality. Karl [Burke, trainer] always says she never shows a lot at home but she obviously saves it for the track and it's a great day for us all. We went quick. My plan was always to go forwards because she's a straightforward filly. She'd the cheekpieces on today and was a bit lit up in them but she wore them the last day. I thought she was doing a bit too much in my hands but I've always thought she's a filly you couldn't go quick enough on and she's toughed it out. She had to be tough, they were coming at her and it got close at the line, we got headed at one point but she came back. She's a tough filly. She dug deep and got the result and we know the softer the better for her, but she tries so hard every time. She's a superstar - Danny Tudhope, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

Absolutely delighted with Fallen Angel. The ground was probably a little bit quicker than ideal, but I was so pleased with what she did in the last 50 yards - she battled on, and she's just a superstar. I think in the autumn when we get a bit of ease in the ground, she'll be back into a big one for us. We might go up in trip - it will be somewhere between a mile and a mile and a quarter, but ease in the ground is going to be important - Richard Brown, owners' representative.

We were disappointed with the Guineas. We sent Fallen Angel to Newmarket fit and she didn't blow that much afterwards. The ground maybe was a bit quick on the undulations, but that's looking for excuses, we were beaten fair and square on the day. She just sparked up in the last week, and physically she looked fantastic today - Karl Burke, trainer.

Fallen Angel jumped great and is a very straightforward ride. You can put her where you want in a race, she stays very well and I thought the last furlong would be her best furlong - Danny Tudhope, rider.

We were very confident coming here today. Fallen Angel is so laidback and travelled so well in the race. She hit the line strong and will go another furlong no problem. We've always liked her at home and she's quite versatile as regards ground - Daniel Tudhope, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: We went to the Lockinge with the expectation that she'd improve for the run and were delighted with how she performed. She's come out of it really well and is bang on target for the Duke of Cambridge. She's definitely sharpened up and you'd hope she'd be the one to beat. 12-06-25

quotes.race: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: It is great to come here and win at the Curragh again. One Look seems to like it here. She is a lovely filly. Ascot didn't work out for her on ground quicker than she would like and a trip as short as she would want to go. She had ran this sort of distance last year at Gowran and we felt on pedigree that stepping her up would suit her. She was brave today, she didn't have the easiest passage and she won nicely at the death. Today was a sit and see. We have to step up now and look at better races. She is in the Nassau, she will be in the races on Arc weekend, she can run in the Blandford Stakes too. We will have a think and pick our spots but she will probably run more than she has in the past. We are lucky to have a good bunch of horses and have great owners -  Paddy Twomey, trainer.

quotes.race: Lodge Park Stud Irish EBF Park Express Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: She's tough and I think a mile is the minimum for now. Ten furlongs will be well within her compass. One Look will have an entry in the Lanwades Stakes. She's a Group winner now and we'll try and go as far up the ladder as we can. The ability was never in question it was just last spring she wasn't ready to do what the world expected her to do. She'll probably prove yet that she is that kind of filly - Paddy Twomey, trainer

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Race
quotes.quote: One Look did what we hoped she would do and it went to plan. I'm thrilled with the way she did it. That's her first piece of work this year. If she goes to Newmarket, she will probably go straight there - Paddy Twomey, trainer

One Look is a nice filly. I thought I would get her out earlier in the year, but it was just the way things worked out. She was just ready to run this week. I thought she was good enough to be competitive. The plan was to track Frankie (Dettori) on Cherry Blossom and see how we got on. I gave her a Guineas entry this week. It was a big performance to do that and I think that will be it for the year - Paddy Twomey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's a lovely filly and I was delighted with her at the Curragh. She only found Porta Fortuna too good there and the Duke of Cambridge has been pencilled in for her. She's smart.  13-06-25

quotes.race: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Sponsored By Oddschecker) (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Sparks Fly has run a blinder, she just didn't get out in time. She's handled the ground fine and that's the first time she's gone and got black type with good in the going description, I'm thrilled with the run. It was a real blinder and it opens up a few more options for her now. I've always thought she was a Group Three horse and she's proved it there. I thought she was the best horse in the race, but just got held up and didn't get there in time - David Loughnane, trainer.

quotes.race: Prix Isola Bella - Fonds Europeen de l'Elevage (Listed Race) (3yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
quotes.quote: We've not done our usual thing of bowling her out in front and Sparks Fly has quickened away really impressively. We've got so much in hand I think that next year's going to be a really fun year with her - Laura Pearson, jockey.

quotes.race: Robin Hood Stakes (Listed Race) (Formerly Known As The Ben Marshall Stakes)
quotes.quote: Sparks Fly is unreal. I'm so proud of her mother [Stepping Out], who has produced some amazing horses for us. She's had her, Caroline Dale and Baby Steps, and they were all very useful. We thought last time in France she could have gone a bit faster even though she was in front, so Laura decided to go forward again this time and play catch me if you can. The filly never stopped and she loved it ? she had her ears pricked the whole way around. We'll go back to Saint-Cloud for the Prix Isola Bella she won last year. It's on November 10 and she'll get her preferred soft ground there - Dave Lowe, owner.

Sparks Fly has not had the best of seasons but the ground was wrong for her at the Curragh and we had to take her to the other end of the track because she hangs and we have to do what she wants to do. This track suits her down to the ground. She has a lot of ability and a lot of spirit and, as long as we point that in the right direction, she takes us there - Laura Pearson, jockey.

quotes.race: Book Bounce Bingo At Ayr Racecourse Handicap
quotes.quote: Every time I ride Sparks Fly she seems to be coming on mentally and physically. The sky's the limit. I remember when I rode her at Wolverhampton on her first go (in a handicap) and she wasn't quite there on the all-weather, but she's really bounced on the turf. She likes the softer ground, she prefers to be swimming more than running. There's definitely more to come and I'd say she's holding a lot more under her belly - Laura Pearson, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: She was a real flag-bearer for us last year, winning eight races in all. To win her first race off 59 in May and then win a Listed race by 12 lengths and finish off on 107 was phenomenal. Once she started winning she kept pulling out more and more. She looks like she's strengthened up loads and I think we'll have a bit of fun again this year. Soft ground is paramount for her. The only bad run she had was at York and I was kicking myself for running as I walked the track and it was too quick, but we thought we have to try her on quick ground at some stage. We know now it's not her gig. As soon as she got back on to soft, she relished it again and finished off the season really strongly. We were struggling for a race to start her off this time and ended up running her over 7f at Haydock, which is too short but we needed to get a run into her. She's got loads of options but it's down to the weather. There's the Group 3 Princess Elizabeth at Epsom on Saturday and then Royal Ascot. If we don't get the ground, we'll have to go to France or Ireland. She's working the house down at home and is ready to rock and roll. 30-05-24

quotes.race: Xin Gin Irish EBF Brownstown Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: She jumped well and I controlled it, it was a steering job. It was snug, it's a long season. Vera's Secret is not short of speed, I told the owners she could drop back in trip or she could even go up to a mile. She's a good filly and a straightforward ride - Seamie Heffernan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

Vera's Secret hasn't had a lot of racing and she's a big, strong six-year-old now. She's a quality filly and proved it today. This year, since she came back into training she's been training lovely and relaxing well. Nathan Crosse was to ride her today but he had to go to Hoppegarten for Willie McCreery. He's done a lot of work with her and she's his ride so we were lucky to get Seamie today. She's entered in the Lanwades on Guineas weekend and we'll probably come back for the Matron Stakes later in the year. She's a bit different this year, more mature and she'll come on for this as she's over 600 kilos and takes plenty of work at home - John Feane, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
quotes.quote: Vera's Secret is a filly we like a lot. It's good that she did things properly today. She wasn't too keen. I think they all wanted to move to the middle and it suited her as she got a freebie and got to prick her ears - John Feane, trainer.

runners.owner: B Fowler/Medallion Racing '20/S Weston/Reeves T'bred Racing

prev_owners.owner: Barry Fowler, Medallion Racing 2020 LLC, Steven I Weston
B Fowler/Medallion Racing '20/S Weston/Reeves T'bred Racing
Barry Fowler, Medallion Racing 2020 LLC, Steven I Weston
B Fowler/Medallion Racing '20/S Weston/Reeves T'bred Racing
Barry Fowler, Medallion Racing 2020 LLC, Steven I Weston

quotes.race: Coolmore Stud Auguste Rodin Irish EBF Athasi Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: She got into that Bellewstown race because of the auction prices and missed the kick, something you shouldn't be able to do there, come from so far back. We threw Atsila in the deep end here but she's obviously a smart filly. I was half thinking of running her in a handicap but Barry (Fowler - owner) said no lets throw her into the Athasi and see where she stands. It looked a good field, fast ground and they went a good gallop so there's no fluke about it. She's quite lazy at home so hard to judge. I think she's deserved a crack at a big one - Donnacha O'Brien, trainer.

runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

quotes.quote: We're thrilled with the run. California Dreamer backed up what she'd been doing at home and got the mile really well and came on lovely from her trial run here last time - Adrian Murray, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's improving all the time and ran a fabulous race to finish second behind Lake Victoria in the Irish 1,000 Guineas. She got the trip very well but we'll drop her in class and distance for the Jersey. 13-06-25

runners.breeder: Exors Of The Late Sir Robert Ogden

quotes.race: Get Raceday Ready EBF "Newcomers" Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Horses Which Have Never Run) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Cathedral has a Moyglare entry but she won't go there as it'll come too soon. We'll make sure she comes out of the race okay but there's a few options. There's the Cheveley Park and the Oh So Sharp, but they're only being considered at the moment, nothing is certain. She's exciting for next season and it's important she gets that experience as a two-year-old - Amy Drummond, racing manager to Amo Racing.

stable_tour.quote: I took her out of the 1,000 Guineas last week as she needs a bit more time. She's a talented filly and we need to look after her. There are plenty of options further down the line over seven furlongs and a mile. She's a breeze-up graduate but she's easy to deal with. 28-04-25

prev_owners.owner: Shane R Stafford/J C Fleming/Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd
J C Fleming & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

quotes.quote: It's 50 years of work by the family, going from a small yard, switching from National Hunt to Flat and and always believing that buying horses and believing that they're going to be good. It's a lifetime's ambition to have a Group 1 winner. Cercene is by Australia, a sire I love, and her half-brother won the Britannia so the pedigree was there and if she was an inch bigger I wouldn't have her. I was hoping Lake Victoria stayed because we'd have a lesser race and then we'd have place and ride her easier you know what I mean. She travelled well, Gary gave her a great ride, and we were thinking that being by Australia she'd stay as well. She was headed and came back again. She waited for something to head her but she's very tough and a dream to train. The plan was today so now we'll draw new plans - Joseph Murphy, trainer.
Cercene will have no problem going up to a mile and a quarter. Gary [Carroll, jockey] said the further she was going, the better she was getting - Joe Murphy, trainer.

Circene looked to be going nowhere and then won well. We think she's a very good filly. The Park Stakes might come a bit soon for her, but she is in the EBF Final here in the autumn, but she might be finished for this year - Joe Murphy junior, assistant trainer.

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.quote: I've always thought Exactly was a very straightforward filly and she hasn't put a foot wrong all year. She's been meeting Bedtime Story and Lake Victoria, so she's done nothing wrong. She's got a lovely mind and she's very easy to do anything with. Every day when you ride her, she just does her usual routine and saves it all for the track. She has a turn of foot and I would say a mile is her trip at the moment anyway - Wayne Lordan, rider

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Exactly stuck her head out and really tried. She's going to be a lovely filly for the second half of the year and might go for a race like the Debutante or the Flame Of Tara - Chris Armstrong, stable representative.

stable_tour.quote: Now that we've decided to give Lake Victoria a bit more time to recover from the Irish Guineas, Exactly will slot into the Coronation Stakes. She's a smart filly in her own right and Ryan [Moore] felt he never got a proper shot at it in the French Guineas. She was just being leaned into the whole way up the straight and she never got into full flow. She seems very well. 13-06-25

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.quote: January has a lot of ability and she keeps improving. She's getting there and I think we're on the right road. There's a Group 1 at Deauville [Prix Rothschild] on August 3 that might suit her - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.
January ran ok - a little bit messy early, the race was, but Ryan said she ran well. Maybe one of the fillies' races at Newmarket next, something like that - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.
January has run a good race. Sean [Levey, rider] was happy with how she handled the track. Obviously we were beaten by a very good filly - Kevin Buckley, owners' UK representative.
January was always going to be suited going up to this trip. They went a good pace and she will have learned a lot. She should be very comfortable going up to a mile - Aidan O'Brien, trainer

stable_tour.quote: The original plan was the Diane, but we've rerouted her to the Coronation Stakes. She's a consistent filly with some very good form from last year. 13-06-25

racecards.verdict: Already twice a Group 1 winner in Ireland and second to Porta Fortuna here 12 months ago, FALLEN ANGEL can enhance her fine record
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 January, 2/1 Fallen Angel, 9/2 Cercene, 12/1 Vera's Secret,  Cathedral, 14/1 Exactly, 16/1 Atsila, 20/1 Sparks Fly, 25/1 California Dreamer, 33/1 Queen Of Thunder, 100/1 Duckadilly

racecards.race_name: Tonybet Solonaway Stakes (Group 2)

quotes.race: Porsche In Support Of Breast Cancer Ireland Amethyst Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Chicago Critic has been up and down in trip, from six furlongs to a mile. I think a mile on fast ground is his ideal trip. They didn't go quick and I had a nice turn of foot towards the end so it's nice to get his head in front again. His best form is over a mile and he feels good at that trip - Ben Coen, jockey.

We're absolutely delighted - that was fantastic. We came over because we thought we had a nice horse on our hands, but when you look at the strength of the race, it was a very, very good race and Chicago Critic ran his heart out. Ben gave him a great ride. We got excited up there for a couple of seconds - we thought he was coming, but he just couldn't get there. No fault of the horse, he ran brilliantly. Today was the plan, and now we know we have a horse of this level, we will think about what's next. He'll stay at seven or a mile - I think he'll get a mile no problem - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

quotes.race: Royal Ascot Trials At Naas Handicap
quotes.quote: Chicago Critic has been gelded since his last run so maybe that and the good ground both helped. I'm delighted with that and he could be a horse to go to Ascot for the Britannia Handicap - Johnny Murtagh, trainer
Ben (Coen, jockey) said Chicago Critic is very professional, very genuine and should get a mile and a quarter next year - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.
Johan is a superstar, a proper horse. It's well documented that he's a hard horse to train but when you get him right he delivers - Jack Channon, trainer.

It's been a bit of a long road since the Lincoln last year and we unearthed a little bit of an issue Johan had in his back midway through last summer. We tried a few different things, but thanks to our brilliant vets at home, we came up with a solution. Coming into this week we pin-pointed this race and thought this was going to be the perfect place to start, then when we got 18 I thought, 'Oh God, here we go'. He's a very, very talented horse who hasn't been the easiest to train and to produce him on a day like that was pretty special - Jack Channon, trainer.

I thought we had a chance. Johan is a very good horse and he'd been working really well. We thought about running in the Listed race but when Chindit was in it we took a punt on this. It's paid off. He's a straightforward horse who lengthens and lengthens and that is Silvestre's bag - if he can just shovel the coal on there's no-one better - Jack Channon, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He finished the year in great form as he won a big handicap at Doncaster and finished third in the Group 3 Prix Perth at Saint-Cloud in October. He's been a little bit slow to come to hand this spring, so we'll look again at something in the high summer. We might go for the Royal Hunt Cup or a couple of stakes races now he's rated 109. He doesn't run often because he isn't the easiest to train but he's a very good horse. When he does turn up you don't want to miss him. 02-04-25

quotes.quote: Lord Massusus disappointed last day but things didn't go right. We got him switched off today and he came home like a good horse. It was a good performance - Joe Murphy, trainer.

quotes.race: The Dillon Marshall Property Consultants Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: We'll make an entry for the Jersey Stakes now, seven furlongs would be ideal - Joe Murphy, trainer of Lord Massusus

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Lord Massusus put up a very good performance and I think he's a good horse. That qualifies him for the final at Naas next month. He did what good horses do today - Joe Murphy, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Premier Nursery Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Lord Massusus was very straightforward and travelled well. He's plenty green, around here it's a tight enough track, but when I squeezed him he picked up and when he hit the front he pricked his ears and won with a little left. Hopefully he'll keep improving, he got the trip well. He's got a bit of scope and will make a nice three-year-old - Gary Carroll, jockey.

You don't expect to be winning these races with the penalties. We were a bit disappointed with Mutasarref last time at the Curragh, but that's the Curragh. I don't think he's ever run his best race there but he does it here. You think the penalties are going to stop him and he just keeps doing things like that. Horses like him make you enjoy the game. The plan is to come back here on Champions Weekend for the Group 2 Boomerang Stakes, which is beyond him, but it will give us another day out - Ger Lyons, trainer.
Mutasarref came back from an injury and as Colin said he's back to himself. That's his grade and seven furlongs is his trip. A mile is just a tad beyond him - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Bahrain Turf Club Knockaire Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Mutasarref has been on the wane all year but the ground here helped his old injuries and his old bones. He has been a very lucky horse for us and on his day he is still a good horse - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Hollywoodbets We're Painting Ireland Purple Handicap
quotes.quote: Mutasarref is a stakes horse in a handicap. I don't know if he's a Group 3 but he's definitely a Listed. We'll go to Champions Weekend with him now - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Jet & Peggy O'Carroll Memorial (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Mutasarref looked like he should go and win today. We'll look for a handicap for him. We thought he would handle the ground well and he did - Kris Weld, assistant trainer.

It was a brave decision not to run when you are one of the favourites for the Hunt Cup but the plan has worked. Skukuza did everything right and I think the further he went the further he was going to win. He likes coming back here for Classic days so maybe there is something for him on Oaks day! - Robin Trevor Jones, stable representative.

We came here for the rain and it came at the right time. Skukuza deserved to win this. He got injured in the Britannia and things haven't gone right for him. He's a smart horse and the could possibly go to the Hunt Cup next - Ed Dunlop, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been very lightly raced and was second in the Britannia at Royal Ascot last year. He ran really well that day but picked up an injury and was out for the rest of the season. He returned in a posh handicap at Haydock over 7f on Saturday and finished fifth. We were very pleased with that. We took a chance on the ground but thankfully he's come out well. He should improve significantly as he hadn't run for a year. We'll see if he steps up to stakes races but we'll have a go at the Hunt Cup and races like that first. Physically he's a big beast and I hope he'll improve from three to four. 30-04-25

quotes.quote: With the four runners it was uncomplicated, Snellen likes to follow and she travelled into it nicely. We picked up well and got a length or so on Colin (Keane). With Gary (Carroll)  suspended it was a nice ride to get  - Shane Foley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

We are delighted, it was a great run. Snellen has course form and that's always a great thing when you come here. Delighted with the run. I thought she was going to hold on for a few seconds, but nowadays I think a mile is her minimum and she will stay further. For that reason, Gary sent her quite early, but she stayed going all the way to the line. We've no plans. The owner just bought her at the London sale on Monday, so we'll have a chat and we'll work it all out - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

When I won at Limerick on Snellen she hung very badly left in the last two furlongs and I stopped riding to correct her and lost momentum. Today I should have pulled my stick through but she started to roll left and I told myself 'I'm not stopping my momentum'. She's still a frame of a filly and she's not fully furnished. She's had two runs in ten days and I'd love to give her a mid-season break and have a go at a big race in the autumn. She won't be at her best until she's three - Gary Carroll, rider.

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Mrs John Magnier & Mrs A M O'Brien

quotes.quote: Comanche Brave ran okay, I don't think he had any excuses, the first two pulled well clear. This is a tough place, any time you hit the board its not a bad result - Donnacha O'Brien, trainer.

Expanded has run a great race considering we only ran him at the Curragh seven days ago but Ryan said that if James Doyle hadn't attacked him early on Ancient Truth he would have won. He was here for experience more than anything, to run against The Lion In Winter originally, but we've always liked him and he went to the Curragh on the back of five pieces of work. As we know he handles the track, he could be back here next year - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Expanded is a lovely horse with loads of class but still very raw. When he puts everything together he is going to be a smashing colt - Chris Armstrong, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He got beat a short head in the Dewhurst having got kind of a raw deal. He was going into the race for experience mainly as second string behind The Lion In Winter, but that horse didn't run and he got put into the front line, so it was a massive run on his second start. He's being trained for Guineas weekend but he could go to the Curragh for the Tetrarch the day after the Guineas if he doesn't go to Newmarket. If Twain goes to Newmarket, he might take the easier option at the Curragh, given he's not a Group winner. 07-04-25

runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

quotes.quote: Mississippi River always runs up to a nice mark. Seven is a lovely trip for him, he is in the handicap at Leopardstown next Saturday, so we will see how he comes out of it, if not, we will find another little race for him and probably try him in stakes company from seven to a mile. He'll have a winner's penalty, but in fairness, he has good form around Leopardstown and it is a good pot so we'll see how he is - Chris Armstrong, Aidan O'Brien's stable representative.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Rectory Road Holdings Limited & B Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien

prev_owners.owner: Rectory Road Holdings Limited & B Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien
Rectory Road Holdings Limited & B Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien

quotes.race: Goffs Vincent O'Brien National Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: I thought it was very impressive and Dylan said Scorthy Champ idled in front, and that he is a top-notcher. I don't know what he will do next but if I was to guess, I'd say he will run again this season. I see him more as a miler so the Prix Lagardere or the Breeders' Cup are options - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

Scorthy Champ is a horse we liked and is well-bred. I thought he was very impressive. Dylan said he was a bit green early so he took his time and when he got a bit of room he opened up really well. The obvious thing would be to return here for the Tyros Stakes - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's not a certain runner yet but he's had a good preparation. It looks a hot race but we're looking forward to running him in that type of company.  13-06-25

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.race: Qatar Prix du Moulin de Longchamp (Group 1) (3yo+) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: I thought The Lion In Winter ran a very good race. He enjoyed getting a lead today and [being] on the turn. I'm very pleased with him - Wayne Lordan, jockey.

quotes.race: The Aga Khan Studs Prix Jacques le Marois (Group 1) (3yo+ No Geldings) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: The Lion In Winter was a bit green, he wasn't sure of himself out in front - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (3yo Colts & Fillies) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: I'm very happy, The Lion In Winter ran a very good race. He has plenty of speed and has the choice of the Sussex or coming back here for the mile race [Jacques le Marois], depending on how he is - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Betfred Derby (In Honour Of His Highness Aga Khan IV) (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: That was disappointing as The Lion In Winter was beaten a long way out - Colin Keane, jockey.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: Ryan was very happy. He said The Lion In WInter jumped out and he was a bit keen and a bit fresh, but when he did get him back he relaxed very well. He said he had to come out earlier than he wanted as they were coming around him and he probably got a little bit tired because it was his first run, but he was very positive about him. Ryan said the ground probably couldn't be quick enough for him and he rides like a horse with a lot of class and a lot of speed, and we just have to decide now do we go to Epsom or does he go to a French Derby or what does he do? The long and the short of it and what is going to tell us is how he is in a week - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

The Lion In Winter broke smart there and Ryan gave him a very uncomplicated, straightforward ride. They just went an even gallop but he found plenty when Ryan asked him. He looks a very nice colt so I'm delighted for everyone. We always thought he'd get further than seven. We came here with the view he'd go back [to the Curragh] for the Goffs Million and we'd get good experience by coming here. He's not short of pace but you'd imagine he'd get further than a mile and a quarter - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Juddmonte Chaldean Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: The Lion In Winter is a lovely horse and had been working lovely. He will have no problem going up in trip but he has plenty of class - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won what looked a really good Acomb Stakes on what proved to be his final run last season as he had to miss the Dewhurst. He's in good shape but took a little time to come right, so he's a little behind the others. He's very well but just carrying plenty of condition, so he probably won't make Newmarket. He might make the Irish Guineas and we'll have a look at the Dante as well. 07-04-25

quotes.quote: To be fair to Jamie for him riding his first stakes winner, and to be an apprentice still with a claim, he was very cool and gave Copacabana Sands a great ride. He pulled his stick through at the right time when he got out and she has hit the line strong. Everything panned out exactly how he said he wanted to ride her. It's a huge day for the filly as she has a nice pedigree and now she's broken her maiden in a Listed race. She's shown that she has ability before now and we only picked her up in February. She's done nothing but go from strength to strength, and improve. She probably prefers an ease so it's testament to the staff here and the job they did. It's also testament to Justin (O'Hanlon) who has recently come on board for race planning. This race was not on my radar and Justin put it forward so plaudits must go to him for picking this race. Justin mentioned the Ballychorus so that could be next - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Despite concerns over the ground it could pay to side with SKUKUZA after a luckless York run, Alakazi looking a danger
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Skukuza, 9/2 Alakazi, 11/2 Johan, 13/2 Chicago Critic, 7/1 Comanche Brave, 9/1 Expanded,  Scorthy Champ, 12/1 Mutasarref, 14/1 Lord Massusus, 16/1 Copacabana Sands

racecards.race_name: Royal Bahrain Irish Champion Stakes (Group 1)

quotes.quote: No real excuses, Anmaat has run an absolute blinder again. Come there to win and credit to John's [Gosden] horse, who just picked up a bit better than us. The others are sort of two-and-a-half lengths back. So proud of him, and no excuses. We know that he's got a preference for a bit of juice in the ground, but Jim [Crowley] said I can't make that as an excuse. Possibly he can quicken on softer ground when others perhaps can't? But as I say, I'm not making any excuses. The Eclipse has been mentioned, but we will just see how he comes out of this. York, Irish Champion... I think back here in October is probably his main, because all being well, we should have a bit of juice in the ground. So let's see how he comes out of this and we will go from there. He's run in two Group Ones now and finished second twice, and not done a lot wrong. I'm very proud of him - Owen Burrows, trainer.
It was a heck of a run, the lack of a run just told. Put it this way, I look forward to taking him [Los Angeles] on again now Anmaat has a run under his belt - Owen Burrows, trainer.

quotes.race: Qipco Champion Stakes (British Champions Middle Distance) (Group 1)
quotes.quote: Anmaat is unbelievable. He's always gone under the radar. It was touch and go whether he'd run in this race, and we came here to finish third or fourth behind Economics and Calandagan. To go and do what he did, it's great. The first half went perfectly as I wanted to sit third or fourth and give myself an option, but I could see early on Los Angeles wasn't taking Ryan [Moore] on at all. When we turned into the straight I had horses on my outside keeping me in and we just had to try and work our way through. We just got completely stopped, there was no option b or c at the time. We were just stuck, but when he got the gap it was extraordinary the way he picked up - Jim Crowley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

Anmaat had to be game, he just got a bit tired but he was entitled to after 439 days off. He needed a run last year to hit peak form so fingers crossed he comes out of this okay. He's in the Irish Champion but we'll let the dust settle after this and have a chat with Sheikha Hissa, Richard Hills and Angus Gold and go from there. It was a complex foot injury and all the team has done a great job to get him back - Owen Burrows, trainer.

Anmaat is pretty laid back about life so he'll never win by a couple of lengths. He doesn't show a huge amount at home but he's bloody tough. The ground was as quick as we'd want to go in today but he doesn't half travel, even on this ground - Owen Burrows, trainer.

It was a solid run. I thought we were up against it giving 5lb to Adayar and he's run his race. Anmaat needed to get his season started so I thought it was a good solid run. He battled away and it's tough out there, it's hard work. He's obviously in the Tattersalls Gold Cup in around three weeks' time and the Prix d'Ispahan in France, so we'll see how he comes out of this. We might try him over a little bit further at some stage. I don't know when but we'll see - Owen Burrows, trainer.

It was a big performance from Anmaat to win first time up at York and he had pleased me since. What surprised me was how far he travelled on the bridle, he's obviously really improving but we didn't expect that. We were stepping out of handicap company and I'm not sure where we'll go next, they'll be nice discussions to have. It was quick at York but interestingly Jim [Crowley] thought the bit of juice in the ground suited him - Owen Burrows, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's fine after Ireland, where he was narrowly beaten in the Tattersalls Gold Cup. He did everything right bar win, but the winner [Los Angeles] had race-fitness and it just told in the last 50 yards. I was thrilled with him and you don't see many travel in Group 1s the way he did. It was a proper race and we were chuffed to bits with him. He's come out of it well and, as long as it's not rattling quick ground, then we'll be heading there. 10-06-25

He has done nothing but progress all through his career, winning seven of his 12 starts with four seconds and a third. You don't get much more genuine than that and it has been a wonderful journey so far. Last year he started off on 103 in July but by the time he had wrapped up the campaign he had won three straight races including the Group 2 Prix Dollar on Arc weekend at Longchamp. He had a very good winter and I was keen to get a Group 1 under his belt, so we targeted the Prix d'Ispahan. He had a very pleasing return when second to Adayar while having to concede 5lb, which was no bad effort. It brought him to just where I wanted and we took the chance of going to France, where you know they can go very slow and then sprint. Thankfully he's very agile and has a great cruising speed and turn of foot. The ground was certainly faster by race time than it was advertised in the morning, but it didn't seem to be a problem, although I wouldn't want to run him on anything quicker. We spoke to Angus [Gold, Shadwell's racing manager] about Royal Ascot and the Prince of Wales's Stakes, but we thought it best to give him an extra couple of weeks after that and look at the Eclipse. Then he would have the obvious targets like the Juddmonte, Irish Champion and ultimately the Champion Stakes at Ascot in October. He put in a career-best performance last time and I don't think he has stopped improving yet. 07-06-23

quotes.quote: I didn't really expect that. I was hoping for a good run and Royal Champion has been working really nicely but he always works nicely. I thought that was a massive step up on what he's done. They can't all have run bad races and not turned up on the day, can they? He was travelling very strongly and I was just waiting for Cliff [Lee, jockey] to press the button. He said when he did press it he really picked up strongly, as he did in the Winter Derby and in the Listed race at Lingfield. Cliff said he felt better again today - Karl Burke, trainer.Royal Champion was very good today. I looked across going to the two-pole and I felt like I had all the field off the bridle. He just had a little canter round ? he's a very good horse but we didn't expect him to do it like that - Clifford Lee, jockey.

Royal Champion did that very well. I thought he'd get the trip as long as he got into a nice rhythm. I was pretty confident all the way round, to be honest. He's a good horse and he's already proved that on the turf, so I don't see why we wouldn't have a lot of fun with him this summer. This was the target, but there's a very valuable race in Hong Kong at the end of April over a mile and two furlongs, worth a couple of million. He's a horse who could be very competitive if the owner is happy to go there. There's lots of options over the summer and we'll tee him up for something - Karl Burke, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.
We've only had Royal Champion five or six weeks since he came back from Australia. He's done that well and the plan is to have a crack at the Winter Derby - Karl Burke, trainer.
Royal Champion is a bit in and out, but we knew there was a big day in him, so I'm delighted. The team had him in great shape for today and Jack gave him a fine ride - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Doonside Cup Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Royal Champion has finally shown what he's been showing us at home. He's always been a very good work horse. If Andrea Atzeni can't ride him in his work I normally ride him and he's always given me a really nice feel. Things haven't gone his way but today was perfect. It's dried out but there's enough juice in the ground. You need to keep him happy in his own space. I thought if I could kick when I did I'd get away from the other horses and he'd run for me - Jack Mitchell. jockey.

quotes.race: Rossdales EBF Stallions Maiden Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: We will have to see how Royal Champion comes out of the race and trains. We could happily put him away and wait for next year but if he really comes on for the run and looks like he needs another run at home we can think about something else. I think he is a nice one for the future - Roger Varian, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: It didn't really happen for him at Sandown last time. He got stuck on a wing and found himself too far back but I'm confident he's better than that. We've talked about stepping up to a mile and a half, so he could go for the Hardwicke. The other option would be to run in the Prince of Wales's but the one thing he does want is quick ground and we'll discuss the options with his owner. 12-06-25

He's won four times, twice at Listed level. His most recent win was when taking the Wolferton Stakes at Royal Ascot. It was nice to give Sheikh Obaid a winner at that meeting, which is so important to everyone involved in the sport. It was a very competitive race and he won well from Bolshoi Ballet. Although he was beaten last time at York when third to Alflaila, he did little wrong there. He's likely to go to Windsor this weekend for the Group 3 Winter Hill Stakes over 1m2f, which is his best trip. York's Strensall Stakes the same day is also a possibility. 25-08-23

quotes.quote: Shin Emperor was definitely the best horse in the field. As long as he jumped well, the plan was to take the lead to avoid any disadvantages or interference. We'll go to the Dubai Sheema Classic next, but as for future plans we'll have to discuss with the owner. In my mind, I'd like to go to Ascot and York - Yoshito Yahagi, trainer.

It was frustrating. You'd have loved to have got a clear run at them. We went a good, even gallop and we were still on top of each other most of the way around. When we turned in I would have had to sit and come around them. I was following Oisin [Murphy, on Kalpana] and I thought she was a nice filly to follow, but we didn't have the room - Colin Keane, rider of White Birch.
White Birch has plenty of pace but would have no bother going a bit further. He has matured massively from three to four - Colin Keane, jockey

quotes.race: Coolmore Stud Sottsass Irish EBF Mooresbridge Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: We'll see how White Birch comes out of that and talk to the owners to make a plan. He has the option of the Tattersalls Gold Cup and also the Coronation Cup at Epsom. He's come on a nice bit from the last day and hopefully he can come on again - John Joseph Murphy, trainer.

You'd always be worried about fitness on that sticky ground but White Birch did it lovely in the end. He behaved very well and we couldn't be happier with him. He travelled lovely throughout the race. He seems more mature now. He's in the Coronation Cup and the Tattersalls Gold Cup. We'll have a chat with the owners and see what they want to do. He's clearly good over a mile and a quarter but going back up to a mile and a half would be no issue either - George Murphy, assistant trainer to father John.

We're delighted with White Birch and he stayed on really well. He just hesitated again at the gates and put himself into a difficult position but it was a great run. I'd say it will be the Irish Derby next, Colin gave him a great ride considering how it played out early on and he galloped all the way to the line, so all going well it'll probably be the Irish Derby next - George Murphy, son and assistant to John Joseph Murphy, trainer.

We're delighted with that. We always thought White Birch could be a serious horse. His work had been super coming into today and he was obviously super impressive in his maiden at Dundalk. We're delighted he's done what we had hoped he could do and I think there could be quite a bit more to come as well. He has a big engine. We'll chat to the owners and make a plan from there. Today, with the pace he showed at Dundalk, we weren't 100 per cent sure he'd get the mile and a quarter but he's clearly got it very well. He's a lot of options and I don't think we'd be too worried going anywhere with him. He's very well balanced and moves very well, so I wouldn't be worried about going to Epsom anyway. I'm sure he'll have some big entries in the future - George Murphy, trainer's son and assistant.

stable_tour.quote: We're very happy with him and he's in super form. We probably won't run him if firm is in the going but if it's good ground then we'll run. If Colin [Keane] is unavailable to ride him we'll discuss the riding arrangements with the owners; there has been no decision on that yet. 13-06-25

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.quote: It was just a sprint. It was a mess really. Obviously the pacemaker jumped out and everyone pulled back off him. Ryan had to sit and suffer then. Delacroix is a big horse and we'd have preferred a more even pace, and that's what the pacemaker is there to do. They decided not to do it and it worked for Ombudsman. Delacroix was at the mercy of the horses in front of him in this race. Everyone was watching William, but we'll have to go again - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

All credit to the team and Ryan gave Delacroix an incredible ride. He [Ryan] said he changed his plans four times! Going out, he said he was happy to make the running and he wasn't the only one who had that on his mind. It just kept changing. But Ryan did say to me that Delacroix is a very fast horse and he wouldn't be surprised if he is a miler. So, I knew what was inside Ryan's head and I had a fair idea what he was thinking but he did say that four times he changed his plans. They went, then they steadied and then they went again. It was very difficult for Ryan but it was a masterful ride - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.It was far from simple. I wanted to go forward but got caught in a pocket and was a long way back. So, I had to wait and go around them. Delacroix showed a very good turn of foot and got up to beat a very good horse. He is a Dubawi. He didn't like Epsom but is out of a great racemare (Tepin) and had a superior turn of foot today - Ryan Moore, jockey.

quotes.race: Betfred Derby (In Honour Of His Highness Aga Khan IV) (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: Ryan [Moore] said Delacroix got taken out quite early and then he was back in the field and in trouble - he said his chance was gone quite early - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Cashel Palace Hotel Derby Trial Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Gavin [Ryan, jockey] went off at an even tempo and Wayne [Lordan] got a lovely lead into it. When he got after him at the furlong and a half point, the most impressive aspect was how he really lengthened and quickened to the line. His home work has been very good since his last race, he's come forward lovely and all being well he'll come forward again, so we're looking forward to Epsom - Chris Armstrong, trainer's representative.Delacroix seems to have improved a lot from his last run here and felt in good form at home. He's well in himself and the race worked beautifully. He has a good cruising speed and can quicken. He hit the line well. I think he's an Epsom horse. He has some class - Wayne Lordan, jockey.

You'd have to be very happy. Delacroix is babyish but he is growing up. I think he'll come back here for the Derby Trial if everything is well, and racing will only help him. We always thought he would stay well and he's a fine big, powerful horse. He'll improve plenty fitness wise. He quickened very well in the straight and is not a horse that will over race - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill Futurity Trophy Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: Delacroix has run a stormer. We hope that bodes well for next year - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Delacroix is a lovely horse and Ryan said he won with a bit in hand. With his pedigree there's no reason why he can't make up into a nice middle-distance prospect for next year - Paul Smith, representing the owners.

quotes.race: Trainor Stone And Tile Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Delacroix was very green the first day and he was still very green here. He was babyish in the stalls and he will come forward again - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was just beaten in the Futurity Trophy at Doncaster last year and you'd have to be very happy with his performance in the Ballysax. He is still babyish but he is growing up. I think he'll go back to Leopardstown for the Derby Trial if everything is well, and racing will only help him. We always thought he would stay well and be a middle-distance horse and he's a fine, big, powerful horse. He'll improve plenty fitness-wise. He quickened very well in the straight and is not a horse who will be over-raced. 07-04-25

quotes.quote: Expanded has run a great race considering we only ran him at the Curragh seven days ago but Ryan said that if James Doyle hadn't attacked him early on Ancient Truth he would have won. He was here for experience more than anything, to run against The Lion In Winter originally, but we've always liked him and he went to the Curragh on the back of five pieces of work. As we know he handles the track, he could be back here next year - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Expanded is a lovely horse with loads of class but still very raw. When he puts everything together he is going to be a smashing colt - Chris Armstrong, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He got beat a short head in the Dewhurst having got kind of a raw deal. He was going into the race for experience mainly as second string behind The Lion In Winter, but that horse didn't run and he got put into the front line, so it was a massive run on his second start. He's being trained for Guineas weekend but he could go to the Curragh for the Tetrarch the day after the Guineas if he doesn't go to Newmarket. If Twain goes to Newmarket, he might take the easier option at the Curragh, given he's not a Group winner. 07-04-25

quotes.race: William Hill Futurity Trophy Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: Hotazhell is  a smashing horse, he's very tough and he loves a battle. I don't think he'll ever win races by large margins, he loves just looking at the other horse and saying 'I'm better than you'.  He's a little bit feisty, he ain't called Hotazhell for nothing.   I'd say he'll start off over a mile and take it from there. He'll probably stay a mile and a quarter. I'm not sure he'll stay a mile and a half but you never know, he's a very relaxed horse and he just might.  He's done very well, he's won a Group 3, a Group 2 and now a Group 1. That's just the start of his career hopefully and he'll go on next year and get better - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

I knew the step up in trip was always going to be an advantage to Hotazhell as was the slower ground - Shane Foley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.  He got a bit free coming down the hill but today Hotazhell came around a bend and did it well. When he gets to the front he pricks his ears and doesn't really do that much. He's straightforward at home but when we come racing we saddle him in the racecourse stables rather than the saddling boxes and keep a red hood on him just to keep the lid on him. He'll go to Doncaster - Jessica Harrington, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.
Hotazhell is a nice colt. They went a good gallop and he picked up well. He is tough and he just does enough. We might go for a nice race over 1m in September - Jessica Harrington, trainer

stable_tour.quote: He's due to do a big bit of work in a few days and if all goes well he'll head to Longchamp for the French 2,000 Guineas [May 11]. He's got the most brilliant attitude - he hates being beaten and never wants to be passed. He's got lots of talent and I think Longchamp will suit him. He's been around a bend at Leopardstown and the Curragh, so he should be fine at Longchamp. He'll wear his customary red hood to the start. 23-04-25

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

quotes.race: Boodles Raindance Dee Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Ryan [Moore] gave  Mount Kilimanjaro a great ride, he was very patient. The pace was very honest and he didn't panic and the horse quickened up well and showed a nice attitude. He'll come forward from this and we're delighted with him. A Derby of some sorts - an Epsom Derby or the French Derby - are the options for him. We'll see how the trials go and where they'll all go - Paul Smith, representing owners Coolmore.

Mount Kilimanjaro ran a lovely race, I was delighted with him. We knew after Longchamp he got the trip well and Ryan said he could be a French Derby horse so we thought we'd let him take his chance in a Group 1 - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had some very good runs last season and won well in France before finishing second in the Group 1 at Saint-Cloud. We might run him in one of those trials at Chester. He was beaten at Dundalk on his return but we felt we might have started him a little too early. He looks like he'll get a mile and a quarter well and handles an ease in the ground. 07-04-25

runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

quotes.quote: Serengeti is a work in progress I'd say with loads more improvement to come and is an exciting horse. Hopefully he'll make into a stakes horse - Declan McDonogh, jockey, talking to Racing TV.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

quotes.quote: We wanted to see where we stood today. Ben [Coen] went the brave man's route, got the gap and he quickened up. Zahrann is very relaxed and doesn't show much at home, but he's learning on the job. He's a typical Aga Khan horse and reminds me a bit of Sinndar that when he clicks into gear some day, he might realise how good he is. He's still underdeveloped, and he could be a really nice four-year-old. I haven't spoken to anybody yet but hopefully we'll be able to go for the Irish Champion. Today was the test. It looked in Ascot like he was a little bit slow, but I think a strong mile and a quarter should suit him around Leopardstown. He has to improve again, but he might - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.He was second at Ascot and lost little in defeat but the ground was quick that day and Zahrann didn't really show the turn of foot he has. He's not a slow horse, he's lazy but he does quicken up for you. Good to yielding is probably his ideal ground - Ben Coen, jockey.

Zahrann lost nothing in defeat. Ben said they quickened up from four out and just had him off the bridle a little bit earlier than ideal. I'm not saying he wants soft ground, but a little bit more juice would help him travel a bit longer. Finishing second here is definitely more frustrating as a trainer than a jockey! I'll speak to the team. He's had a hard race today and he's been progressing quickly, so maybe he'll have a quiet couple of weeks and see how he'll come on. I think he can, and with more ease in the ground, you'd love to think he might be an Arc horse one day, and connections would love that race. He'd have a bit to go to get there, but he might - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Zahrann was still slowly away and is still a big baby. He was on and off the bridle the whole way but, when I grabbed hold of him turning in, he really came alive. He quickened up smartly and lengthened the whole way to the line. That trip was very comfortable for him and an extra two furlongs will be within his compass as well - Ben Coen, jockey, speaking to RacingTV.

stable_tour.quote: I was very happy with his win at Leopardstown last week and I am looking forward to stepping him up to Group 2 company in the King Edward VII Stakes. 13-06-25

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.race: Qatar Prix Vermeille (Group 1) (3yo+ Fillies & Mares) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: It's my first time riding Whirl so I can't say too much. She ran her race in the front. She was flat in the straight. I have no details to say now. We'll see how she is at home - Christophe Soumillon, jockey.

I can't remember a Group 1 race starting without stalls. It was unbelievable and well done everybody getting it going. Whirl is an amazing filly. Ryan gave her a lovely ride and she handles all types of ground - Aidan O'Brien, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.The start wasn't ideal. Whirl just keeps doing what she says she's going to do, she's very uncomplicated and has loads of ability. She stays well, can handle quick ground and can handle slow ground. Aidan called her a racing machine and this filly just keeps progressing over time - Ryan Moore, jockey.

quotes.race: Paddy Power Pretty Polly Stakes (Group 1) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Whirl is very special and Ryan [Moore] gave her a great ride. For a filly coming back from a mile and a half and when you're trying to outstay a rival, it's very difficult. You're walking a tightrope but he got it spot on. She's the ultimate racing machine and it was an unbelievable race to watch. Colin [Keane] and Ryan weren't giving each other an inch the whole way. That's what you want to come racing to watch. It makes it very special and I think it's a race we will remember for a long time. We saw in the Oaks that she wasn't surrendering and we saw it again today. She could go for the Nassau or York for the Yorkshire Oaks. She has so many options - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.Whirl has kept progressing. It took her awhile to break her maiden. I was a bit disappointed in her first run this year. Aidan was very sweet on her chances today coming back in trip. She is a very straightforward filly. The stallion already has had some day. The two-year-olds and fillies who get a mile and a quarter will probably get further - Ryan Moore, jockey.
Whirl ran a great race. She stays and obviously has a lot of class too. We were going to go forward if no one else wanted to - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

As Ryan [Moore, jockey] said to me before the race, that was the plan to bump Whirl out, get a good position and if they caught her then sobeit. I don't think anybody expected that particularly. But Aidan [O'Brien, trainer] seems to be able to do that, as we know. I don't think that Whirl is an Oaks filly, I think she'd probably get a mile and a half but a mile and a quarter in something like the Prix Diane would be more suitable, I think. But I haven't discussed it with Derrick or John or Aidan. That's just my thinking - Michael Tabor, part-owner.

Whirl just had that bit more experience and she got a lovely ride from Wayne. He went his pace and he was left alone to do it, and she was tough. She is another massive filly, these Wootton Bassetts are unbelievable - and they're just getting better - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Coopers Marquees EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Aidan [O'Brien, trainer] thought Whirl would improve and she has. She has a lovely temperament and plenty of size and scope for next year - Kevin Buckley, owners' UK representative.

stable_tour.quote: She won her last two runs last year, including a Group 3 at the Curragh, and has done very well. She could start in a Guineas trial or go to the Oaks trial at Navan. She's working well and we're very happy with her. 07-04-25

racecards.verdict: The obvious one is Delarcroix but it may be worth taking a chance with the progressive ZAHRANN on soft ground that may suit
racecards.betting_forecast: 10/11 Delacroix, 9/2 Shin Emperor, 5/1 Zahrann, 11/2 Anmaat, 13/2 White Birch, 14/1 Royal Champion, 25/1 Hotazhell, 66/1 Mount Kilimanjaro

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Petingo Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.comment: Not discredited under hefty weights in Curragh premier handicaps in June; more needed
runners.spotlight: Three-time Listed winner including here over 1m6f and this time last year awarded 2m Newmarket contest (soft) after winner failed drug test; not discredited under hefty weights in Curragh premier handicaps in June and following month tough enough task when fourth of 7 in Group 2 Curragh Cup; back in handicap company but remains vulnerable.

quotes.quote: Harbour Wind has run a very good race. In that ground, it's probably as far as he wants to go. He's tried hard, he's run well and he looked like he was going to win a furlong down. The ground is probably a little bit slower than we'd like, because he's won on firm ground. He's been a long time between races and I think he'll come on for it. We'll think about it [the Melbourne Cup]. I'll have a chat with his owner, Eva-Maria Bucher-Haefner, and we'll go from there - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Martin Molony Stakes (Listed)
quotes.quote: It was a workmanlike, battling performance from Harbour Wind under a very good ride from Chris Hayes. We'll see how he comes out of the race and discuss future plans with the owner as to where we go next. He'll be sharper for that. The Melbourne Cup is in my thoughts, but it is early days and we will see how the year progresses. He has to step up considerably - Dermot Weld, trainer.
Harbour Wind has run a big race and we're very pleased. He'll make into a lovely horse next year and that's it for this year - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Aer Lingus College Football Classic Vinnie Roe Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Harbour Wind is still a big, immature horse. He's a next-year horse and will be trained accordingly. We'll talk about whether to run him again this year - Dermot Weld, trainer.

Harbour Wind did it well. It was a funny sort of race as I think something happened to Colin Keane's reins and you can't read too much into the distances on testing ground.
