The one thing we didn't know was would he handle the ground and he handled it well. He came up the hill well. We'll take it from there and see how he progresses - Dermot Weld, trainer.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
runners.comment: Ebor also-ran remains opposable off this mark
runners.spotlight: Two wins include 1m4f York handicap last year; hasn't progressed at four, although close behind Harbour Wind in Group 2 Curragh Cup, most recently down the field in Ebor last month; opposable.

runners.owner: Mrs Caroline Hendron & Mrs M Cahill
runners.comment: Demoted from Galway Hurdle win; big player back on the Flat on ground he likes
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (including Group 1 Tattersalls Gold Cup); career-best hurdles run on latest when first past the post in Galway Hurdle but demoted for interference; previously finished good third over 1m6f at the Curragh; acts on good but well suited by soft/heavy; definite contender.

quotes.quote: I said the other day that if Helvic Dream was in a Group race on the Flat, I'd fancy him, because he was that well in himself. He's still novicey for anything big, so I would have thought we'll try and get another run into him somewhere and then tip along. He will go back on the Flat again this year - Noel Meade, trainer.

quotes.race: Tote, Never Beaten by SP Amateur Riders Derby (Q.R.) Race
quotes.quote: Helvic Dream enjoyed himself and did not do a lot in front but he won comfortably. He used to be very keen but he was very settled there in a messy race. He could be a Supreme Novice horse - Patrick Mullins, rider.

Colin (Keane) said he was going to sit a bit closer and have one go at Broome. He felt he went too soon for Helvic Dream the last day and he felt he did again today. He has an electrifying turn of speed but lasted it out. It was a brilliant ride. He's been a pleasure to train and doesn't take a lot of work. There have been plenty of people interesting in buying him but thankfully the owners have kept faith in me as I felt he could win a Group 1 - Noel Meade, trainer.

I thought he was good all year but things just haven't gone right for him with one thing and another. I don't know where he will go next but there is the possibility we could travel with him. He'll stick to Stakes races now. He loves soft ground. He wouldn't want fast ground but I think he'll go on good ground - Noel Meade, trainer

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Race (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Gavin [Ryan] gave him a lovely ride but he said it was very easy. They went a good gallop and he did exactly what he said he would. When he pulled him out he said he just had to change his hands and away he went. He loved the ground and quickened up well on it. Colin [Keane] loved this fella from the twice he rode him and Chris [Hayes] said last time he'd improve a lot because he was still green. The reason I claimed off him was because Dermot Weld was claiming 5lb off his runner and we would have been giving his filly 9lb as it was. I'd imagine we'll head to the final of the Foran Equine Irish EBF Auction Series - Noel Meade, trainer of Helvic Dream

quotes.race: Foran Equine Irish EBF Auction Race (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: He hadn't been showing us much at all at home before he ran well on his first start and he got into a little bit of trouble at Galway. Colin [Keane] has loved him all the time - he was pretty confident that he was a pretty decent horse. I'd imagine he'll go for the final of this series and we'll probably give him another run before that. He's thriving all the time. I'd say the ground was an advantage to him. I felt that he mightn't be a real fast ground horse. I bought him off Joanna Morgan and she told me 'you have to buy him' - she was right. He hasn't got a great pedigree but he's a very good looking horse, big and strong - Noel Meade, trainer of Helvic Dream

runners.comment: Highly-tried, not sure if he can run to this mark in handicap company; stamina unproven
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile in 2022; missed all of 2023 and never hit top form last season; highly tried of late and flattered by recent form-figures, in between beat two much inferior rivals at Gowran; remains uproven over this far.

quotes.quote: Crypto Force is a very exciting horse for next year. Colin thinks he will be better on fast ground, he will have no trouble getting 1m2f but on quicker ground he will have just that bit more pace. Ideally it would be just nice to put him away and train him with next year in mind - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

quotes.race: Ryans Cleaning Waste And Recycling Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Crypto Force did it very well and he will have learned a lot today, he was quite green when he got there. Colin thinks he's very smart. The plan was to go to the Chesham if he was good enough to win one of these - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

runners.comment: Good efforts in big handicaps at Ascot and Goodwood; contender if settling better
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last summer, solid runs over same trip latest two runs this summer in big handicaps at Ascot and Goodwood, despite racing keenly; contender if settling better.

quotes.race: BoyleSports Best Odds Guaranteed Rated Race
quotes.quote: Aeronautic won nicely in Dundalk the last day and it was an impressive performance today, he quickened up well and he's a progressive horse - Joseph O'Brien, trainer
Aeronautic is a big boy and he is only going to get better, there is plenty of improvement to come. He'll get 1m4f too - Dylan Browne McMonagle, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He had some good stakes form last year and has prepared well for the Copper Horse Handicap. 13-06-25

prev_owners.owner: John G Hennessy & C Acheson & Mrs John Harrington

runners.comment: Hasn't progressed this year, not an obvious contender for this step up in trip
runners.spotlight: Won a Group 3 over 1m2f at Naas early last season; hasn't progressed this year however, bit better latest when fourth in 1m4f Group 3 Ballyroan Stakes here last month, barely getting home; not an obvious contender for this step up in trip.

quotes.race: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: Sea The Boss was good last year and I ran her back too quick after Champions Weekend on soft ground. She doesn't go on soft ground. It was a big ask for her today as she hasn't run for seven months and there were three in there who had run. I thought her fitness might catch her out, but she was good and stayed well. She got the trip very well and she has that turn of foot. This was just her fourth run, so she should come forward a good bit from that. I don't think we'll drop back to a mile for the Guineas; she'll move up to a mile and a half at some stage - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

We've always liked Sea The Boss. We liked her a lot in the spring and she grew a lot. She is only really a frame of a filly and is next year's model. We'll see what we do and there is the final series of this race for her - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: I'm happy with her and she seems in great form. All being well, she'll head for the Salsabil Stakes at Navan and I think the step up to a mile and a quarter will suit her. She's grown nicely over the winter and is in good form. 24-04-24

runners.comment: Just okay fourth in Galway conditions event on flat return; opening mark  on the high side
runners.spotlight: Keen running sort a winner on the Flat in France on soft (1m3f) and returned from spell hurdling (useful form) with just okay fourth in Galway conditions event last month; opening mark appears on the high side.

quotes.quote: Bunting is a nice horse, he did everything well and showed a lovely attitude. He jumped well, just had a little look over the last two, but he did it well - Sean O'Keeffe, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He goes novice chasing and has schooled well. I've been happy with him. He's a young horse who could have more to offer over fences. 25-11-24

runners.comment: Tame Galway run but a contender on previous Ascot third; handles soft ground
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins include Naas premier handicap over 2m (yielding) last October; just two Flat runs this year, fine third of 20 in Ascot Stakes and dropped out tamely in valuable Galway amateur handicap; acts on soft, one to consider.

quotes.quote: Comfort Zone ran super, thought for a moment we might close on those leaders, but the ground was probably just on the sharper side for him - Tom Marquand, jockey.

quotes.race: Colm White Bookmaker Beggar's End Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Dylan gave Comfort Zone a lovely ride and it's a lovely race to win. You will probably see him back over hurdles over the winter - Frank Berry, racing manager to JP McManus.

quotes.race: JCB Triumph Trial Juvenile Hurdle (Grade 2) (Registered As The Finesse) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Scriptwriter is probably the best gauge-stick in England so I was happy with the performance. Scriptwriter was a non-runner on the day (at Chepstow) so I suppose this form is a little better. You wouldn't have known. The track probably suited Comfort Zone and riding him like that was a bit more sensible. He definitely deserves his chance at Cheltenham in whatever race. I wouldn't mind riding him anyway - Jonjo O'Neill Jr, jockey.

Comfort Zone jumps well and is a good, honest little horse. He's very straightforward and the ground is hard work so that was a good performance. This was a race we thought he could win, that's why he's here and it's some good placing by the trainer. Joseph's good with his decisions - AP McCoy, representing owner JP McManus.

Joseph has a nice team of juveniles and here's another nice one. The main thing was to get Comfort Zone jumping and travelling, to give him a nice introduction today. It was nice that he was able to go on and win impressively the way he did after doing all that. I was always getting there but the thing was not to pull the trigger too soon. It was about timing and it was easy to do that on a lad like him that was doing everything so easily - JJ Slevin, rider.

stable_tour.quote: At the moment we'll be sticking to jumping with him, but we could be looking for a Flat handicap for him somewhere along the line, be it at Galway or one of the nice staying handicaps in the autumn. 23-04-24

He had a great season, winning twice at Grade 2 level and he was kept very busy so we were conscious not to overdo him since then. He's in good shape and he could be targeted at some handicap hurdles in the spring. He had a nice break earlier in the year and will have another break after his run at Leopardstown, so he will be back in the spring. 27-11-23

prev_owners.owner: Mme Sophie Midy, Mrs Linda Shanahan Et Al

runners.comment: Ebor also-ran can't be ruled out probably needs to step up to play a major role
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in France on heavy; returned from spell hurdling with respectable third in Galway conditions event over this trip (2.5l ahead of stablemate Bunting) before never landing a blow in Ebor when midfield; can't be ruled out probably needs to step up to play a major role.

stable_tour.quote: I was really impressed with him at Naas because he's had an in-and-out season at home. I hoped he'd run well but didn't expect him to be as impressive. He jumped like a handicapper and I was very impressed by that for a Flat horse. 5-2-25

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.comment: Strong-finishing York fourth puts him right in the mix; handles soft ground too
runners.spotlight: No win since 2022 in his French days but some good runs in defeat in big handicaps, second in last year's Chester Cup and most recently came home strongly into fourth after not clear run over 1m4f at York last month; slightly longer trip to suit and handles soft so potentially a big player.

quotes.race: Duke Of Westminster Supporting The Chester Cup (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: It was just bad luck. Zanndabad missed the break and we were always on the back foot. But we're all still here and we live to fight another day - Tony Martin, trainer.

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this year on better ground; soft ground an unknown though
runners.spotlight: Improved form stepped up in trip and tongue-tie fitted to win over C&D in maiden and committed early in straight for decisive win over Floresta in rated event; outstayed by same rival over 1m6f in Listed contest here since; softer ground here an unknown.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Petingo Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: He had a little look when he got to the front, at the camera's on the right-hand side, but Happy Pharaoh is still young and hasn't that much racing in him so he'll learn- Paddy Harnett, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

We were happy to take a lead. Happy Pharaoh relaxed very well, got a nice tow into the race and took off in the last furlong. It was my first winner on the turf - Paddy Harnett, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

runners.comment: Galway disappointment on a retrieval mission but soft ground an unknown
runners.spotlight: Sole win in Britain came over 1m2f early last season (Ascot, good) and standout run on the level for current yard when second of 25 to a stablemate over 2m at the Curragh in June on similar ground; never landed a blow when favourite in valuable Galway amateur handicap following month; unproven on soft.

runners.breeder: E U R L Freddy Head
runners.comment: Restricted campaign this year, plenty more needed and not sure if ground will suit
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m at Clonmel this time last year off 76 and added AW win in December; restricted campaign this year, never counting at Galway last time over 1m4f; plenty more needed and not sure if ground will suit.

runners.comment: Disappointing Galway favourite; cheekpieces tried but unproven over this far
runners.spotlight: Excellent last year for Michael Halford when winning three successive handicaps (7f-1m1f); tried over 1m4f latest two runs this term in premier handicaps, well-backed favourite but never landing a blow at Galway last time; cheekpieces now tried.

quotes.quote: Lord Church was quite buzzy at two and it took us a good while to get him to relax and switch off which he does well now. He's due a break now and then we'll see where we go - Michael Halford, co-trainer
Lord Church did it well in the end. He's a smashing individual. He's a work in progress, he is going to go another furlong and will improve on better ground - Mick Halford, co-trainer.

runners.owner: E Lynch & T C Quinn & John J McGrath
runners.comment: Smart hurdler unexposed on Flat after Ballinrobe maiden win; could have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Very smart hurdler although yet to reach similar heights over fences, mistakes at Galway on latest; had earlier got off the mark on the Flat with ordinary Ballinrobe maiden win over this trip; opening handicap mark high on that evidence but could have more to offer.

quotes.quote: Buddy One jumped great, he was clever and shortened when he had to. Where we go from here, I don't know, but obviously our main aim is to try to get back to Cheltenham. He'll definitely face stiffer opposition through the winter, but that was a lovely experience for him - Paul John Gilligan, trainer.

It would have been nice to win it but we're happy. Buddy One is a fantastic horse. Is he going to win a Grade 1 over hurdles? He hasn't yet but he's on the premises the whole time. He's entered at Punchestown but I'd imagine that's it. He won at Cheltenham in November and he has been boxing in the main arena since and has been boxing with credit - Paul Gilligan, trainer.

It was an absolutely super run. If the ground was just a touch better it would have suited Buddy One better, but I'm not complaining. I won't go to Punchestown, but I think he might go to Aintree as he ran very well there last year. I will talk to his owners to see what we do - Paul Gilligan, trainer.

quotes.race: Paddy Power Games Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I didn't think Buddy One would be beaten today, his Galway run last time was really good. He was winning this race today off top-weight, he has gone out of handicaps now. Depending how he comes out of today he'll go for the Hatton's Grace - you meet Willie [Mullins] and Gordon [Elliott] wherever you go, but this is so special - Paul Gilligan, trainer.

Buddy One deserved to win that. He's been consistent always, he has carried a big weight and won as he liked. We would hope to kick on now to Cheltenham in November. He is going the right way - Paul Gilligan, trainer

prev_owners.owner: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner
C Munro, The Late R Gander and Partner
C Munro, K Ivory, R Gander & Partner

runners.comment: Limerick winner on good; no sort of run on AW latest; 1lb wrong.
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner won at Limerick in June over 1m4f and was unlucky at Galway; no sort of run on AW last time; 1lb wrong.

quotes.quote: They went a bit too slow for God Of Thunder early and I was always bringing him back but I got a lovely run down the outside coming into the home straight and he did it well. He won a maiden hurdle impressively here and hopefully he can progress on the Flat now - Dylan O'Connor, jockey.

runners.owner: P Smith,Mrs J Magnier,M Tabor,D Smith

prev_owners.owner: P Smith,Mrs J Magnier,M Tabor,D Smith
M Matsushima & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.comment: 1m4f maiden winner could have more to offer at this trip; 1lb wrong but rider claims 5lb
runners.spotlight: Improving 3yo went close in 1m2f handicaps early in the season and off the mark in workmanlike fashion on latest in 1m4f Curragh maiden in July; blinkers replace cheekpieces here and could have more to offer at this trip; 1lb wrong but rider claims 5lb.

quotes.race: Hong Kong Thoroughbred Breeders Alliance Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: We thought Light As Air was a well-handicapped horse. He didn't win at Leopardstown when he looked unlucky. We gave him another run at Navan thinking he would win and he was a little disappointing when he finished second. He was a bit disappointing at Ascot last time as well. We thought he would win easier today but we think there is more to come from him. We will give him a little bit of time now and go back again. He stayed well and could even get further - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

runners.breeder: Etreham Riviera Eq Girod De L'Ain Grivel
runners.comment: Improved upped in trip for maiden win; 6lb wrong back in handicap but don't rule out
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for George Scott in Britain and off the mark on yard debut in 1m4f Roscommon maiden last month, relishing step up in trip to win going away (third has won since); 6lb wrong back in handicap but don't rule out further improvement.

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.comment: Stamina concerns on this ground; new headgear combination and tough ask
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort has won over 1m4f on AW but stamina concerns on this ground; new headgear combination tried and tough ask from 8lb out of handicap.

racecards.verdict: Running off the same mark when an unlucky fourth at York last month ZANNDABAD could prove a bit of value on ground he handles
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Light As Air, 13/2 Too Bossy For Us, 15/2 Helvic Dream, 8/1 Zanndabad, 9/1 Aeronautic, 12/1 Charlus,  London City,  Harbour Wind,  Comfort Zone, 14/1 Happy Pharoah,  Buddy One, 16/1 Bunting,  Lord Church, 20/1 Sea The Boss,  En Or,  Crypto Force,  Bryant, 25/1 God Of Thunder, 33/1 Razdan

racecards.race_name: Hong Kong Jockey Club World Pool Autumn Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)

runners.comment: Curragh winner on penultimate start, her stable appears to have stronger candidates
runners.spotlight: Winner of two handicaps last year; out of her depth in Listed races on first two starts this term; profited from a drop in class at the Curragh on penultimate start; her stable appears to have stronger candidates.

quotes.quote: Back in shallower waters today. Quadruple has been tried in stakes company, we thought she would get black type, but it didn't work out. Cheekpieces on and back in handicap company worked - Colin Keane, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

I think Quadruple is on the improve. She's definitely growing and I can't wait to keep stepping her up in trip. She's developed from race to race and next year could be a good year for her - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Regeneron Limerick 'Family Day At The Races' Fillies Handicap
quotes.quote: Quadruple is improving and maturing all the time. She travels well and stays well. We'll try and find something similar and maybe try black type later on - Andy Duff, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Down the field in three runs since Naas win in May, faces a stiff task under a big weight
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three runs since 1m Naas win on good ground in May; faces a stiff task under a big weight.

quotes.quote: The last day over seven, Independent Expert got a bit outpaced and stayed on well. Nowadays, the stiff mile suits her well. She was a good winner of a Killarney handicap last year on good to firm ground so we knew he'd handle it even though she handles juice in the ground as well. She's very tough, honest and genuine. The colours are important to me as they are my assistant Stephen Mooney's grandparents' silks. We'll opt for something similar, she might run well in one of those big fillies' handicaps on the big weekends as she has some good Curragh form and she'll be a nice filly for Galway as well - Stephen Thorne, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)
quotes.quote: Indepenent Expert is a light little filly but the race conditions suited her. She was in well at the weights, so it's great she's a winner at two. It was a valuable race and the owner also has an incentive to buy another horse, getting a 10,000Euros voucher to buy a yearling at an Irish sale. That's it for this year, she ran well at Gowran on testing ground, so she could have a nice mark for turf handicaps early in the season - Andy Slattery, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She won well at Naas in a handicap on Royal Ascot trials day and hit the line hard, so the Kensington Palace looks a suitable race. She might not have as much up her sleeve as my other horse as the handicapper knows her quite well, but she's not been long with us and hopefully she can keep improving. The stiff mile should suit her. 13-06-25

runners.comment: Out of the money on only handicap attempt, eased after receiving interference at Killarney
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden winner; Group 3-placed over 7f at this venue at Leopardstown; out of the money on only handicap attempt; eased after receiving interference in a Listed event at Killarney.

runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps here and at Tipperary, not disgraced in a Group 3 last time
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m AW maiden in March; well beaten in her first two handicaps before getting her act together; up an aggregate 11lb for wins over C&D and at Tipperary; not disgraced in a Group 3 last time; helps to give the stable a strong hand.

quotes.quote: Limerick put me away with Mallavelly, I didn't know if she was getting the trip or parking, but it turned out they had overwatered down there and it didn't suit. This fast ground suited her well - Ger Lyons, trainer, speaking to Racing TV

prev_owners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.comment: Won a 7f handicap in June, faced a tough task in a Listed race on first try at 1m
runners.spotlight: Won twice at two and landed a 7f handicap at Fairyhouse in June; faced a tough task in a Listed race at Killarney on first try at 1m.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Race (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Orchidaceae won well at the Curragh so we were quite hopeful coming here. Everything went to plan and it was very straightforward. She loves that ground and we will have to look at stepping her up in grade - Andy Oliver, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Orchidaceae is one that Andy liked and he thought if I got a bit of cover today she might come home well. I would have no problem going another furlong - Andy Slattery, rider

runners.comment: Winner of three of her last four races, up 6lb for C&D success, same apprentice aboard now
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of her last four races, two of them at this venue; up 6lb for last month's C&D success achieved at the main expense of Aviatrice (first reserve); same apprentice aboard now.

runners.comment: Backed up a maiden with a comfortable Navan success on handicap debut, may improve again
runners.spotlight: Backed up a maiden win at Naas win with a comfortable Navan success on handicap debut, may improve again.

quotes.race: LubriSyn HA+ Premium Equine Joint Protection Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: We were thinking maybe Noli Timere wanted 1m2f so dropping back in trip we stuck cheekpieces on her. The ground is as quick as she would like and Colin (Keane) said she would improve for slower ground. We may go hunting a bit of black type now - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer, speaking to Racing TV

runners.comment: Three-time winner since March, has also put in some poor efforts such as on latest
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner since March, has also put in some poor efforts such as on latest.

quotes.race: Mongey Communications Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
quotes.quote: We were delighted to see Pink Oxalis run well on the slow ground back in April. She is ground versatile. She wasn't right the last day in Leopardstown. The ground got very testing and where she was drawn, nothing was coming from her position. Jack gave her a lovely ride and she travelled very smoothly. I am delighted to see her back to form. The owners are here today, which is great. We will see what is coming in the programme. We were thinking about Galway. I think the small fields suit her and we will see how she is next week. We might look at another fillies' handicap back here and we will hunt for a bit of black type later in the season, she is capable of that. The horses have been running well. It has been a quiet month but we only have 20 horses in and we are focused on a bit of quality - Stephen Thorne, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Handicap
quotes.quote: Pink Oxalis was very impressive. I wasn't too sure beforehand on the ground but she won well over a mile the last day at Dundalk and she's been bouncing at home. She turned a corner and has gotten stronger. Her work had been very good although I wasn't expecting that, she enjoyed the soft ground - Jack Kearney, jockey.

runners.comment: Course winner over 1m1f in June; C&D third behind Mallavelly in July, may turn the tables
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 1m1f in June; C&D behind Mallavelly in July; may turn the tables on that rival and could go well.

quotes.quote: Tachos is a gorgeous filly, has a lovely attitude and is very laid-back. That's probably her right trip, she's a mile-and-a-quarter filly. During the summer hopefully there is another one for her - Dermot Weld, trainer.

runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner, unplaced favourite for Listed race last time, very valuable 7lb off
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly; beaten a short head by the useful Island Hopping (third in this season's Irish Oaks) last September on the second of two juvenile starts; odds-on when making virtually all over 1m in a maiden at Gowran on seasonal debut; unplaced favourite for Listed race at Gowran last time; very valuable 7lb off; potentially of major interest.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: Continuite happens to live right behind my bedroom and for some reason that's the kiss of death to most horses when they say it's one of the boss's favourites! She's that because she's sweet and she's honest. I thought she'd do that and would have been disappointed if she didn't. She's a work in progress - Ger Lyons, trainer.

runners.comment: Listed-placed last year, was entitled to need last month's after a break, cheekpieces now
runners.spotlight: Won 6f soft ground maiden at the Curragh on debut in October 2023; Listed-placed last year; was entitled to need last month's outing on first run since March; cheekpieces now. and useful 7lb off

runners.comment: Made a bright start for this stable at the Curragh in May, absent since, can go well fresh
runners.spotlight: Won Naas handicap on 2024 seasonal debut; unlucky not to get up Curragh fillies' premier handicap before soundly beaten in Galway Mile and Irish Cambridgeshire; made a bright start for this stable at the Curragh in May; absent since but can go well fresh.

runners.comment: Dam was a star, a wide-margin maiden win indicates she is getting the hang of things
runners.spotlight: Dam was a star; a wide-margin maiden win at Rosscommon indicates she is getting the hang of things; Shane Foley prefers here to Serialise, his mount when third in this race last year.

runners.comment: Unplaced favourite for C&D event won by Onemoredance, trainer also runs Proxima Centauri
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year; front-running second in Curragh premier handicap in late May; unplaced favourite for C&D event won by Onemoredance on latest; trainer also runs the less exposed Proxima Centauri; first time cheekpieces.

quotes.quote: Serialise has come forward this year and got her act together. She wants that quick ground and I've been waiting for about three weeks. I'd say we'll stay at a mile - Jessica Harrington, trainer

stable_tour.quote: She ran really well when second in the big mile handicap on Irish Guineas weekend at the Curragh against the boys. She'll go for the Kensington Palace Stakes, which we won two years ago with Villanova Queen. This filly loves fast ground and she's a very uncomplicated horse who should run really well. She's got a big shout. 13-06-25

runners.comment: Bellewstown win came after three consecutive seconds, probably vulnerable in this grade
runners.spotlight: Won 1m Dundalk maiden on 2yo debut for former yard; consistent in handicaps this term, although her record of multiple beaten favouritism strikes a note of caution; Bellewstown win came after three consecutive seconds,; probably vulnerable in this grade

runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon
runners.comment: AW winner in France at two, hard to assess, trainer has two other runners
runners.spotlight: AW winner in France at two; hard to assess, though she was not beaten far in handicap attempts at Saint-Cloud in May and Longchamp in June; trainer has two other runners.

runners.comment: Had plenty to spare when making all to win a maiden at Naas, trainer has a strong hand
runners.spotlight: Had plenty to spare when making all to win a maiden at Naas, trainer has a strong hand.

runners.comment: Has been running over shorter trips, no luck at Tipperary on latest, return to 1m may suit
runners.spotlight: Career best when winning 1m1f Punchestown handicap last September; shorter trips possibly not ideal in three outings this term; no luck at Tipperary on latest, return to 1m may suit.

quotes.race: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Irish EBF Fillies Handicap
quotes.quote: Shavasi  jumped well, travelled strongly and quickened up well around the turn. She was strong at the line. We always thought plenty of her - Dylan Browne McMonagle, rider.

runners.comment: 7f course maiden winner on soft for Dermot Weld, relatively unexposed filly,  stable debut
runners.spotlight: 7f course maiden winner on soft on third and final start for Dermot Weld; relatively unexposed filly; first outing for stable with two other runners here.

runners.comment: Slow-starting tendency is a major concern, fared well in adverse circumstances on latest
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner; best runs this term when placed over this trip at both the Curragh and Limerick; slow-starting tendency has become a major concern; fared well in adverse circumstances (stumbled in early stages) when fourth behind Noli Timere at Navan on latest.

racecards.verdict: A sister to a Group 3 winner, Frankel filly CONTINUITE is potentially interesting with Nicola Burns taking off a valuable 7lb
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/2 Noli Timere, 7/1 Continuite, 8/1 Onemoredance, 9/1 Shavasi, 10/1 Proxima Centauri,  Um Wadi, 12/1 Anvika,  Sindria,  Luscious,  Plume Noire, 14/1 Empress Artemis,  Mallavelly,  Orchidaceae,  Tachos, 16/1 Quadruple,  Serialise,  Letherfly, 20/1 Bella Isabella, 25/1 Pink Oxalis, 33/1 Independent Expert

Red Baron's Barn LLC & Rancho Temescal LLC
